MAAK.description: MAAK is a Laravel based CMS developed by Mahdi Akbari.
MAAK.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
MAAK.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Maatoo.description: Maatoo is an all-in-one marketing tool designed to streamline campaign management, customer engagement, and analytics in a single platform.
Maatoo.history: Maatoo was a Swiss marketing automation startup founded in 2017 in Lyss, Switzerland by Niklaus Jonas Moser and others. The platform provided email marketing, social media monitoring, and customer communication tools. The company closed around March 2024.
Maatoo.summary_abuse: Maatoo was a defunct Swiss marketing automation platform. Closed in 2024. No documented abuse incidents during its operation.

Mabisy.description: Mabisy is an online platform facilitating the creation of custom ecommerce stores.
Mabisy.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Mabisy.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Macaron.description: Macaron is a high productive and modular web framework in Go.
Macaron.history: Macaron is a compiler-augmented, typesafe CSS-in-JS library with zero runtime, developed around 2022. It supports both styled-components and vanilla styling APIs, with Stitches-like variants. Integrates with esbuild and Vite. Has ~785 GitHub stars.
Macaron.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Small open-source CSS-in-JS library with MIT license and a clean, focused codebase.

MachoThemes NewsMag.description: MachoThemes Newsmag is a clean and modern magazine, news or blog WordPress theme.
MachoThemes NewsMag.history: MachoThemes NewsMag is a premium WordPress magazine/news theme created by MachoThemes, founded in 2015 in Bucharest, Romania. The company was later rebranded/acquired by WPChill (founded 2018). The theme offers a free lite version and paid Pro version, used by bloggers and news publishers.
MachoThemes NewsMag.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. A standard WordPress theme product by a Romanian WordPress studio with legitimate freemium business model.

MacroActive.description: MacroActive is a customizable coaching platform designed to help professionals build and manage their own branded programs and client communities.
MacroActive.history: MacroActive was founded in 2015 in Auckland, New Zealand by Josef Rakich and John Franich to help fitness trainers scale their coaching businesses. It provides white-label fitness app creation, nutrition tracking, client management, and marketing automation. Bootstrapped to $10M+ revenue.
MacroActive.summary_abuse: No notable abuse history. Platform collects health and fitness data from end users; primary concern is health data privacy. Legitimate white-label SaaS for fitness coaches.

MadAdsMedia.history: Founded 2011, headquartered in Laurel, New Jersey. MadAdsMedia is a small ad optimization network serving small to mid-sized publishers, connecting them with CPM, CPC, CPA, and CPL campaigns from ad networks, exchanges, and RTB advertisers. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes documented.
MadAdsMedia.summary_abuse: MadAdsMedia is an advertising network primarily serving small to mid-sized publishers. As an ad network, it carries inherent risk of facilitating malvertising campaigns where malicious ads can be injected through the network. Ad networks of this type are sometimes exploited to serve fake antivirus popups, redirect scams, or phishing landing pages. The vendor's small team size and limited visibility into takedown processes suggest moderate risk. No specific major public incident documented.

MadCap Software.description: MadCap Software is a company that specialises in creating software tools for technical writers to author and publish various types of content, including user manuals, online help systems, and knowledge bases.
MadCap Software.history: Founded in 2005 by Anthony Olivier and Bjorn Backlund, former executives from eHelp (makers of RoboHelp), after eHelp was acquired by Macromedia in 2003. MadCap launched Flare as a next-generation XML-based documentation authoring tool competing directly with RoboHelp. In 2023, MadCap acquired IXIASOFT to add enterprise DITA CCMS capabilities. The company remains independent and headquartered in Denver, Colorado.
MadCap Software.summary_abuse: MadCap Software is a B2B technical documentation tool with no notable abuse signal. It is desktop/server software used by technical writers and enterprises; it has no consumer-facing or user-generated content surface that would attract abuse.

MadKudu.description: MadKudu is a platform that enhances sales and marketing systems by providing actionable predictive analytics, enabling businesses to optimize strategies with data-driven insights.
MadKudu.history: Founded in 2014 by Sam Levan and Francis Brero in Mountain View, California. MadKudu built a predictive lead scoring platform for B2B SaaS revenue teams using behavioral and firmographic data signals. In November 2022 the company raised an $18M Series A led by Felicis Ventures. In August 2025, MadKudu was acquired by HG Insights, a technology intelligence firm.
MadKudu.summary_abuse: MadKudu is a B2B lead scoring and sales intelligence platform with no documented abuse signal. It processes firmographic and behavioral data about business prospects; the primary privacy concern is B2B data handling practices rather than consumer abuse. No public reports of misuse or malicious exploitation.

Maestra.description: Maestra is an all-in-one marketing platform designed to support the growth and scaling of brands.
Maestra.history: Founded in 2021 by Maryna Hradovich, Ivan Borovikov, and Alex Gornik. Maestra is an all-in-one marketing personalization platform for DTC and retail brands, providing CDP, email, SMS, loyalty, on-site personalization, and product recommendations. Originally founded in Europe, the company later expanded to Arlington, Virginia.
Maestra.summary_abuse: Maestra is an ecommerce marketing automation platform with no documented abuse signal. It handles email/SMS marketing and customer data for retailers; the primary risk is email spam if misused by customers, though the platform targets established DTC brands rather than anonymous senders.

Maestrus.description: Maestrus is a complete EAD platform that enables course sales, employee training, and secure content delivery with personalization.
Maestrus.history: Founded in 2014, headquartered in Campo Grande, Mato Grosso do Sul, Brazil. Maestrus is an e-learning and corporate training platform (EAD) serving content creators, businesses, and educational institutions in the Brazilian market. No significant ownership changes documented.
Maestrus.summary_abuse: Maestrus is a Brazilian e-learning platform with no notable abuse signal. Its primary use is corporate and educational training in Brazil. No documented incidents of platform misuse for fraud or malicious content distribution.

Magazord.description: Magazord is an all-in-one ecommerce platform.
Magazord.history: Founded September 11, 2014, in Rio Sul, Santa Catarina, Brazil. Magazord is a comprehensive e-commerce platform targeting Brazilian online retailers, offering native ERP, logistics, marketplace integrations, and proprietary payments. In April 2024, Magazord acquired BW Commerce, which continues to operate as an independent brand.
Magazord.summary_abuse: Magazord is a Brazilian e-commerce platform with no notable documented abuse signal. As a hosted storefront platform, merchant stores could potentially be used for fake retail scams, but this is typical of any e-commerce SaaS. No specific public incidents of significant platform abuse documented.

MageMail.description: MageMail is a triggered email application for Magento that helps online retailers enhance customer engagement and increase revenue.
MageMail.history: Founded circa 2013 by Kalen Jordan, a Magento community developer, and headquartered in Boston, Massachusetts. MageMail provided email marketing automation for Magento stores, including abandoned cart recovery and win-back campaigns. In October 2016, MageMail was acquired by FE International. Activity appears dormant in recent years.
MageMail.summary_abuse: MageMail is a Magento-specific email marketing tool with no documented abuse signal. As an email automation platform, there is inherent potential for spam if misused, but the product targeted legitimate Magento merchants rather than providing open mass-email capabilities. The company appears largely dormant.

Magento.description: Magento is an open-source ecommerce platform written in PHP.
Magento.history: Magento was originally developed by Varien Inc. and launched in 2008 as an open-source e-commerce platform. eBay acquired Magento in 2011 and later spun it off as an independent company in 2015, backed by private equity firm Permira. Adobe acquired Magento Commerce for $1.68 billion in June 2018, rebranding it as Adobe Commerce while maintaining the open-source Magento Open Source edition. It became a core component of the Adobe Experience Cloud.
Magento.summary_abuse: Magento/Adobe Commerce is a major e-commerce platform with documented but well-managed abuse exposure. Cybercriminals have historically targeted Magento installations for Magecart-style payment card skimming attacks, injecting malicious JavaScript into checkout pages of poorly-maintained stores. Adobe issues regular security patches and advisories. Outdated self-hosted Magento 1 installations (no longer officially supported since 2020) remain a significant attack surface. The platform's bug bounty program and active security team mitigate risk for current versions.

Magewire.description: Magewire is a Laravel Livewire port for Magento 2.
Magewire.history: Magewire is an open-source port of Laravel Livewire for Magento 2, created by Willem Poortman (associated with Hyva Themes). It was first released around 2022 and reached V3 in April 2026, closely tracking Livewire V3's architecture by reusing its JavaScript bundle and porting the PHP core via a tool called Portman.
Magewire.summary_abuse: Magewire is a Magento 2 PHP library for reactive server-driven UI. It has no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

MageWorx Search Autocomplete.description: MageWorx Search Autocomplete extension offers an AJAX-based popup window that displays and updates relevant search results while a customer forms his or her query.
MageWorx Search Autocomplete.history: MageWorx is a Magento extension vendor that created the Search Autocomplete extension, a free open-source Magento 2 module providing AJAX-powered search suggestions. MageWorx has been a Magento extensions provider since around 2014. The extension is available on Packagist under the mageworx/module-searchsuiteautocomplete namespace.
MageWorx Search Autocomplete.summary_abuse: MageWorx Search Autocomplete is a free open-source Magento 2 extension with no abuse signal. It is a backend library integrated into Magento stores to improve search UX and has no consumer-facing surface exploitable for abuse.

Magic UI.description: Magic UI is a UI library featuring open-source animated components built with React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion.
Magic UI.history: Magic UI is a free, open-source React component library for design engineers, launched around 2023 on GitHub. It features 150+ animated components built with React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Motion, following a copy-paste philosophy similar to shadcn/ui. The project has amassed over 19,000 GitHub stars and raised a seed funding round.
Magic UI.summary_abuse: Magic UI is a front-end component library with no abuse signal. It is a code library that developers copy into their own projects; it has no hosted service or consumer-facing surface exploitable for abuse.

MagicBell.description: MagicBell is an embeddable notification inbox that allows applications to display and manage user notifications within their interfaces.
MagicBell.history: Founded in 2020 by Hana Mohan and Josue Montano. MagicBell provides an in-app notification inbox API for web and mobile applications, supporting push notifications, email, SMS, and Slack delivery. The company is Y Combinator-backed and has raised $2M+ in funding.
MagicBell.summary_abuse: MagicBell is a developer notification API with no documented abuse signal. It is a B2B infrastructure tool used by application developers to deliver in-app and push notifications; it does not expose a public content surface that would be exploited for spam or phishing at scale.

MagicLabs.description: MagicLabs is a platform that simplifies creating and using Web3 wallets by eliminating the need for seed phrases or downloads, providing a way to build on-chain solutions.
MagicLabs.history: Magic Labs was founded in 2018 by Arthur Jen, Jaemin Jin, and Sean Li in San Francisco. Originally named Fortmatic, the company pivoted from a general passwordless auth SDK to focus on Web3 wallet and authentication infrastructure. It raised $31M from investors including Tiger Global, Northzone, Lightspeed, and Social Capital. The product rebranded as Magic in 2020.
MagicLabs.summary_abuse: Magic Labs is a developer authentication SDK for passwordless and Web3 login. As an authentication service, it is a potential target for credential theft and phishing attacks impersonating Magic login emails, but the company focuses on security-by-design. No specific major incidents documented; the service is a security product rather than an abuse enabler.

Magisto.description: Magisto is a video management solution designed to help marketing professionals, agencies, and businesses of all sizes.
Magisto.history: Magisto was founded in 2009 in Israel and grew to over 113 million users. In April 2019, Vimeo acquired Magisto for approximately $200 million, its largest acquisition at the time, to expand short-form video creation capabilities. Post-acquisition, Magisto's technology was integrated into Vimeo's video creation tools and the Magisto brand was eventually absorbed into Vimeo Video Editor.
Magisto.summary_abuse: Magisto, now part of Vimeo, is an AI-powered video creation platform with no notable abuse signal. As a consumer and business video tool with cloud storage, copyrighted content violations are the primary concern, but Vimeo maintains active DMCA enforcement and content policies.

Magixite.description: Magixite offers the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG), a set of guidelines and requirements designed to help designers and developers improve the accessibility of web content, ensuring it can be effectively used by individuals with disabilities.
Magixite.history: Magixite is a web accessibility overlay service offering WCAG compliance tooling to website owners. It appears to have launched around 2023 based on available evidence. Very limited public information is available about the company's ownership or origin.
Magixite.summary_abuse: Magixite is a web accessibility overlay tool with no documented abuse signal. Accessibility overlay products have faced controversy in the accessibility community for claims of automated WCAG compliance, but there are no reports of Magixite being used for fraud or phishing.

Maglr.description: Maglr is a no-code design platform for creating interactive visual stories.
Maglr.history: Founded in 2014 in Breda, Netherlands by Berry van Elk. Maglr is a bootstrapped no-code digital publishing platform for creating interactive online magazines, annual reports, and presentations. The company grew to $2.6M in revenue without raising external capital.
Maglr.summary_abuse: Maglr is a digital publishing platform for business content with no documented abuse signal. It is primarily used by marketing teams and agencies to create branded digital publications, with no anonymous content hosting that would attract significant abuse.

Magnews.description: Magnews is an AI-driven marketing platform designed to support omnichannel customer journey management.
Magnews.history: MagNews was created by Diennea Srl, an Italian company established in Faenza, Italy around 1996-2000. MagNews is a scalable multichannel marketing platform available as SaaS, on-premises, or hybrid. As of 2023, the platform reported annual revenue of €12.6 million with offices in Faenza, Milan, Bologna, and Paris.
Magnews.summary_abuse: MagNews is an Italian email and multichannel marketing platform. As an email marketing tool, it carries potential for spam delivery if misused by customers, but the platform targets established businesses rather than anonymous senders. GDPR-compliant and primarily serving the Italian and European market. No specific public abuse incidents documented.

Magnific Popup.description: Magnific Popup is a responsive lightbox & dialog script with focus on performance and providing best experience for user with any device.
Magnific Popup.history: Magnific Popup is an MIT-licensed jQuery lightbox plugin created by Dmitry Semenov (dimsemenov), first published around 2012. It became widely used across websites for responsive lightbox/modal dialogs. The plugin has been officially deprecated by its creator in favor of the native HTML dialog element and PhotoSwipe for image galleries. Only critical security fixes are still released.
Magnific Popup.summary_abuse: Magnific Popup is a deprecated jQuery lightbox library with no abuse signal. It is a client-side JavaScript library installed on websites; it has no server-side surface or consumer account system exploitable for fraud.

Magnite.description: Magnite is an independent sell-side advertising platform that provides technology for publishers to monetize digital advertising inventory across various formats.
Magnite.history: Magnite was formed through the 2020 merger of Rubicon Project (founded 2007 by Frank Addante et al., IPO in 2014) and Telaria (CTV ad tech). The combined company rebranded as Magnite in July 2020. In 2021, Magnite acquired SpotX from RTL Group for approximately $1.17 billion, creating the largest independent CTV and programmatic video advertising SSP. Headquartered in New York and Los Angeles.
Magnite.summary_abuse: Magnite is a large programmatic advertising supply-side platform (SSP). As with most ad-tech SSPs, Magnite's inventory has historically been subject to ad fraud, domain spoofing, and malvertising campaigns where attackers inject malicious ads into the programmatic pipeline. Magnite participates in industry anti-fraud initiatives (IAB Tech Lab, ads.txt) and has a Trust & Safety team, but the programmatic advertising ecosystem inherently carries meaningful fraud risk.

Magnolia CMS.description: Magnolia CMS is a Java-based, open-source content management system that employs a modular architecture and JCR (Java Content Repository) standards, facilitating the creation, storage, and retrieval of digital content within a customisable framework.
Magnolia CMS.history: Magnolia CMS was founded in 1997 in Basel, Switzerland by Boris Kraft and Pascal Mangold. Driven by dissatisfaction with proprietary CMS tools of the era, they built a Java-based open-source CMS. Magnolia grew into an enterprise headless CMS serving 100+ countries with clients including Swiss Re, Generali, Rabobank, Toshiba, and CNN Philippines. The company has remained privately held and headquartered in Basel.
Magnolia CMS.summary_abuse: Magnolia CMS is an enterprise content management system with no notable abuse signal. It is backend infrastructure used by enterprise organizations for content delivery and digital experience management. No consumer-facing anonymous content hosting surface.

Maguru.description: Maguru is a marketing tool designed to help organizations manage and execute promotional activities.
Maguru.history: Founded in 2014, Maguru is a Copenhagen, Denmark-based marketing and communication platform for small craft businesses and tradespeople, offering call tracking, SEO, Facebook retargeting, and integrated communication tools.
Maguru.summary_abuse: Maguru is a small Danish marketing SaaS for tradespeople with no documented abuse signal. It provides marketing tools to small businesses and has no consumer-facing or anonymous hosting surface exploitable for fraud.

Mahara.description: Mahara is an open source ePortfolio and social networking web application.
Mahara.history: Mahara was initiated in 2006 as a collaboration between four New Zealand universities (Massey University, Auckland University of Technology, Open Polytechnic of New Zealand, and Victoria University of Wellington), funded by the Tertiary Education Commission. Initial development was carried out by Catalyst IT. The first public release came in April 2008. It is an open-source ePortfolio and social networking platform used in schools and higher education globally.
Mahara.summary_abuse: Mahara is an open-source ePortfolio system used in education with no notable abuse signal. It is self-hosted by educational institutions, with no anonymous public content hosting. No documented incidents of significant external abuse.

Maho.description: Maho is a modern, open-source ecommerce platform forked from OpenMage, offering enhanced developer experience while maintaining Magento 1 compatibility.
Maho.history: Maho was created in 2024 by a former OpenMage maintainer as a modern PHP 8.3+ ecommerce platform and drop-in replacement for Magento 1. It forks from unreleased OpenMage v21, replacing all Zend Framework 1 dependencies with Symfony, Doctrine DBAL, and Laminas components, and removing PrototypeJS in favor of vanilla JavaScript.
Maho.summary_abuse: Maho is a self-hosted open-source ecommerce platform with no documented abuse signal. As with any e-commerce platform, individual store deployments could be compromised, but the framework itself has no hosted consumer-facing surface.

Mailcheck.description: Mailcheck is a JavaScript library designed to address the issue of misspelled email addresses during user input.
Mailcheck.history: Mailcheck is an MIT-licensed open-source JavaScript library that suggests correct email domain names when users mistype them (e.g., 'gmil.con' -> 'gmail.com'). It was created around 2012 and published on GitHub under the mailcheck organization. The project has been widely adopted but appears largely dormant with few recent updates.
Mailcheck.summary_abuse: Mailcheck is a client-side email suggestion library with no abuse signal. It is a JavaScript utility embedded in web forms to improve email input accuracy; it has no server or hosted service surface.

MailChimp.description: Mailchimp is a marketing automation platform and email marketing service.
MailChimp.history: Mailchimp was founded in 2001 by Ben Chestnut and Mark Armstrong in Atlanta, Georgia, originally as a side project of a web design agency. The company was bootstrapped for 20 years, famously declining numerous acquisition offers. In September 2021, Intuit acquired Mailchimp for approximately $12 billion in cash and stock, its largest acquisition. The platform rebranded as Intuit Mailchimp post-acquisition and has over 13 million users.
MailChimp.summary_abuse: Mailchimp is one of the most-abused email marketing platforms due to its widespread use and free tier. It is regularly exploited for phishing campaigns, malware distribution, and spam; compromised accounts are used to send fraudulent emails that bypass spam filters because Mailchimp's sending infrastructure has a strong reputation. Mailchimp has experienced multiple high-profile data breaches involving its internal tools (2022, 2023) that exposed customer lists. The platform has an active abuse team and AUP enforcement, but its massive user base and free signup make it a persistent target for misuse.

MailChimp for WooCommerce.description: MailChimp for WooCommerce gives you the ability to automatically sync customer purchase data to your MailChimp account.
MailChimp for WooCommerce.history: MailChimp for WooCommerce is an official WordPress plugin developed and maintained by Mailchimp (now Intuit Mailchimp) to integrate WooCommerce stores with the Mailchimp email marketing platform. It enables automated product follow-ups, abandoned cart emails, and list sync.
MailChimp for WooCommerce.summary_abuse: MailChimp for WooCommerce is an official integration plugin with no direct abuse surface. It connects WooCommerce stores to Mailchimp; abuse risk is inherited from Mailchimp's email platform (spam, phishing) rather than the plugin itself.

MailChimp for WordPress.description: MailChimp for WordPress is an email marketing plugin that enables you to build subscriber lists.
MailChimp for WordPress.history: MailChimp for WordPress (MC4WP) is a popular third-party WordPress plugin by ibericode (Danny van Kooten), first released around 2012. It enables easy Mailchimp list signup form integration on WordPress sites. It is one of the most-installed WordPress email marketing plugins with millions of active installs.
MailChimp for WordPress.summary_abuse: MailChimp for WordPress is a widely-used integration plugin with no direct abuse surface. It facilitates email list signups on WordPress sites; potential misuse involves CSRF or spam list injection if poorly configured, but the plugin itself ships standard security practices.

Mailercloud.description: Mailercloud is an email marketing platform that offers tools for creating, sending, and tracking email campaigns, including automation, contact segmentation, and detailed analytics.
Mailercloud.history: Founded in 2013 by Vinoop Thayatt and Amar C P in Kozhikode, Kerala, India. The company operated under a different name until 2018 when it rebranded to Mailercloud. It provides a cloud-based email marketing platform with campaign tools, automation, and analytics.
Mailercloud.summary_abuse: Mailercloud is an Indian email marketing platform with a free tier, which creates potential for spam and phishing abuse similar to other bulk email providers. The company serves a largely price-sensitive market and has limited public documentation of its abuse enforcement practices.

MailerLite.description: MailerLite is an email marketing tool and website builder for businesses of all shapes and sizes.
MailerLite.history: Founded in 2010 by Ignas Rubezius and team in Vilnius, Lithuania, initially as a web design agency before pivoting to email marketing. MailerLite grew entirely bootstrapped for over a decade, serving over 1 million customers. In 2022, Polish company Vercom S.A. acquired MailerLite for approximately $90 million.
MailerLite.summary_abuse: MailerLite is an email marketing platform with documented abuse issues. Its generous free tier and relatively easy signup process have made it attractive for spammers and phishing campaigns. In 2024, MailerLite was used by attackers to send phishing emails impersonating major crypto brands. The company has an abuse team and AUP enforcement, but remains a meaningful target due to its reputation and free access.

MailerLite plugin.description: The official MailerLite signup forms plugin makes it easy to grow your newsletter subscriber list from your WordPress blog or website. The plugin automatically integrates your WordPress form with your MailerLite email marketing account.
MailerLite plugin.history: MailerLite plugin is the official WordPress plugin by MailerLite (now owned by Vercom S.A.) to integrate MailerLite email marketing with WordPress sites. It enables embedding signup forms and pop-ups. First released to support MailerLite's growing WordPress customer base.
MailerLite plugin.summary_abuse: MailerLite's WordPress plugin is an official integration widget with no direct abuse surface beyond enabling the MailerLite email platform, which carries its own documented abuse exposure from spam and phishing.

MailerLite Website Builder.description: MailerLite Website Builder is a free drag & drop website builder with interactive blocks and ecommerce integrations.
MailerLite Website Builder.history: MailerLite Website Builder is a no-code drag-and-drop website builder feature integrated into the MailerLite email marketing platform, launched as part of MailerLite's expanded suite to allow users to create landing pages and websites alongside their email campaigns. MailerLite is owned by Vercom S.A. since 2022.
MailerLite Website Builder.summary_abuse: MailerLite Website Builder is a no-code website builder embedded in the MailerLite platform. As a hosted website builder with free tier access, it could be used to create phishing landing pages or fake storefronts. Risk is similar to other MailerLite abuse exposure; the platform has AUP enforcement.

Mailgun.description: Mailgun is a transactional email API service for developers.
Mailgun.history: Founded in 2010 by Ev Kontsevoy and Taylor Wakefield, Mailgun was a Y Combinator 2011 graduate. Rackspace acquired Mailgun in 2012. In 2017, it was spun out as independent with $50M in growth capital from Turn/River Capital. Thoma Bravo acquired Mailgun in 2019. In December 2019, Mailgun acquired Mailjet. Both became part of the Pathwire brand in 2021, before Swedish company Sinch acquired Pathwire for $1.9 billion in late 2021.
Mailgun.summary_abuse: Mailgun is a popular developer email API with documented abuse. Its free tier and developer-friendly API make it a frequent target for spammers and phishers who use it to send bulk malicious emails. The platform has been involved in numerous phishing campaigns targeting financial services and crypto. Mailgun maintains an abuse team and AUP enforcement, and participates in industry anti-abuse efforts, but its scale and free access keep abuse risk elevated.

Mailjet.description: Mailjet is an email delivery service for marketing and developer teams.
Mailjet.history: Founded in 2010 in Nantes, France by Wilfried Durand and Julien Tartarin as the first European transactional email service provider. In October 2019, Mailjet was acquired by Mailgun Technology. Both brands became part of the Pathwire portfolio in 2021, and were acquired by Swedish company Sinch in a $1.9 billion deal in late 2021.
Mailjet.summary_abuse: Mailjet is a large transactional and marketing email platform with documented abuse exposure. As an email API with a free tier, it is used by spammers and phishers, particularly since it is the leading European provider and has GDPR-compliant infrastructure. Mailjet/Sinch has active AUP enforcement and abuse reporting mechanisms, but the platform's widespread use keeps abuse risk meaningful.

Mailman.description: Mailman is free software for managing electronic mail discussion and e-newsletter lists. Mailman is integrated with the web, making it easy for users to manage their accounts and for list owners to administer their lists. Mailman supports built-in archiving, automatic bounce processing, content filtering, digest delivery, spam filters, and more.
Mailman.history: GNU Mailman was originally created by John Viega in the mid-1990s. Development through version 1.0 was a community effort led by Barry Warsaw. Mailman became part of the GNU Project. Mailman 2 became the dominant mailing list manager for decades. Mailman 3 (with a REST API and Django-based web interface) was released in 2015. The project is maintained primarily by Mark Sapiro and Barry Warsaw.
Mailman.summary_abuse: GNU Mailman is a self-hosted mailing list manager widely used for open-source project mailing lists, academic lists, and community forums. Mailman-hosted lists are sometimes exploited to send spam to subscribers, or compromised admin credentials used to add external email harvesting. The software is self-hosted, so abuse risk depends on the deploying organization's security practices.

Mailmunch.description: Mailmunch is a lead generation tool that combines landing pages, forms, and email marketing.
Mailmunch.history: Founded in 2014 by Adeel Raza and Zain Zafar in San Francisco. Mailmunch started as a list-building platform focused on email capture popups and landing pages, later expanding to include email marketing automation. The company has grown to run on over 500,000 websites.
Mailmunch.summary_abuse: Mailmunch is an email lead capture and marketing platform. Its popups and forms are sometimes used on low-quality sites to harvest emails without proper consent. As a marketing email platform, it carries potential for spam delivery if misused by customers. No specific major incidents documented.

Mailocator.description: Mailocator is a display platform designed to enhance conversions by facilitating lead collection, data gathering, and sales growth.
Mailocator.history: Mailocator launched in 2017 in the Czech Republic. In February 2019, the company incorporated as Mailnatives s.r.o. It provides on-site campaign management and customer journey analytics with integrations into popular European and international email marketing platforms and e-commerce systems.
Mailocator.summary_abuse: Mailocator is a Czech on-site marketing analytics and campaign tool with no documented abuse signal. It is a B2B tool used by e-commerce and marketing agencies with no consumer content hosting surface.

MailOptin.description: WordPress plugin to create email signup forms that convert visitors into subscribers combined with email automation and newsletter features.
MailOptin.history: MailOptin is a WordPress plugin by Collins Agbonghama (Blue Tag Media, Inc.) for building email subscription forms, popups, and integrating with email marketing services such as Mailchimp, HubSpot, and AWeber. It was introduced around 2017.
MailOptin.summary_abuse: MailOptin is a WordPress email optin and popup plugin with no documented abuse signal. It is a backend tool that collects email signups on WordPress sites; potential misuse involves collecting emails without proper consent if misconfigured by site owners.

Mailshake.description: Mailshake is an AI-powered sales engagement and B2B lead management platform for creating, automating, and tracking outreach campaigns.
Mailshake.history: Founded in 2015 by Bob Senoff, Sujan Patel, and Colin Mathews in Austin, Texas. Mailshake is a bootstrapped sales engagement and cold email platform targeting SMB and mid-market teams. By 2024, the company reached $10M ARR with approximately 50,000 customers.
Mailshake.summary_abuse: Mailshake is a cold email and sales outreach platform. Cold email tools are frequently misused for unsolicited commercial email (spam) and spear-phishing campaigns. Mailshake requires users to connect their own email accounts (Google, Outlook), which shifts some spam-sending risk to those providers, but the platform enables mass outreach at scale. No major specific incidents documented, but the cold email category has inherent high abuse potential.

MainAd.description: MainAd is an international advertising technology company specialising in real-time bidding and programmatic ad retargeting.
MainAd.history: Founded in 2007 in Pescara, Italy by Marco Cotumaccio, Piero Pavone, and Marino Gualano. MainAd is an international programmatic advertising platform using its proprietary Logico technology for dynamic ad optimization. The company expanded from Italy to become a global ad-tech business operating in over 80 countries, with offices in Pescara, Milan, London, Istanbul, and Asia.
MainAd.summary_abuse: MainAd is a programmatic advertising network with the typical ad-tech abuse exposure of ad fraud and malvertising. Programmatic networks are exploited to serve malicious ads to end users. MainAd uses ML-based optimization which could mitigate some invalid traffic, but the programmatic ecosystem inherently carries fraud risk.

Mainboard.description: Mainboard is a software platform designed to manage, schedule, and book talent.
Mainboard.history: Founded in 1997, Mainboard LLC is a New York-based company that provides agency management software for model, talent, and creative agencies. Its Portfoliopad product manages bookings, talent, invoicing, and websites for agencies. The company has been serving the talent agency sector for over 20 years.
Mainboard.summary_abuse: Mainboard is a niche talent and model agency management SaaS with no documented abuse signal. It is B2B software for agency operations with no public content or anonymous access surface.

Mainstay.description: Mainstay is a student engagement platform that is powered by human-centered, AI-enhanced chatbots.
Mainstay.history: Founded in 2014 as AdmitHub, Mainstay is a US-based ed-tech company that provides AI-powered student success coaching for colleges and universities. The platform uses behaviorally intelligent chatbots to support student enrollment, financial aid, and completion. It has served over 5 million students across 200+ institutions. In 2022 it rebranded to Mainstay. In 2026 it was acquired by nonprofit Lemnis.
Mainstay.summary_abuse: Mainstay is a legitimate B2B ed-tech SaaS for higher education institutions. No documented abuse signals. It operates in a regulated education space with verified institutional customers.

Maisey.description: Maisey is a website builder for churches.
Maisey.history: Maisey is a niche website builder targeting churches and ministries, based in Redding, California, US. It provides drag-and-drop website and e-course creation tools tailored for religious organizations and ministries. The platform also supports learning platforms and content delivery for church communities.
Maisey.summary_abuse: Maisey is a small niche SaaS for church website building with no documented abuse signals. It is low-traffic and serves religious ministry organizations exclusively.

Maisie.description: Maisie is an AI-powered conversational assistant that provides automated sales and customer support by handling queries around the clock.
Maisie.history: Maisie AI is a UK-based ecommerce chatbot platform that provides AI-powered customer support, sales automation, and lead generation tools primarily for Shopify stores. It uses GPT-4 to power conversational product recommendations and customer service automation. The platform is available on the Shopify App Store and prices based on monthly unique website visitors.
Maisie.summary_abuse: Maisie is a legitimate ecommerce chatbot SaaS for Shopify merchants. No abuse signals detected. It operates as a standard B2B tool for online retail customer engagement.

MAJIN.description: MAJIN reads the hearts and minds of each customer using real-world data to automate optimal marketing processes.
MAJIN.history: MAJIN is a cloud-based marketing automation platform founded in 2016 in Shinjuku City, Japan. It offers email delivery, pop-up displays, push notifications, customer journey analytics, and campaign reporting. Unfunded, niche Japanese martech platform.
MAJIN.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Small Japanese marketing automation tool with no notable issues.

Makaira.description: Makaira is a commerce frontend designed for headless shop systems.
Makaira.history: Makaira is a German headless commerce and search platform founded in 2019 and based in Mannheim, Germany. It provides advanced search, personalization, A/B testing, and product recommendations as a SaaS add-on for ecommerce platforms. In July 2023 it was acquired by ECOMMERCE ONE. Pricing starts at €249/month for basic packages.
Makaira.summary_abuse: Makaira is a legitimate B2B ecommerce SaaS from Germany with no abuse signals. It focuses on enterprise ecommerce optimization and is a subsidiary of a larger European e-commerce group.

Makane.description: Makane is a platform for creating websites and online stores.
Makane.history: MakanE (formerly ShopGo) is a MENA-focused ecommerce platform founded in 2012 in Amman, Jordan. It enables SMEs to create online stores and mobile apps, with integrations for payments, logistics, and fulfillment. The company was acquired by Mawdoo3 in February 2022 and rebranded as MakanE. It powers over 14,000 merchant partners across the MENA region.
Makane.summary_abuse: MakanE is a legitimate Middle East ecommerce platform serving SMEs. No documented abuse signals. It is a subsidiary of Mawdoo3 with significant regional merchant customer base.

Make-Sense.history: Make-Sense (makesense.ai) is an open-source image annotation tool built with TypeScript/React, created by Piotr Skalski. It runs entirely in the browser with no server-side data transfer, supporting bounding boxes, polygons, and points. It uses TensorFlow.js for AI-assisted annotation. Released under GPLv3, it is freely available at GitHub and as a hosted web app.
Make-Sense.summary_abuse: Make-Sense is a privacy-respecting open-source annotation tool. No abuse signals. All processing happens client-side in the browser, making it especially safe for sensitive image datasets.

Makeplans.description: Makeplans is a scheduling system designed for appointment booking and event registration, providing tools to manage availability, reservations, and attendee organization.
Makeplans.history: Makeplans is a Norwegian online appointment booking and scheduling platform founded in 2010. It supports appointment booking, event sign-up, SMS reminders, Stripe payments, and API integrations with tools like Zapier. It has facilitated over 7 million bookings across 300+ customers. The platform offers a developer-friendly API and no-credit-card free trial.
Makeplans.summary_abuse: Makeplans is a legitimate niche scheduling SaaS from Norway with no abuse signals. It is a small, independent booking tool used by small businesses across various industries.

Maker.description: Maker is a platform that enables the creation, management, and optimization of ecommerce content without requiring coding knowledge.
Maker.history: Maker (maker.co) is an ecommerce content and personalization SaaS platform that integrates with online stores to deliver content creation, optimization, and personalization tools for marketers. It is a small US-based startup targeting ecommerce teams that want to manage storefronts without engineering dependencies.
Maker.summary_abuse: Maker is a small ecommerce personalization SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It operates as a B2B tool for ecommerce marketing teams with limited public profile.

MakeShop.description: MakeShop is a Japanese ecommerce platform.
MakeShop.history: MakeShop is Japan's leading ecommerce platform by annual transaction volume, achieving 14 consecutive years as the domestic #1 EC SaaS. It is operated by GMO Pepabo (a subsidiary of GMO Internet Group) and offers 650+ built-in functions for Japanese merchants. The platform charges flat monthly fees with no sales commissions and has over 11,770 live stores.
MakeShop.summary_abuse: MakeShop is a legitimate, dominant Japanese ecommerce platform backed by GMO Internet Group. No abuse signals. It is a well-established B2B SaaS with significant market share in Japan.

MakeShopKorea.description: MakeShopKorea is a Korean hosting brand that focuses on building ecommerce stores.
MakeShopKorea.history: MakeShop Korea (makeshop.co.kr) is Korea's first shopping mall solution service, operated by KoreaCentre. It provides ecommerce store building tools tailored to South Korean merchants, including mobile apps, templates, order analytics, and smart store integrations. The platform is designed for the East Asian ecommerce landscape and supports overseas expansion.
MakeShopKorea.summary_abuse: MakeShop Korea is a legitimate Korean ecommerce platform with no documented abuse signals. It is a well-established regional SaaS provider serving South Korean merchants.

Makeswift.description: Makeswift is a no-code website builder with the power and detail of a design tool.
Makeswift.history: Makeswift is a US-based no-code visual website builder for Next.js, launched around 2020. It allows marketers to visually build and manage composable React websites without engineering support. In May 2023, the company raised $3M led by Active Capital. It integrates with BigCommerce and deploys on Vercel.
Makeswift.summary_abuse: Makeswift is a legitimate VC-backed Next.js visual website builder with no abuse signals. It is a developer-friendly SaaS targeting marketing teams at companies using React/Next.js stacks.

Malomo.description: Malomo is a cloud-based shipment tracking solution that helps small to midsize eCommerce businesses provide customers with shipping updates via white-label package tracking pages.
Malomo.history: Malomo is a US-based post-purchase shipment tracking and marketing platform for ecommerce brands, founded in 2018. It provides branded tracking pages and transactional notifications that turn order tracking into a marketing channel. It integrates deeply with Shopify, Klaviyo, and similar tools. Malomo was acquired by Redo.
Malomo.summary_abuse: Malomo is a legitimate B2B ecommerce tracking SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves online retailers and is backed by established ecommerce ecosystem partnerships.

Mambo.history: Mambo (originally Mambo Open Source / MOS) is an open-source PHP/MySQL CMS, development started in March 2000 by Miro Construct Pty Ltd in Melbourne, Australia. First released in 2001, it gained popularity for its extensible architecture. In 2005 a governance dispute caused the core team to fork the project and create Joomla!, decimating the Mambo community. The Mambo Foundation was established to manage the remaining codebase but development stalled; the last release was in 2008 and the project is now abandoned.
Mambo.summary_abuse: Mambo is an abandoned CMS last released in 2008. Abandoned CMSs attract abuse through unpatched vulnerabilities; legacy Mambo installations may be exploited for web defacement or spam. No active vendor, no security patches, and no takedown process exist.

Manatee.description: Manatee is a tool designed to enhance online advertising revenue by optimising ad placements and targeting strategies, increasing click-through rates and overall ad performance.
Manatee.history: Manatee (manatee.io) is an ad revenue optimization SaaS that enhances online advertising performance by optimizing ad placements, targeting strategies, and click-through rates. It is a small advertising technology company focused on publisher monetization.
Manatee.summary_abuse: Manatee is an ad-tech SaaS in the advertising optimization category, which inherently involves higher tracker density and data collection. No specific abuse incidents documented, but ad optimization platforms are generally privacy-invasive by design.

MangaReader.description: MangaReader is a WordPress theme designed for creating websites, offering customizable layouts and responsive design.
MangaReader.history: MangaReader is a free manga reading website that hosts thousands of manga titles and allows anonymous reading without login requirements. Multiple domains use the MangaReader brand (mangareader.to, mangareader.net, mangareader.in, etc.) serving primarily scanlated manga content. It has a large global readership with daily chapter uploads from scanlation groups.
MangaReader.summary_abuse: MangaReader hosts scanlated manga content which typically infringes on Japanese publishers' copyrights. The site operates in a legal grey area with no verified DMCA process. It serves anonymous users without KYC and displays third-party ads, which can include malvertising. Multiple domains operating under the same brand suggest a pattern of domain cycling common to piracy sites.

Mangeznotez.description: Mangeznotez is a restaurant table booking widget.
Mangeznotez.history: Mangeznotez is a French restaurant tech SaaS (brand of Resto Pro et MangezNotez) that provides digital solutions for restaurants, including website creation, SEO, online reservations, and a food ordering/delivery widget. It is primarily used by French restaurants.
Mangeznotez.summary_abuse: Mangeznotez is a small French restaurant management SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves restaurant businesses as a B2B tool and has a minimal public consumer surface.

Mangomint.description: Mangomint is a salon and spa management platform that centralizes scheduling, client records, payments, and operational tools for service-based businesses.
Mangomint.history: Mangomint is a US-based salon and spa management SaaS founded in 2017. It is the highest-rated scheduling software for hair salons, spas, nail salons, med spas, and related beauty businesses. The platform provides calendar scheduling, online booking, POS, inventory, staff management, and mobile apps for iOS and Android. It targets businesses with 5+ service providers.
Mangomint.summary_abuse: Mangomint is a legitimate B2B salon management SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It operates in the professional beauty industry serving salon and spa businesses.

Mantine.description: Mantine is an open-source UI framework and component library for React.
Mantine.history: Mantine is a MIT-licensed React UI component library created by Vitaly Rtishchev, first released around 2021. It provides 120+ fully-featured, accessible, and customizable components and hooks. It has grown to 30,000+ GitHub stars and 5M+ monthly downloads. The library has a rich ecosystem including form, notification, chart, and editor packages.
Mantine.summary_abuse: Mantine is a foundational open-source React UI library with no abuse signal. It is a client-side rendering utility with no server surface.

Mantis.description: Mantis is an end-to-end advertising network catering to brands operating in the legal cannabis industry and ancillary markets.
Mantis.history: Mantis Ad Network (mantisadnetwork.com) is a digital advertising network focused on publisher monetization. It provides programmatic ad technology for website publishers, typically operating as an advertising intermediary. Ad networks of this type collect user tracking data and serve targeted display advertising.
Mantis.summary_abuse: Mantis Ad Network is an advertising platform that, like most ad networks, collects behavioral tracking data from website visitors. Ad networks can be abused for malvertising campaigns. No specific documented abuse incidents found, but the privacy-invasive nature of ad tech is inherent to the business model.

MantisBT.description: MantisBT is an open-source, web-based issue tracking system written in PHP with a MySQL database backend, designed to facilitate bug tracking and project management for software development teams.
MantisBT.history: MantisBT (Mantis Bug Tracker) is an open-source PHP web-based bug tracking system, originally created around 2000 and released under the GPL-2.0 license. It supports MySQL, MS SQL, and PostgreSQL databases and has been available in 50 languages. It is widely self-hosted and has an active plugin ecosystem. A hosted SaaS version is offered via MantisHub.
MantisBT.summary_abuse: MantisBT is a self-hosted open-source bug tracker with no documented abuse signal. Its self-hosted model means the vendor has no consumer surface to abuse.

Manula.description: Manula is an online software for creating user manuals.
Manula.history: Manula is a web-based online manual creation and publishing SaaS. It allows businesses to create, maintain, and publish responsive online user manuals, user guides, and handbooks with search, analytics, version control, and PDF export. It is a small independent company, likely based in the Netherlands.
Manula.summary_abuse: Manula is a niche documentation SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves B2B customers publishing product manuals and user guides.

ManyChat.description: ManyChat is a service that allows you to create chatbots for Facebook Messenger.
ManyChat.history: ManyChat is a US-based chat marketing automation platform, founded in 2015. It is an official Meta Business Partner and one of the largest chatbot platforms globally, with 400,000+ business users across 190 countries. It enables automated conversations on Facebook Messenger, Instagram, WhatsApp, and SMS. Pricing starts at $15/month with a free tier for up to 1,000 contacts.
ManyChat.summary_abuse: ManyChat is widely used for legitimate marketing automation but also appears in spam campaigns due to its mass-messaging capabilities on Instagram and Facebook. As an official Meta partner it is subject to Meta platform policies, but its automation features can be and have been used for unsolicited DM campaigns and affiliate spam. Vendor responsiveness to abuse reports is generally adequate.

ManyContacts.description: ManyContacts is an attention-grabbing contact form sitting on top of your website that helps to increase conversion by converting visitors into leads.
ManyContacts.history: ManyContacts is a Spanish WhatsApp CRM and shared inbox platform for business teams. It allows teams to manage WhatsApp conversations collaboratively, with AI automation, lead funnels, and integrations with HubSpot, Salesforce, and Zapier. Pricing starts at €49/month for 3 agents with a 7-day free trial.
ManyContacts.summary_abuse: ManyContacts is a legitimate WhatsApp business CRM. WhatsApp-based tools inherently touch sensitive customer conversation data; no specific abuse incidents documented. The company appears GDPR-aware given its Spanish/EU base.

Mapbox GL JS.description: Mapbox GL JS is a JavaScript library that uses WebGL to render interactive maps from vector tiles and Mapbox styles.
Mapbox GL JS.history: Mapbox GL JS is the JavaScript SDK for Mapbox's commercial map rendering platform, first released in 2015. Mapbox Inc. was founded in 2010 by Eric Gundersen in Washington DC. In December 2020, Mapbox relicensed Mapbox GL JS from BSD to a proprietary, non-OSS license (effective from version 2.0), prompting the community to fork the project as MapLibre GL JS. Mapbox continues to maintain the proprietary version with active development.
Mapbox GL JS.summary_abuse: Mapbox GL JS is a widely used commercial mapping SDK with no documented abuse signal. It is a client-side JavaScript library for map rendering; all server-side tile access requires API keys tied to accounts.

Mapbox.js.description: Mapbox.js is a JavaScript library provided by Mapbox for working with interactive maps and geospatial data.
Mapbox.js.history: Mapbox.js is a legacy JavaScript map rendering library from Mapbox, built on top of Leaflet, released around 2013. It was the predecessor to the modern Mapbox GL JS SDK. Mapbox deprecated Mapbox.js in favor of Mapbox GL JS; it is no longer actively maintained but the repository remains accessible on GitHub.
Mapbox.js.summary_abuse: Mapbox.js is a deprecated legacy map rendering library with no abuse signal. It is a client-side JavaScript library with no server surface.

MapLibre GL JS.description: MapLibre GL JS is an open-source library for publishing maps on your websites.
MapLibre GL JS.history: MapLibre GL JS is a BSD-3-Clause licensed TypeScript library for WebGL-accelerated interactive maps from vector tiles. It was forked from Mapbox GL JS in December 2020 after Mapbox relicensed their library to a restrictive non-OSS license. MapLibre is governed by the MapLibre Organization and stewarded by a community of organizations including AWS, Microsoft, Meta, and others committed to open-source mapping.
MapLibre GL JS.summary_abuse: MapLibre GL JS is a foundational open-source mapping library with no abuse signal. It is a client-side JavaScript rendering library with no server surface.

Mapline.description: Mapline is a geo-powered analytics platform designed to deliver location-based insights worldwide.
Mapline.history: Mapline is a US-based geo-intelligent business intelligence SaaS, founded around 2012. It enables businesses to transform spreadsheet data into interactive maps for territory management, route optimization, and spatial analytics. Pricing starts at $99/month for basic mapping and $349/month for advanced analytics with territory management features.
Mapline.summary_abuse: Mapline is a legitimate B2B mapping analytics SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves businesses with spatial data visualization needs.

MapLoco.description: MapLoco is a tool that displays website visitor locations on an interactive map for analysis and tracking.
MapLoco.history: MapLoco (maploco.com) is a free web visitor map widget service that allows website and blog owners to embed a map showing the geographic location of their visitors. It also lets users create and share visited-countries or visited-states maps. It is a small free service that has existed since the mid-2000s.
MapLoco.summary_abuse: MapLoco is a small free map widget service with minimal abuse risk. It collects visitor IP geolocation data to display visitor maps, which has inherent privacy implications but no documented misuse pattern.

Mapme.description: Mapme is an interactive map builder that allows users to create custom maps.
Mapme.history: Mapme is a no-code interactive map creation platform founded in 2015, used by cities, NGOs, tourism boards, real estate firms, and event organizers. It allows users to create branded, embeddable maps with rich media, CSV import, and analytics. Maps are built on MapLibre.
Mapme.summary_abuse: Mapme is a legitimate no-code mapping SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves organizations and businesses for public-facing interactive maps.

MapMyChannel.description: MapMyChannel is a cloud-based fulfillment platform that integrates with ecommerce systems to automate inventory, shipping, and order management.
MapMyChannel.history: MapMyChannel is a small YouTube channel analytics and management platform that helps businesses manage their YouTube channel partnerships and analyze performance data. It is a niche analytics tool with limited public presence.
MapMyChannel.summary_abuse: MapMyChannel is a small niche YouTube analytics tool with no documented abuse signals. It operates as a B2B analytics platform with minimal public consumer surface.

Mapp.description: Mapp is designed specifically to help consumer-facing brands run highly personalised, cross-channel marketing campaigns powered by real-time customer data and generated insights.
Mapp.history: Mapp (formerly Webtrekk and before that a combination of several acquired European digital marketing companies) is a Germany-based multi-channel marketing automation platform. The current Mapp brand was established around 2015. It provides email, SMS, mobile push, in-app, and web messaging with analytics and personalization for 3,000+ enterprise clients globally.
Mapp.summary_abuse: Mapp is a legitimate enterprise marketing automation SaaS used for permission-based email and multi-channel campaigns. It operates in the marketing automation space where mass email capabilities could be misused, but the platform targets enterprise clients with documented compliance measures.

Mappedin.description: Mappedin is an all-in-one indoor mapping platform designed for scalability.
Mappedin.history: Mappedin is a Canadian indoor mapping and wayfinding SaaS, founded around 2012 in Waterloo, Ontario. It provides AI-powered indoor maps, digital directories, and wayfinding SDKs for enterprise venues including shopping malls (Simon, Tanger, Hudson Yards), airports, and hospitals. The platform supports mobile, web, and kiosk deployments with turn-by-turn blue-dot navigation.
Mappedin.summary_abuse: Mappedin is a legitimate enterprise B2B indoor mapping SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It operates exclusively in enterprise venue mapping with established retailer and venue customers.

Mapplic.description: Mapplic is a plugin for creating interactive maps based on simple image (jpg, png) or vector (svg) files.
Mapplic.history: Mapplic is a premium WordPress plugin for creating custom interactive maps from SVG images and vector graphics, sold on CodeCanyon. It supports unlimited floors, landmarks, zoom/pan, and is used for venue maps, floor plans, and geographic directories. It is a one-time purchase product sold by a small independent developer.
Mapplic.summary_abuse: Mapplic is a self-hosted WordPress plugin with no abuse signal. It renders static SVG maps client-side and has no hosted service surface.

MapPress.description: MapPress is a WordPress plugin that provides easy integration of interactive maps into WordPress websites.
MapPress.history: MapPress is a GPL-2.0 licensed WordPress plugin for embedding interactive Google Maps and Leaflet maps in WordPress posts and pages, created by Chris Richardson. It has millions of downloads and is one of the most popular WordPress mapping plugins. A commercial Pro version (MapPress Pro) with advanced features is also sold.
MapPress.summary_abuse: MapPress is a WordPress mapping plugin with no documented abuse signal. It embeds maps from Google Maps or Leaflet and has no consumer-facing hosted surface.

Maptalks.description: Maptalks is a light and plugable JavaScript library for integrated 2D/3D maps.
Maptalks.history: Maptalks is a BSD-3-Clause licensed open-source JavaScript library for integrated 2D/3D map rendering, created by a Chinese developer team and first published around 2016. It supports Canvas, WebGL, SVG, and CSS3/HTML layers for rendering geometries on maps. Available on npm as the 'maptalks' package and tracked on cdnjs.
Maptalks.summary_abuse: Maptalks is an open-source JavaScript map rendering library with no abuse signal. It is a client-side utility library with no server surface.

Maptiler.description: Maptiler is a mapping platform offering visual tools, global data, SDKs, and APIs for enterprise application development.
Maptiler.history: MapTiler was founded in 2008 by Petr Pridal as an open-source tool for turning digitised paper maps into tiled web maps. The legal entity behind the product is Klokan Technologies GmbH, headquartered in Zug, Switzerland. Over time it grew into a commercial mapping platform offering SDKs, APIs, and global map data. 500 million people view MapTiler-powered maps monthly. No notable acquisitions or ownership changes.
Maptiler.summary_abuse: MapTiler is a legitimate mapping infrastructure provider with no notable abuse signals. Its APIs are used for standard web and mobile mapping applications. The maps API surface is not meaningfully exploited for abuse relative to its large scale use.

MapTrack.description: MapTack is a platform to audit and manage assets from a single interface.
MapTrack.history: MapTrack is an Australian asset tracking, fleet management, and maintenance compliance SaaS founded in 2012. The platform was rebuilt and relaunched in 2022, combining GPS tracking, QR/barcode scanning, OEM telematics, work orders, and compliance registers. It has 500+ customers tracking 2M+ assets across 6+ countries. Per-asset pricing starts at $1 AUD/month.
MapTrack.summary_abuse: MapTrack is a legitimate Australian fleet and asset tracking B2B SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves industrial and field service businesses in a regulated GPS/fleet compliance space.

Marcando.description: Marcando is a SaaS ecommerce solution designed to streamline online retail operations.
Marcando.history: Marcando is a Belgium-based B2B e-commerce platform that integrates with ERP systems to automate online ordering, inventory sync, and customer portals. It serves 175+ SMEs with over 200,000 regular users and has facilitated over €100M in sales. The platform supports B2B ordering, customer portals, integrated webshops, and online catalogs.
Marcando.summary_abuse: Marcando is a legitimate Belgian B2B ecommerce SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves European SMEs in a business-to-business context with ERP integrations.

Marchex.description: Marchex is a B2B call and conversational analytics company.
Marchex.history: Marchex is a publicly traded US call analytics and conversation intelligence SaaS company, founded in 2003 in Seattle. It provides call tracking, dynamic number insertion (DNI), and AI-powered conversation analytics for marketing attribution. The platform helps enterprises attribute phone calls to marketing channels and provides insights on call outcomes, agent performance, and customer intent.
Marchex.summary_abuse: Marchex is a call tracking SaaS that collects detailed phone conversation data for marketing analytics. Dynamic number insertion technology tracks user journeys across marketing channels. No major public abuse incidents documented, though call recording and behavioral analytics involve significant data collection.

Marfeel.description: Marfeel is a publisher platform that allows publishers to create, optimise and monetise their mobile websites.
Marfeel.history: Marfeel was founded in 2011 in Barcelona by Xavi Beumala and Juan Margenat. It is a digital publisher platform providing mobile optimization, ad serving, CDN, Progressive Web App, AMP, and analytics tools for media and publishing websites. The platform delivers 600M+ optimized monthly sessions and has offices in Barcelona and New York.
Marfeel.summary_abuse: Marfeel is an ad-tech and publisher optimization platform that embeds multiple advertising and tracking scripts on publisher sites, resulting in heavy tracker density. As a programmatic advertising intermediary, the platform can expose publisher sites to malvertising risks typical of ad networks. No specific documented incidents found.

MariaDB.description: MariaDB is an open-source relational database management system compatible with MySQL.
MariaDB.history: MariaDB is an open-source community fork of MySQL, created in 2009 by Monty Widenius (the original MySQL creator) and other MySQL core team members after Oracle acquired Sun Microsystems (and thus MySQL). It is maintained by the MariaDB Foundation and MariaDB Corporation. The fork ensures an open, GPL-licensed MySQL-compatible database. Major adopters include Wikimedia Foundation, Google, Mozilla, and ServiceNow. In 2025 MariaDB rebooted its DBaaS strategy with open-source at the core.
MariaDB.summary_abuse: MariaDB is a foundational open-source relational database with no abuse signal. It is infrastructure software with no consumer-facing hosted surface in its self-hosted form.

Marionette.js.description: Marionette is a composite application library for Backbone.js that aims to simplify the construction of large scale JavaScript applications. It is a collection of common design and implementation patterns found in applications.
Marionette.js.history: Backbone.Marionette is a MIT-licensed JavaScript framework built on top of Backbone.js, designed to simplify large-scale single-page application development. It was created by Derick Bailey around 2012 and became popular as Backbone.js grew. With the rise of React, Angular, and Vue, usage declined significantly. The project is now in low maintenance/dormant state as most developers have migrated to modern frameworks.
Marionette.js.summary_abuse: Marionette.js is a legacy client-side JavaScript library with no abuse signal. It is a framework with no server surface.

Markate.description: Markate is an all-in-one CRM and sales and marketing automation platform.
Markate.history: Markate is a US-based field service management SaaS designed for small service businesses including HVAC, cleaning, maintenance, and home service contractors. It provides scheduling, dispatching, invoicing, customer management, and mobile-first tools starting at $5/user/month. Integrations include Google Calendar, Stripe, PayPal, Zapier, and Twilio.
Markate.summary_abuse: Markate is a legitimate field service management SaaS serving small contractors. No documented abuse signals. It is a B2B operations tool with no consumer-facing hosting surface.

Marked.history: Marked is a MIT-licensed JavaScript Markdown parser and compiler, first published around 2011 by Christopher Jeffrey. It is one of the most popular JavaScript Markdown libraries with 32,000+ GitHub stars and hundreds of millions of npm downloads. It runs in both the browser and Node.js and supports GitHub-flavored Markdown.
Marked.summary_abuse: Marked is a foundational JavaScript Markdown parsing library with no abuse signal. It is a client-side/server-side rendering utility with no hosted service surface.

Marker.description: Marker.io is an issue tracker solution for Project Managers, QA Testers and Agency Clients to report feedback & bugs to developers.
Marker.history: Marker.io is a website feedback and bug reporting SaaS that allows teams to capture annotated screenshots with technical metadata and submit them directly to project management tools like Jira, Trello, ClickUp, and Asana. It is operated by Marker.io SRL (a French company) and offers plans starting at $39/month with a 15-day trial.
Marker.summary_abuse: Marker.io is a legitimate website feedback SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves development and QA teams as a B2B tool for bug reporting.

MarketHero.description: Market Hero is a platform that automates ROI calculations for ecommerce businesses, streamlining financial performance tracking and providing data-driven insights.
MarketHero.history: Market Hero is a US-based email marketing automation and sales tracking platform created by Alex Becker, launched around 2015. It markets itself as a revenue-centric autoresponder that tracks email ROI. The platform later became associated with HYROS (formerly Market Hero's email product). It primarily targets small online businesses and internet marketers.
MarketHero.summary_abuse: MarketHero is an email marketing SaaS primarily serving internet marketers and affiliate marketers. Platforms targeting this demographic are often used for aggressive email campaigns. No major documented abuse incidents, but its target user base is associated with higher spam risk than enterprise email tools.

Marketify.description: Marketify is an app designed to engage audiences, expand email lists, and increase conversion rates.
Marketify.history: Marketify is a premium WordPress marketplace theme developed by Astoundify, released around 2013. It integrates with Easy Digital Downloads to create multi-vendor digital product marketplaces. Used by approximately 38 sites, it is a self-hosted WordPress theme sold with a one-time license.
Marketify.summary_abuse: Marketify is a self-hosted WordPress marketplace theme with no abuse signal. Sites using it could host user-submitted digital products, but no documented incidents of abuse specific to this theme.

Marketo.description: Marketo develops and sells marketing automation software for account-based marketing and other marketing services and products including SEO and content creation.
Marketo.history: Marketo was founded in 2006 in San Mateo, California as a B2B marketing automation platform. It went public in 2013. In 2018 Adobe acquired Marketo for $4.75 billion and rebranded it as Adobe Marketo Engage, integrating it into Adobe Experience Cloud. It is the leading marketing automation platform for enterprise B2B, named a Leader in the 2024 Gartner Magic Quadrant for B2B Marketing Automation.
Marketo.summary_abuse: Marketo is a dominant enterprise marketing automation platform used for permission-based email and multi-channel B2B campaigns. As a mass-email platform it has historically appeared in spam research, but Adobe enforces strong compliance controls and responds to abuse reports. No major documented incidents post-acquisition.

Marketo Forms.description: Marketo Forms help create web forms without programming knowledge. Forms can reside on Marketo landing pages and also be embedded on any page of website.
Marketo Forms.history: Marketo Forms is the web form component of Adobe Marketo Engage, the enterprise B2B marketing automation platform. Marketo forms are JavaScript-embedded lead capture forms used on corporate websites to capture prospect data into Marketo's CRM and lead database. They are part of the Adobe Marketo Engage platform, which was acquired by Adobe in 2018 for $4.75 billion.
Marketo Forms.summary_abuse: Marketo Forms are enterprise B2B lead capture forms. Like all web form technologies they can be imitated in phishing pages, but the forms themselves require Marketo account credentials. No specific abuse pattern documented for this particular component.

Marketpath CMS.description: Marketpath CMS is a hosted website content management system used by businesses, churches and schools.
Marketpath CMS.history: Marketpath CMS is a US-based cloud SaaS CMS founded in 2001, based in Indianapolis, Indiana. It provides fully managed website hosting, automatic updates, SEO tools, customer portals, and API access. It targets small businesses, churches, and schools. The platform offers 99.9% uptime guarantees with included technical support.
Marketpath CMS.summary_abuse: Marketpath CMS is a legitimate niche SaaS CMS serving small businesses and nonprofits with no documented abuse signals. It is a managed hosting platform with no anonymous or anonymous content hosting.

MarketPlan.description: MarketPlan is a platform that integrates campaign mapping, simulation, collaboration, and smart analytics, all in one place.
MarketPlan.history: MarketPlan.io is a US-based marketing campaign planning and visualization SaaS, founded around 2018. It provides visual funnel mapping, live campaign analytics, Kanban boards, and Gantt charts for marketing teams and agencies. Pricing starts at $9/month with a free tier available.
MarketPlan.summary_abuse: MarketPlan.io is a niche marketing planning SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves marketing teams and freelancers as a B2B planning tool.

Marko.description: Marko is HTML re-imagined as a language for building dynamic and reactive user interfaces.
Marko.history: Marko is a MIT-licensed JavaScript UI framework created by eBay in 2012 to power ebay.com. It is notable for its superior server-side rendering (SSR) performance. eBay contributed Marko to the OpenJS Foundation to ensure open governance. Marko 5 was released with improved reactivity. eBay-employed engineers continue to actively maintain it. It powers most of ebay.com's UI.
Marko.summary_abuse: Marko is a foundational open-source UI framework with no abuse signal. It is a client-side/server-side JavaScript rendering library with no hosted service surface.

Marquiz.description: Marquiz is an online tool designed for creating user-friendly forms, quizzes, and surveys tailored for digital marketing.
Marquiz.history: Marquiz is a quiz-based lead generation SaaS platform, founded around 2017. It allows marketers to create interactive quiz funnels to pre-qualify and capture leads, with 150+ integrations to CRMs and email tools. Clients have reportedly generated over 200 million leads through the platform. Pricing is based on the number of monthly leads generated.
Marquiz.summary_abuse: Marquiz is a quiz funnel marketing SaaS with no specific documented abuse incidents. Quiz lead generation tools can be used for aggressive data harvesting, but the platform serves legitimate marketing teams. Integrations with major CRMs suggest a business-oriented customer base.

Mars Flag.description: Mars Flag is a search platform provider originating from Japan, known for its leading domestic market share in website search services.
Mars Flag.history: MARS FLAG Corporation is a Japanese IT company providing website search (MARS FINDER), DX/AI chat, and API integration cloud services. The MARS FLAG technology appears as a website search widget detected by tools like BuiltWith and Wappalyzer on approximately 422 websites, primarily in Japan.
Mars Flag.summary_abuse: Mars Flag is a Japanese website search SaaS widget with no documented abuse signals. It operates as a niche B2B tool primarily used by Japanese businesses.

Marsello.description: Marsello is an omnichannel loyalty platform that integrates with sales channels, enhancing customer engagement and retention by providing a unified experience across various points of sale.
Marsello.history: Marsello is a New Zealand-based loyalty and marketing automation SaaS platform, founded around 2015. It provides omnichannel loyalty programs, email, SMS, and marketing automation for retail and hospitality brands. It integrates with Shopify, BigCommerce, LightSpeed, and major POS systems. Customers include Lego Certified Stores, Merrell, and 4,000+ brands.
Marsello.summary_abuse: Marsello is a legitimate loyalty and marketing SaaS with no documented abuse signals. It serves established retail and hospitality brands with verified business accounts.

Marshal.description: Marshal is a platform that ensures businesses achieve compliance and accessibility.
Marshal.history: Marshal (usemarshal.co) is a free cloud security scanning SaaS that scans Box, Dropbox, Google Drive, Microsoft OneDrive, and Onehub to detect exposed sensitive data such as SSNs, credit card numbers, and email addresses. It processes files without storing copies on disk, delivering sanitized reports of findings. It has scanned nearly 2 million files.
Marshal.summary_abuse: Marshal is a security scanning tool designed to detect data exposure, not enable it. No abuse signals. It operates as a privacy-protective SaaS tool for individuals and businesses concerned about sensitive data exposure in cloud storage.

MarsX.description: MarsX is an AI-powered coding platform designed to develop Software as a Service (SaaS) tools.
MarsX.history: MarsX is a US-based AI-powered low-code/no-code development platform, launched around 2021. It combines AI, no-code, and code tools to allow developers and non-developers to build web and mobile applications using a Micro AppStore of pre-built components. It targets solo developers building SaaS products quickly.
MarsX.summary_abuse: MarsX is a small AI-powered low-code platform with no documented abuse signals. It is a developer tool with limited public consumer surface.

Mashore Method.description: Mashore Method is a CRM software designed for real estate businesses to manage customer relationships in one centralized platform.
Mashore Method.history: Mashore Method is a real estate coaching and CRM platform built by Krista Mashore, a US-based real estate agent who sold over 2,300 homes during a 19-year career. She launched her coaching company around 2017, growing it to nearly $72 million in online sales within seven years. The platform provides websites, IDX integration, social media planners, sales funnels, automations, and reputation management tools for real estate agents.
Mashore Method.summary_abuse: Mashore Method is a niche real estate coaching and CRM SaaS with no documented abuse signals. Its user base consists of real estate agents seeking digital marketing tools. No notable incidents or complaints regarding data misuse or fraudulent conduct have been identified.

Masonry.description: Masonry is a JavaScript library that enables a cascading grid layout, positioning elements based on available vertical space for an optimized, gap-free arrangement.
Masonry.history: Masonry is a JavaScript cascading grid layout library created by David DeSandro, first released around 2010. It places elements in optimal position based on available vertical space, similar to a mason fitting stones in a wall. The library has been widely adopted across the web and is available via npm (masonry-layout), CDN, and supports both jQuery and vanilla JS. GitHub repository at desandro/masonry has accumulated approximately 17,000 stars.
Masonry.summary_abuse: Masonry is a purely frontend layout library with no server-side surface area. It has no abuse signals and is not meaningfully exploited in malicious contexts.

Massflow.description: Massflow is a platform offering analytics for businesses, providing tools to evaluate performance, identify trends, and make data-driven decisions.
