Massflow.history: Massflow is a user analytics SaaS offering heatmaps, live session recordings, and visitor analytics for websites. It is a small independent product targeting SMBs as an affordable alternative to tools like Hotjar. No notable acquisitions or significant milestones have been documented.
Massflow.summary_abuse: Massflow is a user analytics tool that records visitor behavior (clicks, mouse movements, session replays) on customer websites. Like all session-recording SaaS tools, it handles behavioral data that must be disclosed to end users under GDPR and similar laws. No specific abuse incidents or malicious use cases have been identified for Massflow specifically.

Master Slider.description: Master Slider is an SEO friendly, responsive image and video slider.
Master Slider.history: Master Slider is a responsive touch slider plugin for WordPress, originally released around 2014 by averta on CodeCanyon. It offers smooth hardware-accelerated transitions, touch navigation, and a free version via WordPress.org with a premium Pro edition. No notable ownership changes or deprecation events.
Master Slider.summary_abuse: Master Slider is a standard WordPress plugin with no notable abuse history. It presents minimal attack surface as a client-side slider; the primary security concern is plugin vulnerabilities in the WordPress ecosystem, not intentional misuse. Vendor is a solo/small developer with no published AUP or abuse reporting mechanism.

Master Slider Plugin.description: Master Slider Plugin is an SEO friendly, responsive image and video slider plugin for WordPress.
Master Slider Plugin.history: Master Slider Plugin is a WordPress touch slider available on WordPress.org, developed by averta. Released around 2014 with a free tier and a premium Pro version sold on CodeCanyon. Actively maintained as of 2026 with compatibility updates for the WordPress translation service.
Master Slider Plugin.summary_abuse: No notable abuse history. Standard WordPress plugin used for image slideshows. Security exposure limited to typical WordPress plugin vulnerabilities; not exploited in any documented mass-scale campaign.

Mastercard.description: MasterCard facilitates electronic funds transfers throughout the world, most commonly through branded credit cards, debit cards and prepaid cards.
Mastercard.history: Mastercard was founded in 1966 when a consortium of California banks formed the Interbank Card Association, creating 'Master Charge: The Interbank Card' as a competitor to BankAmericard (later Visa). It was renamed Mastercard in 1979 and expanded internationally through Eurocard partnerships beginning in 1968. The company became publicly traded on the NYSE in 2006. Today it is one of the two dominant global payment networks.
Mastercard.summary_abuse: Mastercard itself is foundational payment infrastructure with robust fraud controls. While card-not-present fraud, BIN attacks, and carding operations do target Mastercard-branded cards, these represent attacks on the broader payment ecosystem rather than abuse of Mastercard's infrastructure. The company actively invests in fraud detection and has a published responsible disclosure program.

Masteriyo.description: Masteriyo is a course creation and monetization tool for WordPress.
Masteriyo.history: Masteriyo is an LMS plugin for WordPress developed by wpeverest (Nepal), launched around 2021. It competes with LearnDash and TutorLMS by offering built-in payments, quizzes, and certificates at a lower price point. Over 4,000 course creators use the platform. No notable ownership changes.
Masteriyo.summary_abuse: Masteriyo has no notable abuse history. As a WordPress LMS plugin, it is a self-hosted tool with no content-hosting surface for bad actors. Security exposure is limited to WordPress plugin vulnerability disclosures.

MasterkinG32 Framework.history: MasterkinG32 Framework is a niche open-source PHP or web framework developed by MasterkinG32, a small independent developer. It has minimal documentation and community activity. No notable events or ownership changes are documented.
MasterkinG32 Framework.summary_abuse: No notable abuse history. Niche framework with very limited adoption, presenting minimal attack surface.

Mastery Manager.description: Mastery Manager is a student learning system designed to track academic progress, evaluate performance, and support data-driven instructional planning.
Mastery Manager.history: Mastery Manager is a niche SaaS platform designed for competency and skills tracking, typically used in educational or workforce training contexts. Limited publicly available history; appears to be a small independent product.
Mastery Manager.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Small niche training management tool with limited public exposure.

Mastodon.description: Mastodon is a free and open-source self-hosted social networking service.
Mastodon.history: Mastodon was created in 2016 by German developer Eugen Rochko as a decentralized, open-source alternative to Twitter, using the ActivityPub protocol to federate independent server instances. It gained massive user surges following Elon Musk's Twitter acquisition in 2022. Mastodon gGmbH, a US 501(c)(3) non-profit, was established to support its development. As of 2025 it has around 10 million registered users.
Mastodon.summary_abuse: Mastodon instances can host harassment, spam, and CSAM if instance operators have weak moderation policies, since each instance sets its own rules. The federated model means problematic instances may defederate from mainstream ones but can still operate. The software itself has been used by extremist communities on some self-hosted instances. Mainstream hosted instances generally enforce strict codes of conduct.

Matajer.description: Matajer is an ecommerce platform from Saudi Arabia.
Matajer.history: Matajer is a Saudi Arabian e-commerce platform builder allowing merchants to create online stores quickly without coding. It is localized for the Saudi market with support for Mada, Visa, Mastercard, and local shipping integrations. Founded circa 2018 in Saudi Arabia; limited public history.
Matajer.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Regional e-commerce builder focused on Saudi Arabia; some risk of counterfeit or fraudulent storefronts as with any hosted e-commerce platform, but no public incidents on record.

Matchi.description: Matchi is a platform for racket sports enthusiasts to search, book, and play tennis, badminton, table tennis, squash, or padel.
Matchi.history: MATCHi was founded in 2012 in Sweden by Daniel Ekman as an online booking and matchmaking network for racket sports (tennis, padel, badminton, squash). It has grown into a venue management and player matching platform used across Europe and internationally. Backed by Verdane. No major ownership changes.
Matchi.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. MATCHi is a niche sports booking platform with limited consumer-facing risk. Data collected includes user profiles and booking history; GDPR-relevant but no reported incidents.

Matchlab.description: Matchlab is a recruitment platform that enables employers to attract top talent by directly identifying and engaging the best candidates.
Matchlab.history: Matchlab is a Dutch recruitment marketing and employer-branding technology company that helps organizations build careers websites and optimize their hiring funnels. It operates primarily in the Netherlands. Founded circa 2015; limited public history beyond its core service offering.
Matchlab.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Niche B2B recruitment tool with limited consumer-facing surface.

Material Design Lite.description: Material Design Lite is a library of components for web developers.
Material Design Lite.history: Material Design Lite (MDL) was released by Google in July 2015 as a CSS/HTML/JS library to bring Material Design to static websites without requiring a JavaScript framework. It was deprecated in favor of Material Components for the Web (MDC Web) which Google actively maintains. MDL is now in limited support mode with no new development from the core team.
Material Design Lite.summary_abuse: No abuse history. MDL is a deprecated Google CSS library for frontend design. It is a client-side library loaded by end user browsers and presents no server-side attack surface.

Material UI.description: Material UI is a simple and customisable component library to build faster, beautiful, and more accessible React applications.
dom: style[data-meta='MuiPaper'], div.MuiBox-root, div.MuiPaper-root, style[data-meta='MuiButton'], input.MuiInputBase-input, div.MuiContainer-root, .MuiButton-root
Material UI.history: Material UI (now MUI) started in 2014 as an open-source React implementation of Google's Material Design guidelines. It became the most popular React UI component library. In 2021, the company rebranded from Material-UI to MUI with the release of v5, broadening beyond Material Design to include Joy UI and other design systems. MUI is VC-backed and commercially supported.
Material UI.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Material UI / MUI is a widely-used open-source React component library. It is a client-side library with no server-side surface and is not exploited in any documented abuse patterns.

Materialize CSS.description: Materialize CSS is a front-end framework that helps developers create responsive and mobile-first web applications. It is based on Google's Material Design guidelines.
Materialize CSS.history: Materialize CSS is an open-source responsive front-end framework based on Google's Material Design, created by students at Carnegie Mellon University in 2014. It provides CSS/JS components similar to Bootstrap. Development has slowed in recent years compared to Bootstrap and MUI but still maintains a significant install base.
Materialize CSS.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Materialize CSS is a client-side CSS framework with no server-side component and no documented misuse patterns.

math.js.description: Math.js a JavaScript library that provides a comprehensive set of mathematical functions and capabilities for performing complex calculations and operations in web applications.
math.js.history: math.js was created by Jos de Jong in 2013 as a comprehensive mathematics library for JavaScript and Node.js, supporting complex numbers, matrices, units, and symbolic computation. It is actively maintained by its creator and contributors.
math.js.summary_abuse: math.js has no notable abuse signal; it is a mathematics computation library with no server-side component or consumer-facing surface.

MathJax.description: MathJax is a cross-browser JavaScript library that displays mathematical notation in web browsers, using MathML, LaTeX and ASCIIMathML markup.
MathJax.history: MathJax began in 2009 as a successor to jsMath (2004), launched through a partnership between the American Mathematical Society, Design Science, and SIAM. It renders LaTeX, MathML, and AsciiMath in browsers. Version 3.0 (2019) was a full TypeScript rewrite with improved modular architecture. Version 4.0 was released in 2025. It is widely used by academic publishers and educational platforms.
MathJax.summary_abuse: No abuse history. MathJax is a math rendering library used by academic and educational platforms. It is a client-side script with no server-side component and no documented misuse patterns.

Matjrah.description: Matjrah is an ecommerce platform for building online stores.
Matjrah.history: Matjrah is an early-stage Saudi Arabian e-commerce platform builder based in Jeddah, competing with Salla and other regional platforms. It received pre-seed funding and targets local Saudi merchants. Limited publicly available history.
Matjrah.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Regional e-commerce platform with potential for fraudulent storefronts typical of any hosted commerce builder. No public incidents on record.

Matomo Analytics.description: Matomo Analytics is a free and open-source web analytics application, that runs on a PHP/MySQL web-server.
Matomo Analytics.history: Matomo (formerly Piwik) was started in 2007 by Matthieu Aubry as an open-source Google Analytics alternative. Renamed Matomo in January 2018 ('honesty' in Japanese). In 2013, Matthieu Aubry and Maciej Zawadziński co-founded Piwik PRO (a separate enterprise fork). Clearcode bought out Aubry from Piwik PRO in 2016. The core Matomo project is used by over 1 million websites and ~6% of top 10k global sites.
Matomo Analytics.summary_abuse: Matomo is a privacy-first analytics platform designed to avoid the data-collection practices it competes against. It is self-hosted by default and collects no PII by default. No documented abuse incidents. It actively positions itself as the privacy-respecting alternative to Google Analytics.

Matomo Tag Manager.description: Matomo Tag Manager manages tracking and marketing tags.
Matomo Tag Manager.history: Matomo Tag Manager was released as an open-source beta in June 2018 by the Matomo Analytics team. It is the only self-hosted open-source tag management system, bundled with Matomo Analytics. It serves as a privacy-respecting alternative to Google Tag Manager.
Matomo Tag Manager.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Matomo Tag Manager is the privacy-focused open-source alternative to Google Tag Manager. It is self-hosted and requires no third-party data sharing. No known misuse patterns.

MATORI.NET.description: MATORI.NET is a fully managed reverse proxy.
MATORI.NET.history: MATORI.NET is a fully managed reverse proxy service. No notable history events documented.
MATORI.NET.summary_abuse: MATORI.NET is a reverse proxy service. Reverse proxies can be used to mask malicious origins or bypass geographic restrictions, though no specific abuse incidents have been documented for this service.

Mattaki.description: Mattaki is an automotive platform from Australia.
Mattaki.history: Mattaki is a Sydney, Australia-based web development platform launched in 2009 specifically for automotive dealerships. It provides managed CMS websites optimized for inventory display, lead management, and mobile/SEO. It serves Australian dealers with a flat monthly subscription model.
Mattaki.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Niche automotive dealer web platform with limited consumer-facing risk beyond standard website concerns.

Matter.js.description: Matter.js is a JavaScript 2D rigid body physics engine for the web.
Matter.js.history: Matter.js is a 2D rigid body physics engine for the web created by Liam Brummitt (liabru) around 2013. It enables complex physics simulations in browsers using JavaScript and HTML5 Canvas. It became a popular choice for web-based games and interactive demos, with over 16k GitHub stars. Maintained by its solo creator.
Matter.js.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Matter.js is a client-side JavaScript physics library used for games and interactive web content. It has no server-side component and presents no abuse surface.

Mattermost.history: Mattermost originated as an internal messaging tool at SpinPunch (game studio), was open-sourced in 2015, and Mattermost, Inc. was formally established in 2016 in Palo Alto, CA by Ian Tien. Backed by Battery Ventures, Redpoint, YC Continuity. Serves 800,000+ workspaces including NASA, European Parliament, and Nasdaq. Positioned as the Slack alternative for security-conscious enterprises.
html: <noscript> To use Mattermost, please enable JavaScript\. </noscript>
Mattermost.summary_abuse: Mattermost is a self-hosted or cloud messaging platform for enterprise teams. Abuse risk is low as it is deployed in controlled corporate environments. No documented public incidents of mass abuse. Its on-prem option is highly favored by defense and regulated industries for security.

Mautic.description: Mautic is a free and open-source marketing automation tool for Content Management, Social Media, Email Marketing, and can be used for the integration of social networks, campaign management, forms, questionnaires, reports, etc.
Mautic.history: Mautic was created by DB Hurley in 2014 as the world's first open-source marketing automation platform under GPL v3. In 2019, Acquia became a major enterprise partner through acquisition. Mautic is now governed as a community-led open-source project used by approximately 250,000 organizations in 35 languages. Acquia and the community jointly steward the project.
Mautic.summary_abuse: Mautic is a self-hosted marketing automation platform capable of sending mass email campaigns. When deployed by organizations without proper list hygiene or recipient consent, it can be used for spam campaigns. The tool itself is legitimate, but its email-sending capabilities make it attractive for spammers on compromised servers. No abuse from the Mautic project itself is documented.

Mava.description: Mava is an AI-driven customer support platform that scales customer support and success by connecting to private, group, and community channels.
Mava.history: Mava is a customer support platform launched around 2021, purpose-built for community-driven companies using Discord, Telegram, and Slack. It combines a shared inbox with AI-powered auto-responses (GPT-4 based). Targeted at Web3 and startup communities. Venture-backed startup.
Mava.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Mava is a B2B customer support tool. Its AI-powered responses are controlled by the operating organization. No mass-abuse patterns identified.

Mavo.description: Mavo is a JavaScript library that enables web developers to turn regular HTML into reactive web applications without the need for writing custom JavaScript.
Mavo.history: Mavo is a reactive data-driven web authoring library created by Lea Verou et al. at MIT CSAIL, presented at UIST 2016. It allows HTML authors to build CRUD web apps without JavaScript. Originally developed as part of Lea Verou's MIT PhD research. Maintained as an open-source project under MIT license; development has slowed in recent years.
Mavo.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Mavo is a niche academic-origin web library with very limited adoption. No documented misuse patterns.

Max Mega Menu.description: Max Mega Menu is a popular WordPress plugin that enhances the menu functionality on WordPress websites.
Max Mega Menu.history: Max Mega Menu is a WordPress plugin for creating sophisticated navigation mega menus, launched around 2013. It has over 400,000 active installations on WordPress.org. Available as a free version plus a Pro upgrade starting at $29. Developed by an independent UK developer, actively maintained.
Max Mega Menu.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Standard WordPress navigation plugin with no server-side attack surface beyond typical WordPress plugin vulnerabilities.

MaxCDN.description: MaxCDN is a content delivery network, which accelerates web-sites and decreases the server load.
MaxCDN.history: MaxCDN was founded in 2009 as a content delivery network under NetDNA, LLC. It was acquired by StackPath in 2016 for its CDN assets. StackPath itself ceased all operations in June 2024 after selling its enterprise CDN contracts to Akamai in 2023. MaxCDN is thus fully defunct as of 2024.
MaxCDN.summary_abuse: MaxCDN was a legitimate CDN with no notable abuse patterns. It is now defunct after StackPath wound down operations in 2024.

Maxemail.history: Maxemail was founded as Emailcenter UK in 2001 by Nigel Williams in Northamptonshire, UK. It served 200+ enterprise customers in travel, e-commerce, insurance, and government verticals. In March 2018, it was acquired by Xtremepush, an Irish multichannel analytics and engagement company. The product was rebranded as Maxemail by Xtremepush.
Maxemail.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Maxemail is an enterprise email marketing platform with managed deliverability. Its use case (enterprise email marketing) requires legitimate sender identity and is not associated with spam campaigns at the platform level.

MaxenceDEVCMS.description: MaxenceDEVCMS is a simple CMS for ecommerce, esport, landing page.
MaxenceDEVCMS.history: MaxenceDEVCMS is a simple open-source CMS for ecommerce, esports, and landing pages created by a French developer. Very small project with no notable history events.
MaxenceDEVCMS.summary_abuse: MaxenceDEVCMS is a simple open-source CMS with no notable abuse signal.

MaxiCMS.description: MaxiCMS is a content management system (CMS) that allows website owners to manage and update content independently.
MaxiCMS.history: MaxiCMS is a cloud-based, closed-source CMS developed in the Netherlands for small and medium businesses. It is offered through Dutch web design agencies as a white-label managed website system. Pricing starts around €17.50/month. Very limited international presence.
MaxiCMS.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Regional Dutch CMS with very limited adoption outside the Netherlands.

MaxMind.description: MaxMind is a provider of geolocation and online fraud detection tools.
MaxMind.history: MaxMind was founded in 2002 by Thomas Mather in Malden, Massachusetts, coining the term 'GeoIP'. It created the GeoIP database family and the GeoLite free dataset. In 2004, it launched the minFraud fraud detection service. It developed the open MMDB binary format for IP lookups and grew organically to $11.3M revenue serving 100,000+ customers by 2024.
MaxMind.summary_abuse: MaxMind's GeoIP data is widely used in legitimate fraud prevention and analytics. Its data can be misused for geofencing malicious content by region or to circumvent IP reputation checks, but MaxMind itself is a trustworthy provider. The free GeoLite database is sometimes used to build crude geolocation-based phishing pages, but this is incidental misuse of public data.

MaxSite CMS.description: MaxSite CMS is a PHP-based content management system from Ukraine.
MaxSite CMS.history: MaxSite CMS is an open-source PHP/MySQL CMS developed in Ukraine, positioned as a lightweight alternative to WordPress. It has minimal global adoption (~81 live sites tracked by Wappalyzer) and is primarily used in the CIS region. Actively maintained on GitHub by its solo developer.
MaxSite CMS.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Very niche CMS with low global adoption. Self-hosted nature means no central abuse vector.

Maxxton.description: Maxxton is a reservation and property management system.
Maxxton.history: Maxxton was founded in 1998 in the Netherlands as a hospitality reservation and property management software provider. It specializes in the vacation rental and holiday park sector, managing over 100,000 units and 1.5 million reservations annually. Clients include Roompot, EuroParcs, and Siblu. Has offices in Netherlands, France, and India.
Maxxton.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Maxxton is an enterprise B2B hospitality SaaS with no consumer-facing surface relevant to fraud or scam patterns.

Mazrica.description: Mazrica is a sales support tool that leverages organizational knowledge, using AI algorithms to analyze accumulated sales data and evaluate success and failure cases.
Mazrica.history: Mazrica Inc. was founded in 2015 in Tokyo. The company developed Mazrica Sales (formerly Senses), an AI-assisted CRM/SFA tool tailored for Japanese sales teams. It raised $10.6M from Mitsubishi UFJ Capital and NTT Docomo Ventures (Series C). Primarily serves the Japanese domestic market.
Mazrica.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Mazrica is a B2B CRM platform for Japanese SMBs with no consumer-facing abuse surface.

mdBook.description: mdBook is a utility to create modern online books from Markdown files.
mdBook.history: mdBook is a documentation tool for creating online books from Markdown files, developed in Rust. It was created by the Rust community and is the official tool used to build the Rust programming language documentation. No notable ownership changes.
mdBook.summary_abuse: mdBook has no notable abuse signal; it is a static documentation generator with no server-side component or consumer-facing hosted service.

MDBootstrap.description: MDBootstrap (Material Design for Bootstrap) is a complete UI package that can be integrated with other frameworks such as Angular, React, Vue, etc. It is used to design a fully responsive and mobile-friendly layout using various components, plugins, animation.
MDBootstrap.history: MDBootstrap (MDB) is a Material Design UI kit built on Bootstrap. Created around 2015 by a Polish team. Offers React, Vue, Angular, and vanilla JS versions. 3M+ developers use it; trusted by IKEA, Samsung, Amazon, and NASA.
MDBootstrap.summary_abuse: MDBootstrap is a legitimate open-source UI framework. No abuse risk. Widely used in production web development.

MDBootstrap WP theme.description: MDBootstrap WP theme is a WordPress theme built on top of the MDBootstrap front-end UI library. It provides a set of pre-designed layouts, widgets, and components that can be easily customised and integrated into WordPress websites.
MDBootstrap WP theme.history: MDBootstrap WP Theme is an unofficial WordPress starter theme built with Bootstrap 4 and Material Design, created by developer BobDempsey as a tutorial project. Published on GitHub; less than 0.1% market share in WordPress themes. Community-made, not officially supported by MDBootstrap (the Polish UI framework company).
MDBootstrap WP theme.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Community-built tutorial WordPress theme with minimal usage and no known issues.

MDirector.description: MDirector is an all-in-one digital marketing platform that centralizes email, SMS, and automation tools to manage campaigns and analyze audience engagement.
MDirector.history: MDirector is a Spanish email marketing and marketing automation platform launched in 2007 in Madrid. Part of ISPD Network Holding. Serves 20+ countries with 17 employees and offices in Madrid, Barcelona, Milan, Paris, New York, Colombia, and Mexico City. Provides email, SMS, WhatsApp, pop-ups, CRM, and automation.
MDirector.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Established Spanish email marketing platform with legitimate operations in multiple countries.

MDS Brand.description: MDS Brand is a provider of website, CRM, vrtual BDC, SEO, PPC, and live chat solutions for Marine, RV, Powersports, and automotive industries.
MDS Brand.history: MDS Brand is a US-based digital marketing platform specializing in website design, CRM, SEO, and lead generation for boat and RV dealers and OEMs. Notable products include CallersIQ CRM, ModelMixer 3D boat configurator, and MADIS AI inventory management. Only ~37 live websites use the platform.
MDS Brand.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Niche US digital marketing platform serving boat and RV dealers with specialized tools.

MDUI.description: MDUI is a CSS Framework based on material design.
MDUI.history: MDUI is a Material Design 3 (Material You) UI component library using Web Components, developed by Chinese developer zdhxiong. Started around 2016 as a CSS framework; v2 rebuilt with Web Components and TypeScript. Supports Vue, React, and Angular. MIT licensed.
MDUI.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. MIT-licensed Material Design UI library by an independent Chinese developer with clean history.

Measured.description: Measured is an analytics platform to measure efficiency of marketing channels.
Measured.history: Measured was founded in 2017 as a marketing measurement platform for consumer brands, specializing in incrementality-based media attribution. It is privacy-resilient by design (not relying on user-level tracking) and serves enterprise brands for cross-channel ad spend optimization. Venture-backed.
Measured.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Measured is a B2B marketing analytics platform used to optimize ad spending. No consumer-facing abuse surface.

Meazy.description: Meazy is a tool that provides automated remarketing solutions tailored for online stores.
Meazy.history: Meazy was founded in 2018 in Vilnius, Lithuania as a retargeting ad automation platform for e-commerce. It received seed funding from Startup Wise Guys in 2019. The company is documented as deadpooled and inactive.
Meazy.summary_abuse: Meazy was a retargeting/ad-tech platform that placed tracking pixels on e-commerce sites and displayed personalized banner ads. Ad-tech platforms inherently collect behavioral data across sites; Meazy's defunct status makes current risk moot, but its pixel-based tracking model carried standard ad-tech privacy concerns.

Medallia.description: Medallia (formerly Kampyle) is a complete customer feedback platform that helps digital enterprises listen, understand, and act across all digital touch-points.
Medallia.history: Medallia was founded in 2001 by Borge Hald and Amy Pressman in San Francisco. It launched its SaaS customer experience management (CEM) platform in 2003 and went public on NYSE (MDLA) in July 2019. In October 2021, Thoma Bravo acquired Medallia in an all-cash transaction valued at $6.4 billion, taking it private. The company serves hospitality, retail, financial services, and B2B verticals.
Medallia.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Medallia is an enterprise customer experience platform collecting survey and feedback data. Privacy risk is limited to its handling of customer PII for enterprise clients; it maintains GDPR and HIPAA compliance programs.

Medchat.description: Medchat is a HIPAA-compliant platform that provides patient chat services powered by AI agents.
Medchat.history: Medchat (medchat.ai) is a US-based AI-powered healthcare chatbot platform for patient communication, automating workflows across providers, payers, and life sciences. It enables no-code AI agent deployment across phone, chat, SMS, and email channels. HIPAA-compliant. Founded circa 2015.
Medchat.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Medchat handles sensitive patient health data (PHI) and maintains HIPAA compliance. Risk is limited to healthcare data privacy; no documented security incidents. Not associated with fraud or scam patterns.

Media.net.description: Media.net is an advertising company focused on providing monetization products to digital publishers.
Media.net.history: Media.net was founded around 2010 by Divyank Turakhia and Bhavin Turakhia in Dubai, UAE. It powers the Yahoo! Bing Network Contextual Ads program through a partnership announced in 2012. It became one of the world's five largest ad tech companies and the second-largest contextual advertising business. In 2016, a Chinese consortium led by Zhang Zhiyong acquired Media.net for $900M.
Media.net.summary_abuse: Media.net is an ad network that places contextual advertising across publisher websites. Like other ad networks, it can be exploited to serve malvertisements or inappropriate ads, and its pixels track user behavior across sites. There have been reports of low-quality or misleading ad placements on some publisher sites. Vendor has an AUP but enforcement of ad quality varies.

MediaElement.js.description: MediaElement.js is a set of custom Flash plugins that mimic the HTML5 MediaElement API for browsers that don't support HTML5 or don't support the media codecs.
MediaElement.js.history: MediaElement.js is an open-source HTML5 audio/video player library created around 2010 by John Dyer. It provides a unified player API across browsers and supports MP4, WebM, HLS, DASH, YouTube, and more. Widely used as the default media player in WordPress core. Actively maintained on GitHub.
MediaElement.js.summary_abuse: No abuse history. MediaElement.js is a client-side media player library. It presents no server-side attack surface and is not associated with any known misuse patterns.

Mediahawk.description: Mediahawk is a call analytics software for marketers.
Mediahawk.history: Mediahawk is a UK-based call tracking and marketing attribution analytics platform with over 20 years of experience, serving blue-chip enterprise clients. It tracks phone calls from digital and offline marketing channels and provides AI-powered speech analytics. Headquartered in the UK.
Mediahawk.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Mediahawk is a B2B call analytics tool that collects marketing attribution data. Privacy risk is primarily GDPR-relevant call recording; no documented malicious use.

MediaPlatform.description: MediaPlatform is enterprise video and webcasting software designed for corporate communications.
MediaPlatform.history: MediaPlatform is a North Hollywood, CA enterprise video platform provider for live webcasting and on-demand video management. Named a Leader by Gartner in its Magic Quadrant for Enterprise Video Content Management. Serves large enterprise clients for executive messaging and eLearning video delivery.
MediaPlatform.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. MediaPlatform is an enterprise B2B video hosting platform used for internal webcasting. No consumer-facing abuse surface.

Mediavine.description: Mediavine is a full service ad management platform.
Mediavine.history: Mediavine was founded in 2004 as an internet publisher (The Hollywood Gossip, TV Fanatic, Food Fanatic). It launched as an ad management company for third-party publishers in early 2015 and grew to become the largest full-service exclusive ad management firm in the US. Celebrates its 20th anniversary in 2024, headquartered in Boca Raton, FL.
Mediavine.summary_abuse: Mediavine loads a heavy ad stack on publisher pages including numerous third-party trackers for programmatic advertising. Like other premium ad networks, its scripts can deliver malvertisements if DSP partners serve rogue creatives. Mediavine has strong publisher-side controls and a reputation for quality, but ad networks inherently carry malvertising risk.

MediaWiki.description: MediaWiki is a free and open-source wiki engine.
MediaWiki.history: MediaWiki was developed by Magnus Manske and first deployed on Wikipedia on January 25, 2002, replacing UseModWiki. It was officially named MediaWiki in 2003. Developed and maintained by the Wikimedia Foundation, it powers Wikipedia and tens of thousands of other wikis. It is the world's most widely deployed wiki software and has been developed continuously for over 20 years.
MediaWiki.summary_abuse: MediaWiki itself is foundational infrastructure with no intrinsic abuse. Wikis can host spam, vandalism, or coordinated disinformation campaigns if improperly configured, but the Wikimedia-hosted instances have strong abuse mitigation. Self-hosted MediaWiki installations vary widely in moderation quality. No abuse inherent to the software itself.

Medium.description: Medium is an online publishing platform.
Medium.history: Medium was founded by Twitter co-founder Evan Williams and launched in August 2012 as a long-form publishing platform. Initially invitation-only, it opened to the public in 2013. Williams stepped down as CEO in July 2022, replaced by Tony Stubblebine. The platform introduced a membership subscription ($5/month) and partner program for writer monetization. It has gone through multiple strategy pivots between ad-supported and subscription models.
Medium.summary_abuse: Medium has been used for spam content, phishing-related articles with malicious links, and SEO abuse campaigns. Its free tier and domain authority make it attractive for spammers to post articles with links to malicious or spammy sites. Medium has content moderation but the volume of user-generated content means abusive posts persist for hours before removal. Paywall-bypass link schemes and crypto scam posts are documented.

Medoc.description: Medoc is a retail and ecommerce system.
Medoc.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Medoc.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Medusajs.description: Medusa is an open-source ecommerce platform that enables users to create online stores.
Medusajs.history: Medusajs was founded in 2020 in Denmark. It rapidly gained traction as an open-source Node.js/TypeScript headless commerce engine, reaching #1 on GitHub for open-source commerce. It received venture funding and grew to 22,000+ GitHub stars. In 2023/2024 it released Medusa 2.0 with a redesigned modular framework. It is 100% MIT licensed with no transaction fees.
Medusajs.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Medusajs is a self-hosted open-source commerce framework with no hosted version. Security exposure is limited to the operator's own deployment; no central platform for abuse.

Meebo.description: Meebo is a widget that is integrated into websites to facilitate link sharing.
Meebo.history: Meebo was founded in September 2005 by Sandy Jen, Seth Sternberg, and Elaine Wherry in Mountain View, CA as a web-based instant messaging aggregator supporting Yahoo!, MSN, AIM, and others. It expanded to a 'Meebo Bar' widget for third-party sites. Google acquired Meebo in June 2012 for ~$100 million to bolster Google+ development. All Meebo services were discontinued by July 2013.
Meebo.summary_abuse: Meebo is defunct (shut down 2013). During its operation, the Meebo Bar widget was a common presence on media sites and collected user behavioral data for tracking. No major abuse incidents were documented.

Meeting Scheduler.description: Meeting Scheduler is a schedule appointments widget.
Meeting Scheduler.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Meeting Scheduler.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Meetup Express.description: Meetup Express is an appointment scheduling system designed to simplify booking and managing meetings.
Meetup Express.history: Meetup Express is an embeddable widget provided by Meetup.com (founded 2002, NYC) that allows website owners to display Meetup group events on their own pages via JavaScript embed code. Meetup.com was acquired by WeWork in 2017, then sold to AlumnIQ in 2020 after WeWork's financial collapse. The widget/express embed is a feature of the Meetup platform API ecosystem.
Meetup Express.summary_abuse: No documented abuse history. Meetup Express is a simple embed widget for displaying community event listings. Minimal risk profile as it only displays public event data.

Mega.description: Mega is a platform that applies artificial intelligence to automate and improve search engine optimization processes and paid advertising campaign performance.
Mega.history: Mega (mega.nz) launched January 19, 2013 by Kim Dotcom, one year after the US government seized Megaupload. Built around client-side end-to-end encryption with 50GB free storage. Kim Dotcom resigned as director in September 2013, severed all ties by 2015, alleging NZ govt seized shares of a Chinese investor. A 2022 academic study found cryptographic weaknesses enabling decryption attacks. The service has been used to distribute copyrighted content and CSAM, prompting takedowns.
Mega.summary_abuse: Mega has a significant abuse track record: frequently used for hosting pirated software, copyrighted media, and illegal content due to its encrypted storage and no-knowledge claims. 2022 cryptographic flaws were disclosed. Considered higher-risk for enterprise use due to historical misuse by uploaders.

Megagroup CMS.S3.description: Megagroup CMS.S3 management system is provided not as a “boxed product”, but as a separate service, that is, it works using SaaS technology. This means that you manage your site directly through your browser using an intuitive interface.
Megagroup CMS.S3.history: Megagroup.ru is a Russian web studio founded in 1997. Its proprietary SaaS CMS, CMS.S3, has been developed since 2006. Delivered exclusively as a SaaS (no boxed product), written in PHP with Percona/MySQL. As of 2021, it ranked 4th overall in Russian CMS usage (3.06%) and 1st in the SaaS CMS segment (19.62%). Primarily serves Russian SMB market. Operations may be affected by post-2022 sanctions environment.
Megagroup CMS.S3.summary_abuse: No documented malware or abuse incidents. Risk is elevated due to Russian jurisdiction and potential data sovereignty concerns for non-Russian users, especially post-2022. Not recommended for EU/US businesses with compliance requirements.

Meilisearch.description: Meilisearch is a search engine created by Meili, a software development company based in France.
Meilisearch.history: Meilisearch is an open-source, Rust-based search engine founded in 2018 in Paris by Quentin de Quelen and team. Raised $15M Series A (Felicis/CRV) and $5M seed, ~$40M total. Self-host or use Meilisearch Cloud SaaS. 46,000+ GitHub stars, 20,000+ teams using it. MIT-licensed core with commercial cloud offering. Known for developer-friendly REST API, fast indexing, typo tolerance, and instant search.
Meilisearch.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Meilisearch is a well-regarded open-source project with active development and strong community trust. No security incidents or malicious use cases documented.

Meks Simple Flickr Widget.description: Meks Simple Flickr Widget is a WordPress plugin created by Meks. This plugin is designed to help WordPress website owners easily integrate Flickr photo streams into their websites.
Meks Simple Flickr Widget.history: Meks Simple Flickr Widget is a free WordPress plugin by Meks (mekshq.com), a Serbian WordPress theme and plugin shop. Displays Flickr photos in a WordPress sidebar widget using a user ID or group ID, with no API auth required. 20,000+ active installations, rated 4.3/5 on WordPress.org. Last updated July 2024. Requires only Flickr user/group ID input.
Meks Simple Flickr Widget.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Simple open-source Flickr widget with no tracking or malicious behavior documented. Low risk profile.

Melibo.description: Melibo is an AI platform focused on enhancing customer service and advancing chatbot technology.
Melibo.history: Melibo is a German conversational AI and chatbot platform founded around 2019, specializing in e-commerce customer service automation. Hosted in Germany, GDPR-compliant, with AI Agents integrating into Shopify and other e-commerce systems. Plans start at ~750 EUR/month. The platform evolved from rule-based chatbots into AI Agents handling full ticket workflows by 2025-2026.
Melibo.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Melibo is a legitimate German B2B SaaS with GDPR compliance focus, serving e-commerce businesses. Low risk.

Melis Platform.history: Melis Platform is an open-source, modular digital experience platform and CMS by Melis Technology (France). Community Edition uses Open Software License (OSL3). Built on Zend Framework (PHP), supports Symfony, Laravel, Lumen, Silex in a cross-framework architecture. First platform of its kind to be cross-framework. Available in community (free) and commercial editions. Niche adoption mainly in European markets.
Melis Platform.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Melis Platform is a legitimate open-source CMS with small but genuine user base. Low risk.

Meltwater.description: Meltwater is a media monitoring platform that tracks, analyzes, and provides insights from online news, social media, and broadcast sources to support data-driven decision-making.
Meltwater.history: Meltwater was founded in Oslo, Norway in 2001 by Jørn Lyseggen, bootstrapped with $15,000. Originally Meltwater News, relocated to San Francisco in 2005. The first online media monitoring company to go public, listing on Euronext Growth Oslo (MWTR) in December 2020. Acquired by Marlin Equity Partners in January 2023. ~2,200 employees, 27,000+ customers, 50 offices across 6 continents. Market leader in media intelligence and social listening.
Meltwater.summary_abuse: No significant abuse history. Meltwater is a well-established enterprise media monitoring company. Some critics note aggressive sales tactics and limited pricing transparency, but no security incidents or data breaches documented.

Membership Toolkit.description: Membership Toolkit is a volunteer management platform that organizes scheduling, tracks participation, and streamlines administrative tasks for groups that coordinate community, school, or event-based service activities.
Membership Toolkit.history: Membership Toolkit is a US-based SaaS platform designed for PTAs, PTOs, booster clubs, HOAs, and small nonprofits. Provides membership management, online payments, fundraising, volunteer tracking, and communication tools. Plans: Essential ($550/yr), Premium ($850/yr), Concierge ($1,150/yr). Focused entirely on volunteer-led nonprofit organizations needing simple administration tools.
Membership Toolkit.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Membership Toolkit is a legitimate niche SaaS for nonprofit organizations. Low risk profile.

MembershipWorks.description: MembershipWorks is a membership software platform that provides tools for managing member records, offering a searchable member directory, and supporting event calendars with integrated registration features.
MembershipWorks.history: MembershipWorks is a US-based membership management SaaS targeting associations, nonprofits, and small organizations. Free tier for up to 50 members; paid plans from $29/month (up to 300 members). Integrates with WordPress, Squarespace, Weebly, and other website platforms. Features: member directory, event calendar, online payments, donation management, QuickBooks/Xero integration. No transaction fees on payments.
MembershipWorks.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. MembershipWorks is a legitimate niche membership management SaaS with transparent pricing and no hidden fees. Low risk.

MemberSpace.description: MemberSpace is a cloud-based membership solution, which allows users to provide role-based access to content.
MemberSpace.history: MemberSpace is a membership platform that adds gated content and paid memberships to existing websites (Squarespace, Webflow, Wix, HTML). Founded circa 2016 in the US. Pricing: $39/month plus 5% transaction fee on the Professional plan. Works without rebuilding a site by adding member-only pages via a JavaScript snippet. Targeted at individual creators, small businesses, and content publishers.
MemberSpace.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. MemberSpace is a straightforward membership SaaS. The 5% transaction fee model is clearly disclosed. Low risk profile.

MemberStack.description: MemberStack is a no-code membership platform for Webflow.
MemberStack.history: Memberstack (memberstack.com) was founded in 2018 by Tyler Bell and Duncan Hamra in Williamsburg, VA. Y Combinator-backed. Provides membership, auth, gated content, and payments built natively for Webflow and WordPress. 50,000+ founders use it; 3,000 companies generating $130M+ ARR through the platform. Expanded to broader no-code ecosystem. 8-person team.
MemberStack.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Memberstack is a well-regarded no-code membership tool popular in the Webflow community. Low risk profile.

Mendix.description: Mendix is a low-code platform utilized by businesses for development of mobile and web applications at scale.
Mendix.history: Mendix is a low-code application development platform founded in Rotterdam, Netherlands in 2005 by Derek Roos, Derckjan Kruit, and Roald Kuit. In 2018, Siemens acquired Mendix for approximately $700M, making it a wholly-owned Siemens subsidiary focused on industrial and enterprise digital transformation. Mendix is consistently a Gartner Magic Quadrant leader for low-code platforms. Used by 4,000+ enterprise customers globally.
Mendix.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Mendix is a market-leading enterprise low-code platform owned by Siemens. Strong compliance credentials (SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA). Very low risk.

Mentad.description: Mentad is a predictive marketing platform to acquire new customers.
Mentad.history: MentAd was a predictive marketing platform founded in 2011 in Tel Aviv, Israel. Used machine learning to analyze first and third-party conversion data to identify lookalike customer acquisition opportunities across advertising channels. Acquired by SocialCode (a social media marketing company) in January 2017. Following acquisition, the MentAd brand and standalone platform appear to have been absorbed into SocialCode. Limited active presence post-2017.
Mentad.summary_abuse: No specific abuse incidents documented. MentAd was an ad-tech firm focused on audience targeting; typical privacy concerns around third-party data use apply. Dormant since 2017 acquisition.

Mentaya.description: Mentaya is a platform that simplifies mental health care by leveraging out-of-network benefits to reduce patient costs while ensuring therapists receive full fees.
Mentaya.history: Mentaya is a US-based healthcare SaaS that helps therapists and their clients navigate out-of-network insurance reimbursements. The platform verifies OON benefits, submits superbills to insurance, and advocates for clients. Free for therapists; clients pay a 5% fee per claim. Positions itself as a HIPAA-compliant alternative to manual insurance billing. Focused on mental health / therapy practice management.
Mentaya.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Mentaya handles sensitive health insurance data and is positioned as HIPAA-compliant. Low risk, niche mental health billing platform.

Mention Me.description: Mention Me is a referral marketing platform for conversion-obsessed ecommerce brands.
Mention Me.history: Mention Me was founded in 2013 in London, UK by Andy Cockburn and Tim Boughton. Provides an AI-powered referral marketing platform for brands. Raised $32M from Octopus Ventures and Eight Roads. Has delivered 6+ million referrals generating $2B+ in revenue for 500+ global brands including ASOS, PUMA, Charlotte Tilbury. Holds 16.56% market share in the advocate-marketing segment as of 2026.
Mention Me.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Mention Me is a UK-based referral marketing platform with GDPR compliance and solid enterprise customer base. Sets tracking cookies for referral attribution. Low risk.

Menufy Online Ordering.description: Menufy is an online and mobile meal ordering service.
Menufy Online Ordering.history: Menufy was founded in 2009 in Leawood, Kansas (Kansas City area) as an online ordering platform for independent restaurants. Grew to serve 12,000+ independently owned restaurants across 3,000 US cities. Acquired by HungerRush (restaurant POS and cloud software company) in October 2021. Post-acquisition, Menufy was integrated into HungerRush 360, expanding it with cloud POS, AI-driven text ordering, and marketing tools.
Menufy Online Ordering.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Menufy is a legitimate restaurant online ordering platform, now part of HungerRush. Handles PCI-sensitive payment data through standard payment processors. Low risk.

Menufy Website.description: Menufy is an online and mobile meal ordering service.
Menufy Website.history: Menufy Website is the branded restaurant website product offered by Menufy (part of HungerRush since October 2021). Provides commission-free direct ordering restaurant websites for independent restaurants. Originally founded in Kansas City in 2009, serving 12,000+ restaurants. The website builder component creates mobile-friendly, SEO-optimized restaurant websites with integrated online ordering, integrated with the broader HungerRush 360 POS ecosystem.
Menufy Website.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Menufy Website is the restaurant website product under Menufy/HungerRush. Low risk, focused on independent restaurant market.

Menuu.description: MENUU is an online ordering system for restaurants and food delivery.
Menuu.history: Menuu (menuu.global) is a restaurant digital ordering and delivery platform offering consolidated online ordering, delivery, and kiosk solutions for restaurant operators. Provides white-label ordering infrastructure and centralized order management. Competes with MENU Technologies, BentoBox, and other restaurant tech platforms. Limited public information on founding year or funding.
Menuu.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Menuu is a niche restaurant ordering platform. Limited public documentation. Low risk.

Mercado Shops.description: Mercado Shops is an all-in-one ecommerce platform.
Mercado Shops.history: Mercado Shops is an e-commerce website builder launched by MercadoLibre in 2012, enabling small businesses to create branded storefronts integrated with Mercado Libre's marketplace. MercadoLibre (MELI, NASDAQ) was founded in Buenos Aires in 1999 by Marcos Galperín. Now Latin America's largest e-commerce and fintech platform, present in 18 countries. Mercado Shops had ~43,000 active stores as of 2024.
Mercado Shops.summary_abuse: Mercado Shops / MercadoLibre has had documented fraud and counterfeit goods issues typical of large marketplaces. The platform invests heavily in buyer/seller protection. Abuse risk is moderate as with all large Latin American e-commerce platforms.

Mercer.description: Mercer is a platform that supports companies in transforming talent, health, retirement, and investment strategies to enhance overall performance and outcomes.
Mercer.history: Mercer is a global management consulting firm founded in 1945, headquartered in New York. A wholly-owned subsidiary of Marsh McLennan (NYSE: MMC). Specializes in HR consulting, retirement/benefits, health, and investment consulting. Detected as a website technology typically through Mercer's embedded HR portals or analytics tracking scripts used on employee benefit websites. One of the world's largest HR consulting firms.
Mercer.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents related to Mercer technology. As a global consulting firm subsidiary of Marsh McLennan, Mercer maintains enterprise-grade security standards. Low risk.

Merchello.description: Merchello is an open source ecommerce package for the Umbraco CMS.
Merchello.history: Merchello is an open-source e-commerce package for Umbraco CMS v7, licensed under MIT. Developed by the Umbraco community starting around 2013-2014. Provided shopping cart, product management, payment gateways (PayPal, Braintree), and order management for Umbraco websites. No longer actively developed; the project recommends Vendr for Umbraco v8+. Last significant activity on GitHub around 2018-2020.
Merchello.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Abandoned open-source project. Sites still using Merchello may have unpatched security vulnerabilities due to lack of maintenance. Migration to actively maintained alternatives is recommended.

Mercurycloud.description: Mercurycloud is a hosting service provider offering a range of solutions, including web hosting, virtual private servers (VPS), dedicated servers, and game servers.
Mercurycloud.history: Mercurycloud (mercurycloud.fr) is a French hosting provider offering web hosting, VPS, dedicated servers, and game servers. A small regional hosting company serving primarily French-speaking markets. Detected when sites are hosted on Mercurycloud infrastructure. Limited public information on company history or founding date.
Mercurycloud.summary_abuse: No documented abuse incidents. Niche French hosting provider. As with any shared hosting, abuse potential exists but no specific documented cases.

Merge.description: Merge is a data infrastructure platform for AI products that enables connection to multiple customer data sources through a single API.
Merge.history: Merge was founded in 2020 by Shensi Ding and Gil Feig in San Francisco and NYC. Provides a unified API platform enabling B2B SaaS products to add hundreds of HR, payroll, recruiting, and accounting integrations via a single API. Raised $74.5M total including a $55M Series B led by Accel (October 2022), with NEA and Addition. 1,000+ companies use Merge. Offices in SF, NYC, and Berlin.
Merge.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Merge handles sensitive HR, payroll, and accounting data via its unified API and maintains SOC 2 Type II compliance. Low risk for its enterprise developer customer base.

Merit.description: Merit is a platform tailored for higher education marketing.
Merit.history: Merit (meritpages.com) is a higher education marketing automation platform that helps colleges and universities publish and share student achievement stories for marketing and alumni engagement. Students receive shareable achievement pages when recognized by their institution. Founded in the US circa 2012. Pricing is enterprise-only/custom, targeted at higher education institutions.
Merit.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Merit publishes student achievement data which raises FERPA considerations but this is managed by the subscribing institutions. Low risk.

Merlin.description: Merlin is a platform that builds bots for interactive visitor, lead, and customer engagement, integrating with tools and databases to automate processes and improve lead conversion without requiring a developer.
Merlin.history: Merlin (gomerlin.com.br) is a Brazilian live chat and bot platform for interactive visitor, lead, and customer engagement. Builds conversational bots that integrate with tools and databases to automate processes and improve lead conversion without requiring developer skills. Primarily serves the Brazilian market. Limited public information on founding history or funding.
Merlin.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Merlin is a niche Brazilian chat/bot platform. Limited public footprint. Low risk.

Mermaid.history: Mermaid.js is an open-source JavaScript diagramming library created by Knut Sveidqvist in 2014. Generates flowcharts, sequence diagrams, Gantt charts, class diagrams, and more from Markdown-like text syntax. MIT-licensed. Won the 2019 JS Open Source Award. Natively integrated into GitHub, GitLab, Notion, and many documentation platforms. 70,000+ GitHub stars. Mermaid Chart offers a commercial collaboration platform. Active development as of May 2026.
Mermaid.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Mermaid.js is a widely trusted open-source diagramming library integrated into GitHub, GitLab, and countless documentation tools. No security incidents documented.

Mesmerize Companion.description: The Mesmerize Companion is a WordPress plugin that enhances the Mesmerize theme by adding drag-and-drop page builder functionality.
Mesmerize Companion.history: Mesmerize Companion is a free WordPress plugin developed by Horea Radu / Extend Themes, first released in 2017. It serves as a companion plugin to the Mesmerize WordPress theme, providing drag-and-drop page builder functionality with 30+ pre-built content sections. GPL3-licensed. ~60,000 active installations. Maintained and updated as of late 2025.
Mesmerize Companion.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Standard GPL-licensed WordPress companion plugin. Low risk profile.

MessageGears.description: MessageGears is a cross-channel messaging platform designed to help businesses deliver communications across multiple channels from a single system.
MessageGears.history: MessageGears was founded in 2010 in Atlanta, GA by Dan Roy, Michael Robinson, Taylor Jones, and Greg Smith. A warehouse-native email and cross-channel engagement platform that lets enterprises send messages directly from their data warehouse (Snowflake, BigQuery, Redshift) without data replication. Raised $79.6M total across 5 rounds including a $62M Series D in 2022. Customers include Expedia, Chewy, OpenTable, Vimeo. Won 'Best B2C Email Marketing Solution' 2025.
MessageGears.summary_abuse: No abuse or spam incidents documented. MessageGears is a legitimate enterprise email platform that connects to customer data warehouses. SOC 2 compliant. Low risk.

MessageMedia.description: MessageMedia is a software service provider that helps businesses communicate with their customers and other contacts using SMS messages.
MessageMedia.history: MessageMedia is an Australian SMS/mobile messaging company founded circa 2000. Acquired by Sinch (Swedish cloud communications company) in 2021 for USD $1.3 billion, making it a wholly-owned Sinch subsidiary. Serves 60,000+ customers handling 5+ billion messages annually. Focused on SMB market. Provides two-way SMS, MMS, voice, and mobile messaging APIs without requiring coding expertise.
MessageMedia.summary_abuse: As an SMS platform, MessageMedia has standard telecom abuse vectors (spam, phishing SMS). Sinch/MessageMedia maintains anti-spam controls and TCPA compliance. Moderate abuse score due to SMS channel risk inherent to all messaging providers.

MetaMap.description: MetaMap is an identity verification platform that provides customizable solutions for gaining customer insights and making informed decisions.
MetaMap.history: MetaMap (formerly Mati) was founded in 2017 by Amaury Soviche and Filip Victor. Provides identity verification, KYC, AML compliance, and onboarding solutions primarily for LatAm, Africa, and Asia. Raised $97.6M including a $70M Series B (December 2021, led by Tribe Capital with Craft Ventures and Alameda Research). Acquired by Incode in June 2024, ending its independent operation. 650+ enterprise customers including Binance and Allianz.
MetaMap.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents documented. MetaMap handles sensitive biometric identity data for KYC processes. The Alameda Research investment (associated with FTX collapse) raised investor concerns but MetaMap itself was not implicated in fraud. Now part of Incode.

MetaSlider.description: MetaSlider is a WordPress plugin for adding responsive sliders and carousels to websites.
MetaSlider.history: MetaSlider is the world's most popular responsive WordPress slider plugin, with 800,000+ active installations. Originally launched around 2012. Now managed by PublishPress (Steve Burge). Provides image, video, layer, and carousel slides with a page-builder-friendly interface. Free version available on WordPress.org; Pro version with additional features. Actively maintained with updates into 2026.
MetaSlider.summary_abuse: No abuse history. MetaSlider is one of the most widely used WordPress slider plugins with a clean security record. Low risk.

Meteor.description: Meteor is a JavaScript-based platform used for developing web and mobile applications with a unified language across all layers of the application stack.
Meteor.history: Meteor (Meteor.js) is a full-stack JavaScript framework created in 2012 by Geoff Schmidt and Matt DeBergalis at Meteor Development Group. Built on Node.js with integrated MongoDB and DDP (Distributed Data Protocol) for real-time data sync. Raised $11.2M from a16z. Acquired by Tiny Capital in October 2019 and renamed Meteor Software. Actively maintained with v3.x as of 2026, adding Rspack bundler support (4x faster builds). MIT-licensed with Galaxy managed hosting. 40,000+ Slack members.
Meteor.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Meteor is a mature open-source full-stack framework with an active community under Tiny Capital. Low risk.

Methode.history: Méthode is the flagship editorial and publishing CMS by Eidosmedia, a Milan-based company founded in 1999. Used by major news organizations worldwide for integrated print and digital publishing including Le Monde, Figaro, The Times, Financial Times, Frankfurter Allgemeine, The Boston Globe, and The Washington Post. The most evolved editorial publishing platform for large news media operations. Offices in Italy, Germany, US, France, UK, China, and Australia.
Methode.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Méthode is a legitimate enterprise CMS exclusively used by large news organizations. Very low risk profile.

Meticular.description: Meticular is a Swiss platform that provides business solutions and services to support organizational operations.
Meticular.history: Meticular (metikular.ch) is a Swiss SaaS platform providing business solutions and organizational operations support. Appears to be a small Swiss business software company. Limited public information available on founding year, funding, or specific product details. Classified as miscellaneous business tools by Wappalyzer.
Meticular.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Swiss niche business platform. Low risk.

metisMenu.description: metisMenu is a collapsible jQuery menu plugin.
metisMenu.history: metisMenu is a small jQuery plugin for creating collapsible sidebar menus commonly used in admin dashboard templates. No notable ownership or deprecation events.
metisMenu.summary_abuse: metisMenu has no notable abuse signal; it is a small client-side menu plugin with no server-side component.

Metomic.description: Metomic is a platform that helps businesses manage and comply with data privacy regulations, offering tools for consent management, data governance, and compliance with data privacy laws such as GDPR and CCPA.
Metomic.history: Metomic is a London-based data security SaaS founded around 2018. Provides agentless data loss prevention (DLP) for SaaS applications and AI tools, protecting sensitive data across Google Workspace, Slack, and other cloud platforms. Supports GDPR, HIPAA, and PCI compliance. CEO Bethany Ayers leads the company. VC-backed with partnerships including HANDD. Focused on helping enterprises discover, classify, and control sensitive data.
Metomic.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Metomic is a data security company with strong compliance credentials (SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, PCI). Low risk.

Metorik.description: Metorik is a WooCommerce and Shopify store automation system providing custom reports and email marketing tools to manage and analyze ecommerce operations.
Metorik.history: Metorik is an e-commerce analytics and email automation platform for WooCommerce and Shopify stores. Founded circa 2016, likely in Australia. Serves 8,000+ stores analyzing billions in sales. Pricing from $25/month based on order volume (not users). All features included in every plan. Provides real-time reports, profit tracking with COGS, segmentation, and email automation. Independent bootstrapped company.
Metorik.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Metorik is a well-regarded WooCommerce analytics tool used by thousands of stores. Low risk.

Metrics Key.description: Metrics Key is software used for tracking performance in software development and search marketing campaigns.
Metrics Key.history: Metrics Key (metricskey.net) is a SaaS analytics platform for tracking performance in software development and search marketing campaigns. Limited public information on company history, funding, or founding date. Small US-based analytics tool targeting developers and digital marketers.
Metrics Key.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Niche analytics SaaS with limited public footprint. Low risk.

MetricsCube.description: MetricsCube is a WHMCS-integrated business analysis platform, offering real-time stats and reports.
MetricsCube.history: MetricsCube was founded around 2017 by Konrad Keck in Rzeszów, Poland. Keck brought nearly a decade of WHMCS billing integration expertise, having previously co-founded ModulesGarden, a provider of WHMCS modules. MetricsCube was built to fill the business analytics gap for web hosting companies using WHMCS, providing real-time dashboards and flexible reports for hosting metrics, revenue, and customer data.
MetricsCube.summary_abuse: MetricsCube is a business analytics platform exclusively integrated with WHMCS, the billing system used by web hosting providers. It has no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse history. Its use is limited to hosting industry operators reviewing internal business metrics.

Metrilo.description: Metrilo is an ecommerce analytics, email marketing software provider.
Metrilo.history: Metrilo is a Bulgarian e-commerce analytics and email marketing platform founded around 2014 in Sofia, Bulgaria. Provides customer retention analytics, revenue attribution, and targeted email automation for WooCommerce and other e-commerce platforms. GDPR-compliant. Serves thousands of online stores. Bootstrapped independent company.
Metrilo.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Metrilo is a legitimate e-commerce analytics SaaS based in Bulgaria. Low risk.

Metro CreativeX.description: Metro CreativeX is a free WordPress theme with a fully responsive design.
Metro CreativeX.history: Metro CreativeX (metrox) is a free, fully responsive WordPress theme developed by ThemeIsle (a Romanian WordPress theme and plugin company). Inspired by Microsoft's Metro/Fluent design aesthetics. GPL-licensed, available at themeisle.com. ThemeIsle was founded in Romania and is known for popular themes like Neve and Hestia.
Metro CreativeX.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Standard GPL-licensed free WordPress theme from ThemeIsle. Low risk.

Metronet Profile Picture.description: Set a custom profile image for a user using the standard WordPress media upload tool.
Metronet Profile Picture.history: Metronet Profile Picture is a free GPL-licensed WordPress plugin by Metronet (Ronald Huereca). Allows setting a custom profile image for WordPress users via the standard WordPress media upload tool, replacing the default Gravatar. Simple utility plugin with moderate install base. Available on WordPress.org.
Metronet Profile Picture.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Simple utility WordPress plugin for custom profile pictures. Low risk.

MetroUI.description: MetroUI is an open-source frontend toolkit inspired by Microsoft Fluent (former Metro) design principles.
MetroUI.history: MetroUI (Metro-UI-CSS) is an open-source frontend CSS/JS toolkit created by Sergei Parilov (olton) on GitHub, inspired by Microsoft's Metro/Fluent design language. MIT-licensed. Provides a comprehensive set of UI components, icons, and layout tools. First released around 2012. Still maintained with ongoing commits as of 2026. Popular for building Windows-style web UIs without Microsoft's official tooling.
MetroUI.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Open-source frontend UI framework with MIT license. Low risk.

Mews.description: Mews is a hospitalitions service that enables hotels to track their bookings.
Mews.history: Mews is a cloud-based hotel property management system (PMS) founded in Prague/Amsterdam in 2012 by Richard Valtr and Matthijs Welle. Raised significant VC funding and serves 15,000+ hotels, hostels, and apartments across 85 countries. Headquarters in Amsterdam/Prague. Known for open API ecosystem and modern UX. Competes with Oracle Hospitality, Cloudbeds, and Apaleo. SOC2, ISO27001, PCI DSS compliant.
Mews.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Mews is a leading cloud hospitality PMS with strong compliance credentials. Low risk.

Mexty.description: Mexty is a no-code, AI-powered authoring platform that enables educators to create interactive lessons, activities, and courses quickly.
Mexty.history: Mexty is an AI-powered no-code authoring platform for educators to create interactive lessons, activities, and courses. Founded circa 2021. Freemium pricing with low recurring plans and enterprise options. Primarily serves the education/e-learning market. Limited public information on company history and funding.
Mexty.summary_abuse: No abuse or security incidents documented. Niche AI-powered LMS platform. Low risk.

Mezereon.description: Mezereon is an AI-powered platform that provides search, filters, insights, analytics, personalization, autocomplete, and recommendations to help businesses optimize their user experiences.
Mezereon.history: Mezereon is an AI-powered personalization and search platform for e-commerce, providing site search, filters, analytics, autocomplete, and product recommendations. Appears to be a Scandinavian startup. Freemium/mid pricing. Collects behavioral data for personalization. Limited public company information.
Mezereon.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents documented. Mezereon collects behavioral tracking data for personalization, standard for this category. Low risk.

MGID.description: MGID is a programmatic advertising platform frequently used by misinformation websites.
MGID.history: MGID is a global native advertising network founded in 2008. Grew from 6 employees to a large multinational serving 185B+ impressions/month across 200+ countries. Headquartered in Santa Monica, CA. Known for content recommendation widgets placed on publisher sites.
MGID.summary_abuse: MGID is a native ad network associated with low-quality and misleading advertisements. Has been flagged by ad-blocker lists and security researchers for serving malvertising in the past. High tracker density and dark pattern risk from clickbait-style native ads.

Mginex.description: Mginex is an online store website creation platform.
Mginex.history: Mginex (mginex.com) is an online store website creation platform. Limited public information available. Appears to be a small Eastern European e-commerce SaaS builder. No documentation of company history, funding, or compliance certifications found.
Mginex.summary_abuse: No specific abuse incidents documented. Niche e-commerce builder with limited public footprint. Somewhat elevated risk due to lack of compliance transparency.

MGPanel.description: MGPanel has it all, a content management system that gives programmers the freedom to create professional web pages from scratch, also providing their clients with a self-managing platform.
MGPanel.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
MGPanel.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Miappi.description: Miappi is a user-generated content platform designed to build trust and boost sales for brands.
Miappi.history: Miappi is a UK-based user-generated content (UGC) platform founded circa 2013. Helps brands collect, curate, and display social media content to build trust and boost sales. Aggregates UGC from Instagram, Twitter, and other social platforms. GDPR-compliant. Low/recurring pricing. Serves e-commerce brands.
Miappi.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents documented. Miappi aggregates social media content for brands. Standard UGC platform risks around content moderation. Low risk.

MICE Operations.description: MICE Operations is a software solution tailored for event venues, catering, and hospitality businesses, streamlining operational processes, sales, and client relationship management.
MICE Operations.history: MICE Operations is a Dutch (Netherlands) event management and venue booking SaaS platform with over 10 years of operation. MICE stands for Meetings, Incentives, Conferences, and Exhibitions. Used by 1,100+ event venues; one of the largest players in the Netherlands, active in 8 countries.
MICE Operations.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Legitimate Dutch event management SaaS with proven track record in the events industry.

MichiJS.description: MichiJS is a library enabling the creation of web components using TSX.
MichiJS.history: MichiJS is an open-source JavaScript/TypeScript library enabling creation of web components using TSX syntax. A small independent project with limited community adoption. Available on GitHub and npm. Free/open-source. Very niche, low GitHub star count. No commercial backing.
MichiJS.summary_abuse: No abuse history. Small open-source web components library with minimal footprint. Slightly elevated AI-generated likelihood given its obscurity. Low actual risk.

Mico.description: Mico is a customer relationship management platform that supports personalized, continuous communication between businesses and customers and includes tools to improve brand visibility across search engines and AI-driven platforms.
Mico.history: Mico (mico-inc.com) is a CRM platform supporting personalized, continuous communication between businesses and customers, with tools for brand visibility across search engines and AI-driven platforms. Limited public company information. US-based startup. POA pricing model indicates enterprise/custom sales.
Mico.summary_abuse: No abuse incidents documented. Niche CRM with limited public footprint. Low risk.

microCMS.description: microCMS is a Japan-based headless CMS that enables editors and developers to build delicate sites and apps.
microCMS.history: microCMS is a Japan-based API-first headless CMS founded in 2019, popular primarily in the Japanese developer community. In May 2024, it was acquired by Ateam for approximately 1.5 billion yen.
microCMS.summary_abuse: microCMS is a headless CMS with no notable abuse signal. It is a developer-focused API tool primarily used for content management in Japan, with no characteristics that make it a preferred tool for fraudsters.

Microsoft 365.description: Microsoft 365 is a line of subscription services offered by Microsoft as part of the Microsoft Office product line.
Microsoft 365.history: Microsoft 365 (formerly Office 365, rebranded 2020) is Microsoft's cloud productivity suite launched in 2011. Includes Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Teams, Exchange, SharePoint, and more. Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) was founded in 1975 by Bill Gates and Paul Allen. As of 2026, Microsoft 365 has 400M+ paid seats and is the dominant enterprise productivity platform globally. Detected on websites via Teams widgets, SharePoint, embedded Office documents, or Microsoft login buttons.
Microsoft 365.summary_abuse: Microsoft 365 is a top target for phishing campaigns impersonating Microsoft login pages. Microsoft invests heavily in anti-phishing and security. Platform itself is legitimate. Moderate abuse score reflects its use as a phishing lure target, not inherent platform abuse.

Microsoft Advertising.description: Microsoft Advertising is an online advertising platform developed by Microsoft.
Microsoft Advertising.history: Microsoft Advertising (formerly Bing Ads, rebranded 2019) is Microsoft's pay-per-click digital advertising platform, launched in 2006 as Microsoft adCenter. Reaches 500M+ monthly unique users across Bing, Yahoo, DuckDuckGo, and MSN. Second largest search advertising platform globally after Google Ads. Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) acquired aQuantive for $6.3B in 2007 as part of its ad strategy. Detected via the Universal Event Tracking (UET) pixel on advertiser websites.
Microsoft Advertising.summary_abuse: Microsoft Advertising UET tag collects extensive behavioral tracking data across the web. Platform is legitimate but is a heavy tracker. Some malvertising incidents documented industry-wide but Microsoft invests in ad quality controls. Elevated tracker density score reflects extensive cross-site tracking.

Microsoft Ajax Content Delivery Network.description: Microsoft Ajax Content Delivery Network hosts popular third party JavaScript libraries such as jQuery and enables you to easily add them to your web applications.
Microsoft Ajax Content Delivery Network.history: Microsoft Ajax CDN launched around 2009 as part of Microsoft's ASP.NET Ajax framework ecosystem, offering free hosting of popular JavaScript libraries like jQuery, jQuery UI, and MooTools. It was designed to reduce load times for ASP.NET web applications. Still operational as of 2024 but overshadowed by more modern CDNs.
Microsoft Ajax Content Delivery Network.summary_abuse: The Microsoft Ajax CDN is a static file delivery service for open-source JavaScript libraries. It does not host user-generated content, does not support custom domains for end users, and has no signup or KYC process.
