Abuse potential is very low; the service has no documented history of being used for phishing or malware distribution at the CDN layer. Microsoft operates robust AUP enforcement.

Microsoft Application Insights.description: Microsoft Application Insights is a feature of Azure Monitor that provides extensible application performance management (APM) and monitoring for live web apps.
Microsoft Application Insights.history: Microsoft Application Insights launched in 2013 as a cloud-based APM service integrated into Azure. It became part of Azure Monitor in 2018, consolidating observability offerings. It monitors live web applications for performance issues and anomalies.
Microsoft Application Insights.summary_abuse: Application Insights collects telemetry including user sessions and custom events. Microsoft processes this data with GDPR-compliant handling. The main privacy concern is inadvertent PII capture through telemetry, which documentation addresses with data masking guidance.

Microsoft ASP.NET.description: ASP.NET is an open-source, server-side web-application framework designed for web development to produce dynamic web pages.
Microsoft ASP.NET.history: Microsoft ASP.NET is Microsoft's open-source web application framework, first released in 2002 as part of .NET Framework. In 2016, Microsoft released ASP.NET Core as a fully open-source, cross-platform rewrite. Now MIT-licensed on GitHub. Used by millions of websites globally. Powers enterprise applications across all industries. Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT), founded 1975, is one of the world's largest companies.
Microsoft ASP.NET.summary_abuse: No inherent abuse. ASP.NET is the framework powering countless legitimate enterprise and government websites. Some ASP.NET-based legacy sites may have ViewState vulnerabilities, but the framework itself is secure and actively maintained.

Microsoft Authentication.description: The Microsoft Authentication Library for JavaScript enables both client-side and server-side JavaScript applications to authenticate users using Azure AD for work and school accounts (AAD), Microsoft personal accounts (MSA), and social identity providers like Facebook, Google, LinkedIn, Microsoft accounts, etc. through Azure AD B2C service.
Microsoft Authentication.history: Microsoft Authentication Library (MSAL) and associated Azure AD/Entra ID authentication services evolved from older ADAL libraries. Azure Active Directory authentication became the standard enterprise SSO/OAuth2/OIDC provider for Microsoft 365 and Azure. Rebranded to Microsoft Entra ID in 2023.
Microsoft Authentication.summary_abuse: Microsoft Authentication underpins Azure AD/Entra ID identity for millions of organizations. The service itself is a target for phishing and credential-stuffing attacks targeting Microsoft accounts, but this reflects the platform's popularity rather than inherent weaknesses. Microsoft deploys advanced threat protection, anomaly detection, and supports MFA and conditional access policies. Credential phishing lures mimicking Microsoft login pages are common, but are attacks against Microsoft rather than by it.

Microsoft Azure Maps.description: Microsoft Azure Maps is a cloud-based mapping and geospatial platform provided by Microsoft.
Microsoft Azure Maps.history: Microsoft Azure Maps launched in 2018 as part of the Azure cloud platform, built on mapping technology acquired from Nokia/HERE and Bing Maps. It provides REST APIs for mapping, search, routing, and geofencing. Competes with Google Maps Platform and Mapbox.
Microsoft Azure Maps.summary_abuse: Azure Maps is a backend mapping API with no user-hosted content surface. Abuse risk is minimal; it requires Azure subscription and API key authentication, preventing anonymous access. No documented abuse patterns specific to this service.

Microsoft Clarity.description: Microsoft's Clarity is a analytics tool which provides website usage statistics, session recording, and heatmaps.
Microsoft Clarity.history: Microsoft Clarity launched publicly in 2020 as a free user behaviour analytics tool with session recordings, heatmaps, and click maps. It grew rapidly due to its zero-cost model and Microsoft backing, becoming a popular alternative to Hotjar and FullStory. Privacy advocates have raised concerns about session recording capturing sensitive data.
Microsoft Clarity.summary_abuse: Clarity records full user sessions including mouse movements and keystrokes, creating privacy risks if implemented on pages with sensitive input fields. Microsoft filters some sensitive inputs by default but misconfiguration can expose passwords or PII. The free pricing model with no site count limits makes it trivially deployable. GDPR compliance requires explicit consent banners. No major documented abuse incidents, but security researchers have noted that session-recording tools like Clarity can inadvertently capture payment card data.

Microsoft Dynamics 365 Commerce.description: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Commerce, an omnichannel ecommerce solution that allows you to build a website, connect physical and digital stores, track customer behaviours and requirements, deliver personalised experiences.
Microsoft Dynamics 365 Commerce.history: Microsoft Dynamics 365 Commerce (formerly Retail) was launched in 2019 as part of the Dynamics 365 platform. It evolved from Microsoft Dynamics AX retail functionality and represents Microsoft's unified omnichannel commerce solution for large retailers.
Microsoft Dynamics 365 Commerce.summary_abuse: Dynamics 365 Commerce is an enterprise ecommerce platform requiring signed contracts and KYC. It is not used by individual bad actors. Abuse risk is negligible due to enterprise-only sales processes.

Microsoft Excel.description: Microsoft Excel is an electronic spreadsheet program used for storing, organizing, and manipulating data.
Microsoft Excel.history: Microsoft Excel was first released in 1985 for Macintosh and in 1987 for Windows, quickly surpassing Lotus 1-2-3 to become the dominant spreadsheet application. It has been the world's most widely used spreadsheet software for decades, continuously updated as part of Microsoft Office and later Microsoft 365.
html: (?:<html [^>]*xmlns:w="urn:schemas-microsoft-com:office:excel"|<!--\s*(?:START|END) OF OUTPUT FROM EXCEL PUBLISH AS WEB PAGE WIZARD\s*-->|<div [^>]*x:publishsource="?Excel"?)
Microsoft Excel.summary_abuse: Excel is occasionally used to distribute malware via malicious macro-laden spreadsheets (macro viruses, .xlsm files with embedded VBA). Microsoft has taken steps to mitigate this by blocking macros from internet-sourced files by default since 2022. Excel itself is legitimate software; abuse involves weaponized documents rather than the platform being inherently abusive.

Microsoft HTTPAPI.description: Microsoft HTTPAPI is a kernel-mode HTTP driver in the Windows operating system responsible for handling HTTP requests and responses with efficiency, scalability, and security.
Microsoft HTTPAPI.history: Microsoft HTTP API (HTTP.sys) is a kernel-mode HTTP server component introduced in Windows Server 2003. It forms the foundation for IIS, WCF, and other Windows HTTP services. It is bundled with Windows and has been updated with successive Windows Server releases.
Microsoft HTTPAPI.summary_abuse: Microsoft HTTPAPI is a kernel-mode Windows component used by IIS and WCF services. Its server banner appears in HTTP responses identifying the underlying Windows HTTP stack. No inherent abuse surface; it is background infrastructure. Occasionally exploited through vulnerabilities in the Windows HTTP stack, but these are patched by Microsoft through regular updates.

Microsoft Power BI.description: Microsoft Power BI is a data analytics platform that transforms company data into rich visuals for insights and decision-making.
Microsoft Power BI.history: Microsoft Power BI launched in 2014 after evolving from Power Query, Power Pivot, and Power View Excel add-ins. It became generally available in 2015 and rapidly grew to become one of the leading BI platforms globally, consistently placed as a Leader in Gartner's Analytics and BI Magic Quadrant.
Microsoft Power BI.summary_abuse: Power BI is a business intelligence tool. Its embedded reports can potentially expose sensitive organizational data if sharing settings are misconfigured (public publish-to-web). Microsoft has documented this risk and provides controls to prevent accidental public exposure. No notable external abuse patterns beyond misconfiguration risks.

Microsoft PowerPoint.description: Microsoft PowerPoint is a tool to create presentations using simple drag and drop tools.
Microsoft PowerPoint.history: PowerPoint was created by Forethought Inc and first released in 1987 for Macintosh. Microsoft acquired Forethought for $14 million in 1987, making it one of Microsoft's earliest acquisitions. It became the dominant presentation software globally, part of Microsoft Office since 1990.
Microsoft PowerPoint.summary_abuse: PowerPoint files are occasionally weaponized to deliver malware via malicious macros, OLE objects, or exploits in the file parser. Microsoft patches these vulnerabilities through regular security updates and has implemented default macro blocking. Phishing decoys and social engineering documents are commonly formatted as .pptx files, but this reflects attacker use of a ubiquitous format rather than any inherent platform weakness.

Microsoft Publisher.description: Microsoft Publisher is an application that allows you to create professional documents such as newsletters, postcards, flyers, invitations, brochures.
Microsoft Publisher.history: Microsoft Publisher launched in 1991 as a desktop publishing application for Windows. It was positioned as a more affordable alternative to professional DTP tools. Microsoft announced in 2022 that Publisher would be retired in October 2026, with Microsoft 365 users encouraged to migrate to Word or Designer.
Microsoft Publisher.summary_abuse: Microsoft Publisher is a desktop publishing application being retired in 2026. It has minimal abuse surface as a local desktop application. No notable history of abuse patterns.

Microsoft SharePoint.description: SharePoint is a cloud-based collaborative platform to manage and share content.
Microsoft SharePoint.history: Microsoft SharePoint launched in 2001 as a document management and intranet platform. It evolved through multiple major versions (2003, 2007, 2010, 2013, 2016, 2019) and became available as SharePoint Online within Microsoft 365. It remains the dominant enterprise content management and intranet platform globally.
Microsoft SharePoint.summary_abuse: SharePoint is occasionally abused by attackers to host phishing pages or malware, leveraging the trusted microsoft.com or sharepoint.com domain to bypass email security filters. Because SharePoint allows file sharing with external users, attackers use compromised accounts to distribute malicious links. Microsoft implements file scanning and sharing policy controls to mitigate this.

Microsoft Silverlight.description: Microsoft Silverlight is a cross-browser, cross-platform plug-in designed for delivering advanced .NET-based media experiences.
Microsoft Silverlight.history: Microsoft Silverlight launched in 2007 as a competitor to Adobe Flash, offering rich internet application capabilities. Microsoft officially ended Silverlight support in October 2021. Netflix and other major media platforms dropped Silverlight support earlier, following browser vendors removing the plugin. Sites still running Silverlight are considered legacy.
Microsoft Silverlight.summary_abuse: Silverlight is an end-of-life browser plugin deprecated in 2021. Remaining Silverlight deployments present security risk as no patches are being issued. Attackers have historically used fake Silverlight update prompts to deliver malware. Any site still requiring Silverlight should be considered high-risk from a client security standpoint.

Microsoft Visual Studio.description: Microsoft Visual Studio is an integrated development environment from Microsoft.
Microsoft Visual Studio.history: Microsoft Visual Studio was first released in 1997, consolidating earlier developer tools. It evolved from Visual Basic, Visual C++, and other tools into an integrated IDE. The Community edition became free in 2014. VS Code (a separate product) launched in 2015. Visual Studio remains the leading Windows development environment.
Microsoft Visual Studio.summary_abuse: Visual Studio is a local IDE with no hosting surface. No notable abuse patterns. Occasionally used by malware developers, but this is not a platform characteristic.

Microsoft Word.description: MS Word is a word-processing program used primarily for creating documents.
Microsoft Word.history: Microsoft Word first shipped in 1983 for MS-DOS, then for Macintosh in 1985 and Windows in 1989. It overtook WordPerfect as the dominant word processor in the early 1990s. Word is part of Microsoft Office/Microsoft 365 and remains the world's most widely used word processing software.
Microsoft Word.summary_abuse: Word documents are commonly weaponized to distribute malware via malicious macros (historically .doc/.docm) or exploit vulnerabilities in the document parser. Microsoft blocked auto-execution of macros in internet-sourced files by default in 2022. Word is also used for phishing lures and social engineering documents. These are attacks using the file format, not platform-specific abuse.

Microweber.description: Open Source drag and drop style hosted CMS system.
Microweber.history: Microweber is an open-source drag-and-drop CMS built on Laravel, founded around 2013 by a Bulgarian team. It offers live editing capabilities and is self-hostable. It remains a niche CMS with a small community.
Microweber.summary_abuse: Microweber is a self-hosted CMS with no major documented abuse patterns. As with any CMS platform, installations may be targeted by automated exploitation if not kept updated. Its small market share makes it a less common attack target.

Midtrans.description: Midtrans is a payment gateway system from Indonesia.
Midtrans.history: Midtrans was founded in 2012 as an Indonesian payment gateway. It was acquired by Gojek (now GoTo Group) in 2017. It is one of the leading payment processors in Southeast Asia, supporting Indonesian local payment methods including bank transfers, e-wallets, and convenience store payments.
Midtrans.summary_abuse: Midtrans is a legitimate payment processor operating in Indonesia and Southeast Asia. Like all payment processors, it is a target for fraudulent transactions and card-not-present fraud. PCI DSS certification is maintained. No major public incidents of platform-level abuse documented, though fraud is endemic to the payments industry.

Mieruca.description: Mieruca is a Japanese SEO analysis tool designed to measure and improve website visibility through data-driven insights.
Mieruca.history: Mieruca is a Japanese SEO analytics tool for content optimization and keyword tracking. It serves the Japanese digital marketing market.
Mieruca.summary_abuse: Mieruca is a Japanese SEO tool with no notable abuse surface. No documented abuse patterns.

Miestro.description: Miestro is an all-in-one ecommerce platform wich allow create online course and membership sites.
Miestro.history: Miestro is a membership and online course platform for content creators, founded around 2017. It competes with Teachable and Kajabi in the creator economy space.
Miestro.summary_abuse: Miestro is a membership platform for digital content creators. Like similar platforms, it could be used to sell fraudulent or misleading digital content. No major documented abuse incidents specific to this platform.

Mighty Network.description: Mighty Networks is a platform that facilitates the creation and management of online communities, offering customisable websites, discussion forums, member profiles, events, courses, and multimedia content sharing for individuals and organisations.
Mighty Network.history: Mighty Networks was founded in 2017 by Gina Bianchini and is built around the concept of paid community platforms for creators. It positions itself as a community platform alternative to Facebook Groups, with integrated courses and paid memberships.
Mighty Network.summary_abuse: Mighty Networks hosts community and course platforms for creators. Like all content hosting platforms, it can be used to run misleading or fraudulent paid communities. The platform requires credit card payment for hosting, providing some KYC. No major documented abuse incidents.

Mile.tech.description: Mile.tech is a digital media products company that provides a suite of programmatic monetisation solutions.
Mile.tech.history: Mile.tech was founded in 2013 (originally 2014 in some sources) in New York as an ad network pioneering in-image advertising using computer vision for brand-safe placements. Over the following decade the company evolved into a full-stack programmatic monetisation platform, adding server-side header bidding, dynamic price floors, contextual ads, and video formats, positioning itself as a Google Certified Publishing Partner.
Mile.tech.summary_abuse: Mile.tech is a programmatic advertising and publisher monetisation platform. Ad-tech platforms inherently carry some abuse risk through malvertising - fraudulent ads served through programmatic pipelines that redirect users to phishing or malware landing pages. Publishers using Mile.tech inject third-party ad code onto their sites, which creates an attack surface. Mile is a Google Certified Publishing Partner, suggesting a baseline of quality standards, but like most ad networks it cannot fully prevent all malvertising in real-time bidding environments.

Milestone CMS.description: Milestone CMS is a SEO-first CMS that offers built-in advanced schema markups and Core Web Vitals conformance for improved search performance.
Milestone CMS.history: Milestone CMS is a content management system used mainly in the hospitality and travel industry for managing hotel and destination websites. It has been in operation for multiple decades serving the travel sector.
Milestone CMS.summary_abuse: Milestone CMS is a niche hospitality industry CMS. No notable abuse patterns documented.

Milligram.description: Milligram is a minimalist CSS framework designed to provide a base for web development projects.
Milligram.history: Milligram is a minimal CSS framework released in 2015 by CJ Patoilo. It provides a minimal set of styles for a faster and cleaner development start. The project has limited maintenance activity in recent years.
Milligram.summary_abuse: Milligram is a minimal open-source CSS framework with no hosting surface and no documented abuse patterns.

Milonic.description: Milonic is a platform for developing Dynamic Hypertext Markup Language (DHTML) JavaScript and building dynamic, DHTML-based websites.
Milonic.history: Milonic is a JavaScript DHTML menu system created around 2001 by Andy Woolley. It was widely used in the early 2000s for navigation menus before CSS became more capable. The project is now largely abandoned.
Milonic.summary_abuse: Milonic is an obsolete JavaScript menu library. No abuse surface. Sites still using it have outdated JavaScript that may have unpatched vulnerabilities.

MiloTree.description: MiloTree is a pop-up tool that helps websites grow by converting visitors into social media followers and customers.
MiloTree.history: MiloTree is a pop-up tool for bloggers and content creators to grow social media followers, founded around 2016 by Jen and Corey Snyder. It embeds a popup widget on websites to prompt visitors to follow social profiles.
MiloTree.summary_abuse: MiloTree is a social follow popup widget. It displays pop-ups on websites which can contribute to obtrusive UX. The popup format is sometimes misused to display misleading offers. No significant documented abuse.

Milvus.description: Milvus is an intelligent system designed to manage IT operations and provide help desk support.
Milvus.history: Milvus is an open-source vector database created by Zilliz, first released in 2019. It became the leading vector database for AI/ML applications and similarity search. It was accepted into the LF AI & Data Foundation in 2021. The cloud-managed version is Zilliz Cloud.
Milvus.summary_abuse: Milvus is a vector database used for AI similarity search. It is a backend infrastructure component with no public-facing consumer surface. No documented abuse patterns.

Mimiran.description: Mimiran is a CRM system designed for managing customer relationships.
Mimiran.history: Mimiran was founded by Reuben Swartz, a software engineer and consultant based in Austin, Texas, with a copyright dating back to 2001. Swartz built it after observing that moving the proposal process online would allow tracking when prospects read proposals, evolving it into a CRM specifically designed for solo consultants, coaches, and fractional executives who want relationship-driven selling rather than traditional pipeline management.
Mimiran.summary_abuse: Mimiran is a small-footprint CRM for independent consultants with no notable abuse signal. It is a subscription B2B tool with no anonymous sign-up or user-generated content features that would attract phishing or spam operations.

MindBody.description: Mindbody is a (SaaS) company that provides cloud-based online scheduling and other business management software for the wellness services industry.
MindBody.history: Mindbody (MINDBODY) was founded in 2001 by Rick Stollmeyer and Blake Beltram in San Luis Obispo, California as business management software for health, wellness, and fitness studios. It went public on NASDAQ in 2015 and was taken private by Vista Equity Partners in 2019 for approximately $1.9 billion.
MindBody.summary_abuse: Mindbody is a wellness business management platform. It processes payments and stores customer health and financial data. No major platform-level abuse documented. The platform implements PCI DSS controls for payment data. Privacy concerns relate to the breadth of health and behavioral data collected from fitness studio clients.

Mindbox.description: Mindbox is a marketing automation platform.
Mindbox.history: Mindbox is a Russian customer data platform (CDP) and marketing automation tool founded in 2006. It operates primarily in the Russian market with some CIS country presence. The company has faced challenges with Western market access following geopolitical events in 2022.
Mindbox.summary_abuse: Mindbox is a marketing automation platform based in Russia, primarily serving Russian and CIS markets. The platform handles customer PII and behavioral data at scale. Russian-origin software has increased scrutiny in Western markets post-2022 sanctions. No specific abuse incidents documented, but the geopolitical risk profile warrants consideration in enterprise deployments.

MindStudio.description: MindStudio is a platform for creating AI solutions that are simple and hassle-free.
MindStudio.history: MindStudio is an AI application builder platform launched around 2023, allowing users to build AI-powered applications without coding. It is part of the post-ChatGPT wave of no-code AI platforms.
MindStudio.summary_abuse: MindStudio is a no-code AI app builder. As with similar platforms, AI apps built on it could be used for automated spam or social engineering. No major documented abuse incidents specific to the platform.

MineLabz donations.description: Minelabz is a donations platform built on WordPress and WooCommerce, enabling cracked Minecraft servers to accept donations through their stores.
MineLabz donations.history: MineLabz donations appears to be a Minecraft-related donation or in-game purchases platform for server operators.
MineLabz donations.summary_abuse: Donation platforms for gaming communities like Minecraft servers can be vectors for fraudulent chargebacks and scam storefronts. The gaming donation space has documented patterns of minors using parents' payment details without authorization. Platform-level controls on this type of service are typically minimal.

Minero.cc.description: Minero.cc is a bot that helps run crypto mining application.
Minero.cc.history: Minero.cc is a browser-based cryptocurrency mining service (cryptojacking-as-a-service), launched during the 2017-2018 browser mining boom following Coinhive. These services embed JavaScript mining code in websites to use visitor CPU resources for mining Monero.
Minero.cc.summary_abuse: Minero.cc provides browser-based cryptocurrency mining scripts similar to Coinhive. Browser mining scripts are inherently controversial and considered a form of resource abuse when deployed without explicit visitor consent. Many security tools classify these as potentially unwanted programs (PUPs) or malicious. Ad blockers and security products routinely block Minero.cc scripts. The business model depends on website operators embedding mining scripts, which regulators in several jurisdictions have deemed unlawful without consent.

Minerstat.description: Minerstat is a software provider offering management solutions for crypto mining professionals and mining farms.
Minerstat.history: Minerstat is a cryptocurrency mining management and monitoring platform founded around 2017. It provides tools for managing GPU and ASIC mining rigs, monitoring hashrates, and optimizing mining profitability.
Minerstat.summary_abuse: Minerstat is a legitimate cryptocurrency mining management platform. The crypto mining industry has documented associations with gray-area activity. The platform itself is used by professional miners; no specific platform-level abuse documented beyond the inherent association with the cryptocurrency mining sector.

mini_httpd.history: mini_httpd is a small, minimal HTTP server created by Jef Poskanzer of ACME Labs, designed for embedded and low-resource environments. It has been available since the late 1990s and is still occasionally used in embedded systems.
mini_httpd.summary_abuse: mini_httpd has no notable abuse signal; it is a minimal web server used in embedded systems with no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudsters.

MiniBC.description: MiniBC is a BigCommerce payment app builder that enables the creation and management of custom payment solutions within the BigCommerce platform.
MiniBC.history: MiniBC is a small e-commerce solution or hosting provider. Limited public information is available about this platform.
MiniBC.summary_abuse: MiniBC is a small e-commerce or hosting platform. No notable documented abuse patterns.

miniCal.description: miniCal is an open-source property management system.
miniCal.history: miniCal is an open-source property management system available at minical.io. It is a niche self-hosted tool for hospitality businesses. No notable ownership changes documented.
miniCal.summary_abuse: miniCal has no notable abuse signal. It is a self-hosted open-source property management system with no consumer-facing surface that would attract fraudsters.

Miniflat.description: Miniflat is an Iranian and Persian content management system developed with flat file technology.
Miniflat.history: Miniflat is an Iranian flat-file CMS designed to build Persian and Iranian content websites without a database backend. It is a niche open-source project primarily serving the Farsi-language web community. Very limited public information is available about the founding date or maintainer identity.
Miniflat.summary_abuse: Miniflat is a self-hosted flat-file CMS for Persian-language websites with no consumer-facing hosted service and no documented abuse signal.

MiniOrange Login.description: Social Login with Discord, Facebook, Google, Twitter, LinkedIn and other apps.
MiniOrange Login.history: miniOrange was founded in 2012 in Pune, India. It provides identity and access management solutions including SSO, MFA, and social login plugins for WordPress and other platforms. It has grown to serve thousands of organizations globally.
MiniOrange Login.summary_abuse: miniOrange Login is an authentication and SSO plugin. As an identity provider, it processes user credentials and authentication tokens. No major documented security incidents. Authentication plugins require careful vetting as they handle sensitive login flows; the vendor maintains responsible disclosure policies.

MiniServ.history: MiniServ is the web server component of Webmin, the Unix/Linux system administration panel created by Jamie Cameron in 1997. Webmin/MiniServ is widely used for managing Linux servers through a web interface. In 2019, Webmin suffered a supply-chain compromise where a backdoor was injected into official downloads for over a year.
MiniServ.summary_abuse: MiniServ/Webmin had a critical supply-chain security incident in 2019 where a backdoor was covertly inserted into official Webmin packages, enabling remote code execution. The attack targeted internet-exposed Webmin installations. This represents a significant historical trust event. Exposed MiniServ instances are routinely targeted by automated scanners due to the privileged system access they provide.

MinMaxify.description: MinMaxify is an app that allows Shopify store owners to set minimum and maximum values for customer orders built by Intillium.
MinMaxify.history: MinMaxify is an e-commerce optimization tool offering features like quantity pickers and product customization widgets for online stores.
MinMaxify.summary_abuse: MinMaxify is a small e-commerce widget tool. No notable documented abuse patterns.

Mint.description: Mint is an extensible, self-hosted web site analytics program.
Mint.history: Mint.com was founded in 2006 by Aaron Patzer as a personal finance and budgeting tool. Intuit acquired Mint in 2009 for $170 million. It grew to millions of users for expense tracking and budgeting. Intuit announced the shutdown of Mint in January 2024, directing users to Credit Karma.
Mint.summary_abuse: Mint aggregated banking credentials and account data for millions of users, creating significant phishing and credential theft risk. The service shut down in early 2024. Phishing sites impersonating Mint were documented during its operation. Intuit maintained security practices and the shutdown was handled with user data migration paths to Credit Karma.

Minted.description: Minted is a marketplace that offers premium design goods created by independent artists and designers.
Minted.history: Minted was founded in 2007 by Mariam Naficy as an online marketplace for independent artists and designers selling stationery, art prints, and holiday cards. It raised over $200 million in VC funding. Experienced a significant data breach in 2020 where 4.9 million customer records were compromised and sold on the dark web.
Minted.summary_abuse: Minted suffered a major data breach in May 2020 when attackers compromised 4.9 million customer records including names, email addresses, and hashed passwords. The stolen data was offered for sale on the dark web. Minted disclosed the breach and prompted password resets. No subsequent major incidents documented.

Mintlify.description: Mintlify is a platform that enables developers to create and maintain documentation for their projects using Markdown format and automatically generate and deploy static websites via a continuous integration and deployment system.
Mintlify.history: Mintlify was founded in 2021 by Han Wang and Hahnbee Lee. It offers a documentation platform for software teams with modern design and AI-powered search. The company raised $18.5 million in Series A funding in 2023 and has become popular among developer-tool startups for their documentation.
Mintlify.summary_abuse: Mintlify is a documentation hosting platform. In March 2024, Mintlify disclosed a security incident where unauthorized access to internal systems led to the exposure of customer GitHub tokens. The company revoked affected tokens and notified customers. Beyond this incident, documentation hosting has a low abuse surface area.

Mintox.description: Mintox is a business software designed to streamline operations and enhance efficiency.
Mintox.history: Mintox was built by founder and director Michael Craig starting in 1999, and the company is headquartered in Perth, Western Australia, with offices in South East Asia. It has evolved from an early e-business software system into a Work OS platform that consolidates data and communications for SMB and midmarket businesses.
Mintox.summary_abuse: Mintox is a small Australian business Work OS platform with no documented abuse signal. It serves SMB clients as a business management tool and has no consumer-facing hosted content features that would attract phishing operators.

Mintpay.description: Mintpay is a system that allows purchases to be made immediately and paid for later in installments.
Mintpay.history: MintPay is a Sri Lankan buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) payment solution founded around 2018. It provides installment payment options for Sri Lankan online and retail merchants.
Mintpay.summary_abuse: MintPay is a BNPL payment platform operating in Sri Lanka. Like all BNPL services, it carries risk of fraud through fraudulent applications. No major documented platform-level abuse incidents.

Mioot.description: Mioot is a live chat software that facilitates real-time communication between service agents and customers on digital platforms.
Mioot.history: Mioot was founded in 2003 by Kalai Mani in Chennai, India, as a live chat solution enabling real-time customer support on websites. It is one of the earlier live chat platforms in the Indian market and has operated continuously serving SMB customers.
Mioot.summary_abuse: Mioot is a live chat platform used by small businesses for customer support. Like any embedded chat widget, it could theoretically be abused to conduct social engineering against website visitors, but there are no documented incidents of Mioot-specific abuse. The platform requires account registration and targets legitimate businesses.

Mirador.description: Mirador is a platform designed to enhance customer acquisition, streamline digital loan applications, and enable intelligent product routing and digitization.
Mirador.history: Mirador is an open-source IIIF (International Image Interoperability Framework) viewer for displaying digitized images and manuscripts. It was initially developed by a consortium of universities and cultural heritage institutions including Stanford, Harvard, and Yale around 2012.
Mirador.summary_abuse: Mirador is an open-source image viewer used by libraries and museums. No documented abuse patterns due to its specialized cultural heritage use case.

Mirai.description: Mirai is a hotel management system.
Mirai.history: Mirai (the static analysis tool) is an abstract interpreter for Rust code developed by Facebook Research. Note: this should not be confused with the Mirai botnet malware. The analysis tool was first published around 2018.
Mirai.summary_abuse: Mirai (as a Rust static analysis tool) has no abuse surface. The name Mirai is most notoriously associated with the Mirai botnet malware from 2016 which exploited IoT devices, but that is separate software and not what this entry represents.

Mirakl.description: Mirakl is a marketplace SaaS platform designed to support both B2B and B2C commerce operations.
Mirakl.history: Mirakl was founded in 2012 in Paris by Adrien Nussenbaum and Philippe Corrot. It provides a marketplace platform-as-a-service enabling retailers to build third-party seller marketplaces. Valued at $3.5 billion after a $555 million funding round in 2021.
Mirakl.summary_abuse: Mirakl powers third-party seller marketplaces for major retailers. Third-party marketplace sellers are a well-documented vector for counterfeit goods and fraud. Mirakl provides the platform infrastructure but responsibility for seller vetting lies with the operator. No Mirakl-specific security incidents documented.

mirrAR.description: mirrAR is a real-time augmented reality platform for retail brands that enables consumers to virtually try on products and experience how it feels to own them before the actual purchase, both in-store and online.
mirrAR.history: mirrAR was founded in 2015 as an augmented reality virtual try-on platform for retail brands, initially focusing on jewelry and expanding to beauty, eyewear, and accessories. The company has raised $3.45M in funding from multiple investors.
mirrAR.summary_abuse: mirrAR is an AR virtual try-on platform for retail with no notable abuse signal. It is used by legitimate retail brands and has no features that would attract fraudsters.

Mirus.description: Mirus is a platform that provides scalable solutions capable of adjusting capacity to meet demand while maintaining resilience against unexpected challenges.
Mirus.history: Mirus is a Canadian digital marketing agency and web technology provider for the restaurant industry. Limited public information available.
Mirus.summary_abuse: Mirus is a niche restaurant industry digital marketing tool. No documented abuse patterns.

Mirvac.description: Mirvac is an Australian property management platform focused on real estate development.
Mirvac.history: Mirvac is an Australian publicly listed property development and investment group. Founded in 1972 and listed on the ASX, it operates the Mirvac digital platform for property listings and residential community management.
Mirvac.summary_abuse: Mirvac is an Australian property company website and community platform. No notable abuse patterns documented.

Miso.description: Miso is a real-time personalisation APIs for search, recommendation, and marketing.
Miso.history: Miso was founded in 2017 in San Francisco, California, building on NSF and MacArthur Foundation-funded research on privacy-centric personalization conducted by co-founders with Professor Deborah Estrin at Cornell Tech. The company raised $6.1M in seed funding from investors including O'Reilly AlphaTech Ventures, 500 Global, Susa Ventures, and v1.vc, and has evolved its platform into a real-time personalization API stack covering semantic search, product recommendations, and marketing for ecommerce and content publishers.
Miso.summary_abuse: Miso.ai is a B2B personalization API platform used by ecommerce sites and publishers. It processes user behaviour data to power recommendations, giving it a moderate privacy surface. There is no documented abuse or fraud history. The API model requires developer integration and enterprise agreements, preventing anonymous or low-friction misuse.

MissionSuite.description: Mission Suite is an all-in-one marketing software solution which combines CRM, email marketing, marketing automation, and inbound marketing tools.
MissionSuite.history: MissionSuite was founded in 2011 in Denver, Colorado, as an all-in-one marketing automation and CRM platform targeting small and midsize businesses. It combines email marketing, inbound marketing, and CRM capabilities in a single hosted platform.
MissionSuite.summary_abuse: MissionSuite is a marketing automation and CRM tool for SMBs, providing email marketing capabilities. Email marketing platforms can be used for spam, but MissionSuite is a niche B2B product with account-gated access and no documented abuse history.

Misskey.description: Misskey is a distributed microblogging platform.
Misskey.history: Misskey is a Japanese open-source decentralized social network developed by syuilo, first released in 2014. It is part of the Fediverse via ActivityPub. Interest grew significantly following Twitter/X ownership changes in 2022-2023. Multiple forks exist including Calckey and Firefish.
Misskey.summary_abuse: As a decentralized social platform, Misskey instances vary widely in moderation quality. Some self-hosted instances have been used to distribute CSAM, harassment campaigns, or spam. Federation with poorly-moderated instances is a known challenge. AGPL license means the software itself is auditable, but instance operators are responsible for moderation.

Mithril.description: A Javascript Framework for building applications.
Mithril.history: Mithril.js is a lightweight JavaScript framework for building single-page applications, created by Leo Horie in 2014. It is known for its small size (~10KB gzipped) and performance. It uses a virtual DOM similar to React but is not affiliated with any major company.
Mithril.summary_abuse: Mithril is an open-source JavaScript framework. No documented abuse patterns.

Mithril.js.description: Mithril.js is a lightweight JavaScript framework for building efficient and declarative single-page applications (SPAs).
Mithril.js.history: Mithril.js is a client-side JavaScript framework created by Leo Horie in 2014. It is known for its small footprint and virtual DOM implementation. This entry and Mithril refer to the same library.
Mithril.js.summary_abuse: Mithril.js is an open-source JavaScript framework with no hosting surface and no documented abuse patterns.

Mittwald.description: Mittwald is a web hosting agency, established in 2003 in Espelkamp, Germany
Mittwald.history: Mittwald is a German web hosting company founded in 1995, headquartered in Espelkamp, Germany. It is known as a GDPR-friendly German hosting provider popular with agencies and developers in the DACH region.
Mittwald.summary_abuse: Mittwald is a German hosting provider. Like all hosting providers, some hosted sites may engage in abuse. German legal requirements and GDPR enforcement provide stronger accountability compared to many international hosts. No major platform-level abuse incidents documented.

Miva.description: Miva is a privately owned ecommerce shopping cart software and hosting company with headquarters in San Diego.
Miva.history: Miva (originally Miva Merchant) was founded in 1997 as one of the early e-commerce platforms. It has served mid-market and enterprise merchants for over 25 years and continues as a managed e-commerce platform focusing on high-volume merchants.
Miva.summary_abuse: Miva is a legitimate e-commerce platform serving mid-market merchants. Counterfeit product sales and fraudulent merchant accounts are endemic to the e-commerce sector but no specific major incidents documented for Miva.

Mixin.description: Mixin is a highly-available ecommerce cloud.
Mixin.history: Mixin Network is a cryptocurrency transaction network and messenger app founded around 2017. It operates as a layer-2 network over multiple blockchains. In September 2023 the Mixin Network suffered a $200 million hack when its cloud service provider database was compromised.
Mixin.summary_abuse: Mixin Network suffered a major $200 million hack in September 2023 attributed to a cloud database breach. Cryptocurrency platforms are frequently targeted by hackers. The platform handles significant financial assets and has been used for cross-chain transfers. The 2023 incident raised concerns about its security architecture.

Mixitup.description: A CSS3 and jQuery Filter & Sort Plugin.
Mixitup.history: MixItUp is a JavaScript library for animated content filtering and sorting, created by Patrick Kunka around 2014. It provides filter/sort animations for grid layouts. The project has both a free MIT-licensed version and a commercial license.
Mixitup.summary_abuse: MixItUp is a JavaScript filtering animation library. No abuse surface and no documented abuse patterns.

Mixpanel.description: Mixpanel provides a business analytics service. It tracks user interactions with web and mobile applications and provides tools for targeted communication with them. Its toolset contains in-app A/B tests and user survey forms.
Mixpanel.history: Mixpanel was founded in 2009 by Suhail Doshi and Tim Trefren. It pioneered event-based product analytics, tracking user interactions in mobile and web apps. The company raised $865 million and became a standard analytics tool for product teams. It went through layoffs and management changes in 2022-2023.
Mixpanel.summary_abuse: Mixpanel collects detailed user behavioral data including clicks, page views, and custom events. It processes personal data at scale and is embedded on millions of sites. GDPR compliance requires obtaining consent. The platform is legitimate product analytics, not ad-tech, but the data collection depth and cross-site tracking capabilities make it a significant privacy consideration. No platform-level abuse incidents documented.

MIYN Online Appointment.description: MIYN Online Appointment is an advanced cloud-based online appointment scheduling software.
MIYN Online Appointment.history: MIYN is a cloud-based online appointment scheduling, booking, CRM, and landing page platform for small businesses. Headquartered in Phoenix, AZ, USA. Targets small businesses with a freemium model. WordPress plugin available.
MIYN Online Appointment.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Small US appointment scheduling SaaS for small businesses with no notable issues.

MizbanCloud.description: MizbanCloud is a total cloud infrastructure solutions, CDN service provider and Cloud Computing Services, Cloud DNS, Cloud Security, VoD Streaming Service, Live Streaming, Cloud Object Storage.
MizbanCloud.history: MizbanCloud is an Iranian cloud hosting and server infrastructure provider. Founded around 2013, it serves Iranian businesses and websites.
MizbanCloud.summary_abuse: MizbanCloud is an Iranian hosting provider. Iranian hosting infrastructure operates outside Western regulatory oversight and has been associated with hosting content that evades sanctions or facilitates state-sponsored activity. Iranian cloud providers face restricted access to international payment systems and security research communities. Abuse risk is elevated relative to Western hosting providers due to reduced accountability mechanisms.

MkDocs.description: MkDocs is a static site generator, used for building project documentation.
MkDocs.history: MkDocs is an open-source static site generator for project documentation, written in Python. It was first released in 2014 by Tom Christie. It uses Markdown source files and YAML configuration. MkDocs and its popular theme Material for MkDocs are widely used for technical documentation.
MkDocs.summary_abuse: MkDocs is a static documentation site generator. It generates static HTML files with no backend or hosting surface. No documented abuse patterns.

MNTN.description: MNTN (formerly SteelHouse) is a marketing technology company that provides a platform for digital advertising, including tools for programmatic advertising, creative management, and campaign optimization.
MNTN.history: MNTN (formerly SteelHouse) was founded in 2009 by Mark Douglas and Brooke Partelow in Austin, TX. Rebranded to MNTN and pivoted to Connected TV (CTV) performance advertising. Ryan Reynolds' creative agency Maximum Effort became part of MNTN. Raised $119M in 2022 and went public in 2026.
MNTN.summary_abuse: MNTN is a CTV ad platform used by brands for TV advertising. Collects viewer data for ad targeting. No documented malvertising incidents, but inherent privacy concerns from CTV tracking.

Moast.description: Moast is a Shopify app that enables brands to leverage user-generated content for marketing and engagement purposes.
Moast.history: Moast is a customer review and social proof widget tool for e-commerce stores, focused on video testimonials and social proof display. Founded around 2021.
Moast.summary_abuse: Moast is a social proof widget tool for e-commerce. No notable documented abuse patterns.

mobicred.description: Mobicred is a credit facility that allows you to safely shop online with our participating retailers.
mobicred.history: Mobicred was founded in 2013 in South Africa as a buy-now-pay-later credit facility for online shopping. It was acquired by RCS Group, the consumer finance arm of BNP Paribas, which provides financial stability and regulatory oversight.
mobicred.summary_abuse: Mobicred is a South African BNPL payment service regulated under BNP Paribas's RCS Group. Like other credit facilities it is subject to standard identity verification for account creation. No notable cases of widespread fraud abuse have been documented, though payment platforms generally require ongoing fraud monitoring.

Mobify.description: Mobify is a web storefront platform for headless commerce.
Mobify.history: Mobify was founded in 2007 in Vancouver, Canada as a mobile web acceleration and PWA platform. Salesforce acquired Mobify in October 2020 and integrated its technology into Salesforce Commerce Cloud. The standalone Mobify platform is being sunset.
Mobify.summary_abuse: Mobify was a PWA/mobile commerce platform now integrated into Salesforce Commerce Cloud. No notable abuse patterns documented.

mobile-detect.js.description: Mobile-detect.js is a compact JavaScript library designed to detect devices by comparing patterns against a given User-Agent string.
mobile-detect.js.history: mobile-detect.js is a lightweight JavaScript library for detecting mobile devices by parsing the user-agent string. No notable ownership changes.
mobile-detect.js.summary_abuse: mobile-detect.js has no notable abuse signal; it is a client-side device detection library with no server-side component.

Mobirise.description: Mobirise is a free offline website builder for Windows and macOS that enables non-coders to create small and medium-sized websites locally using drag-and-drop pre-made blocks and themes.
Mobirise.history: Mobirise is an offline website builder developed by a Moldovan company, first released around 2015. It produces mobile-friendly HTML/CSS websites. It has been criticized for dark patterns and aggressive upselling.
html: <!-- Site made with Mobirise Website Builder v([\d.]+)\;version:\1
Mobirise.summary_abuse: Mobirise is a website builder used to create static websites. Sites built with Mobirise are occasionally used for phishing or fake product pages due to its ease-of-use and low cost. The free tier produces sites with Mobirise branding. User reviews report aggressive upselling and dark patterns in license management. Some security researchers have flagged Mobirise-generated sites in phishing reports.

MobX.history: MobX is a reactive state management library for JavaScript applications created by Michel Weststrate and first released in 2015. It is commonly used with React applications as an alternative to Redux. It uses transparent functional reactive programming (TFRP) patterns.
MobX.summary_abuse: MobX is an open-source state management library with no hosting or user-facing surface. No documented abuse patterns.

MochiKit.history: MochiKit is a JavaScript library released in 2005 by Bob Ippolito, inspired by Python programming conventions. It was popular in the mid-2000s before jQuery became dominant. The project is effectively abandoned and no longer maintained.
MochiKit.summary_abuse: MochiKit is an abandoned JavaScript library from 2005. Sites still using it have outdated, unpatched JavaScript. No documented abuse patterns.

MochiWeb.history: MochiWeb is an Erlang library for building lightweight HTTP servers, created by Bob Ippolito around 2007. It was notably used as the HTTP layer for Apache CouchDB and other Erlang web applications.
MochiWeb.summary_abuse: MochiWeb is an Erlang HTTP server library used primarily for backend services. No user-facing surface and no documented abuse patterns.

mod_auth_pam.description: Mod_auth_pam is used to configure ways for authenticating users.
mod_auth_pam.history: Apache module for PAM (Pluggable Authentication Modules) integration in Apache web servers. It is part of the Apache project ecosystem.
mod_auth_pam.summary_abuse: mod_auth_pam has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

mod_dav.description: Mod_dav is an Apache module to provide WebDAV capabilities for your Apache web server. It is an open-source module, provided under an Apache-style license.
mod_dav.history: mod_dav is an Apache module that provides WebDAV (Web-based Distributed Authoring and Versioning) capabilities, allowing collaborative editing of files over HTTP. It has been part of Apache httpd since version 2.0.
mod_dav.summary_abuse: mod_dav has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

mod_fastcgi.description: Mod_fcgid is a high performance alternative to mod_cgi or mod_cgid, which starts a sufficient number instances of the CGI program to handle concurrent requests, and these programs remain running to handle further incoming requests.
mod_fastcgi.history: mod_fastcgi is an Apache module that enables FastCGI protocol support for running dynamic web applications. mod_fcgid is a compatible alternative that is now more commonly used.
mod_fastcgi.summary_abuse: mod_fastcgi has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

mod_jk.description: Mod_jk is an Apache module used to connect the Tomcat servlet container with web servers such as Apache, iPlanet, Sun ONE (formerly Netscape) and even IIS using the Apache JServ Protocol. A web server waits for client HTTP requests.
mod_jk.history: mod_jk is an Apache module that connects Apache httpd to Tomcat servlet containers via the AJP protocol, enabling Apache to front Java web applications.
mod_jk.summary_abuse: mod_jk has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

mod_perl.description: Mod_perl is an optional module for the Apache HTTP server. It embeds a Perl interpreter into the Apache server. In addition to allowing Apache modules to be written in the Perl programming language, it allows the Apache web server to be dynamically configured by Perl programs.
mod_perl.history: Mod_perl embeds a Perl interpreter into the Apache web server for high-performance Perl web applications. It has been part of the Apache ecosystem since the late 1990s.
mod_perl.summary_abuse: mod_perl has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

mod_python.description: Mod_python is an Apache HTTP Server module that integrates the Python programming language with the server. It is intended to provide a Python language binding for the Apache HTTP Server.
mod_python.history: Mod_python embeds a Python interpreter into Apache for high-performance Python web applications. It was deprecated in favor of mod_wsgi and is now functionally obsolete.
mod_python.summary_abuse: mod_python has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

mod_rack.description: Mod_rack is a free web server and application server with support for Ruby, Python and Node.js.
mod_rack.history: Mod_rack is the Rack interface module for Phusion Passenger, enabling Ruby applications to run under Apache. No notable ownership changes.
mod_rack.summary_abuse: mod_rack has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

mod_rails.description: Mod_rails is a free web server and application server with support for Ruby, Python and Node.js.
mod_rails.history: Mod_rails is the Rails interface component of Phusion Passenger for Apache, allowing Ruby on Rails applications to be served directly by Apache. No notable ownership changes.
mod_rails.summary_abuse: mod_rails has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

mod_ssl.description: mod_ssl is an optional module for the Apache HTTP Server. It provides strong cryptography for the Apache web server via the Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) and Transport Layer Security (TLS) cryptographic protocols by the help of the open-source SSL/TLS toolkit OpenSSL.
mod_ssl.history: mod_ssl is the standard Apache module providing SSL/TLS support for the Apache web server, enabling HTTPS. It has been bundled with Apache httpd since version 2.0.
mod_ssl.summary_abuse: mod_ssl has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

mod_wsgi.description: mod_wsgi is an Apache HTTP Server module that provides a WSGI compliant interface for hosting Python based web applications under Apache.
mod_wsgi.history: mod_wsgi is an Apache module providing a WSGI-compliant interface for hosting Python web applications. It was created by Graham Dumpleton and is widely used with Django and other Python frameworks.
mod_wsgi.summary_abuse: mod_wsgi has no notable abuse signal; it is a web server extension module with no consumer-facing surface.

Modal Forms.description: Modal Forms is a tool that enhances your website by adding interactive pop-ups.
Modal Forms.history: Modal Forms is a small JavaScript library or SaaS tool for creating modal dialog form overlays on websites. Limited public information available.
Modal Forms.summary_abuse: Modal Forms is a form overlay widget. Modal-based forms can be used for misleading opt-in forms or dark pattern sign-ups, but no specific abuse incidents documented for this product.

Modelo.description: Modelo is a 3D viewer and model platform that allows users to edit, share, and collaborate on digital models.
Modelo.history: Modelo was founded in 2014 in Somerville, Massachusetts by Qi Su and Tian Deng, building a cloud-based 3D viewer and collaboration platform for architecture, engineering, and construction professionals. The company raised a $1M seed round in December 2014 with participation from ZhenFund. In April 2020, Modelo was acquired by Kujiale, a Chinese interior design and 3D visualization platform.
Modelo.summary_abuse: Modelo is a 3D model sharing and collaboration platform for architecture professionals. It has no notable abuse signal; the specialized domain and professional user base make it an unlikely target for phishing or scam campaigns.

Moder.description: Moder is a PMS system for hotels and resorts to manage and grow their tourism businesses.
Moder.history: Moder appears to be a small open-source hosting or CMS project with limited public visibility.
Moder.summary_abuse: Moder is a small open-source project. No documented abuse patterns.

Modern Image Formats.description: Performance plugin from the WordPress Performance Team, which adds support for outputting images in WebP or AVIF format and supports the `picture` element to enable format fallbacks.
Modern Image Formats.history: Modern image formats refers to next-generation web image standards including WebP (released by Google in 2010), AVIF (based on AV1 codec, standardized around 2019), and HEIC. These formats offer significantly better compression than JPEG and PNG. Browser support reached broad adoption by 2021-2023.
Modern Image Formats.summary_abuse: Modern image formats are technical web standards. No abuse surface beyond any image format.

Modernizr.description: Modernizr is a JavaScript library that detects the features available in a user's browser.
Modernizr.history: Modernizr is a JavaScript library for detecting HTML5 and CSS3 features in browsers, created by Faruk Ates and Paul Irish in 2009. It was widely used for progressive enhancement. The project is dormant as most browsers now support modern web standards.
Modernizr.summary_abuse: Modernizr is an open-source browser feature detection library. No documented abuse patterns.

ModiFace.description: ModiFace is a provider of Augmented Reality technology for the beauty industry.
ModiFace.history: ModiFace was founded in 2006 in Toronto, Canada as an augmented reality beauty try-on technology company. L'Oreal acquired ModiFace in 2018 for an undisclosed amount, making it one of the largest acquisitions in beauty tech. ModiFace technology powers virtual try-on for major beauty retailers.
ModiFace.summary_abuse: ModiFace collects facial data for augmented reality beauty try-on experiences. This involves camera access and facial landmark detection in browsers and apps. Facial biometric data collection is regulated under BIPA (Illinois) and GDPR. No major security incidents documented, but the biometric data collection model warrants privacy-conscious scrutiny.

Modified.history: Modified is a German open-source e-commerce platform based on osCommerce, first released around 2009. It is popular in German-speaking markets as a self-hosted online shop solution.
Modified.summary_abuse: Modified eCommerce is a self-hosted shop system. Like all open-source e-commerce platforms, outdated installations can be exploited for phishing or malicious checkout pages. The platform has no hosted cloud component.

Modified eCommerce.description: Modified eCommerce is a PHP-based open-source ecommerce platform designed for creating and managing online stores with customizable features and functionalities.
Modified eCommerce.history: Modified eCommerce is a German open-source e-commerce platform forked from osCommerce around 2009. Also known simply as Modified Shop, it is used primarily by German-speaking online merchants as a self-hosted alternative to commercial platforms.
Modified eCommerce.summary_abuse: Modified eCommerce is a self-hosted German shop system. Outdated installations can be exploited to host phishing checkout pages. Active maintenance and updates from operators are required for security.

Modio.description: Modio is an ecommerce tracking system designed to monitor, analyze, and report online store performance and customer activity.
Modio.history: mod.io was founded in 2018 in Perth, Australia as a cross-platform user-generated content (UGC) and modding platform for games. It provides APIs for game studios to integrate mod support. The company has partnered with major game publishers.
Modio.summary_abuse: mod.io hosts user-generated game modifications. User-created content platforms can host malicious files disguised as game mods. mod.io implements content moderation and has policies against malicious uploads, but user-generated executable content carries inherent risk. No major documented incidents of malware distribution through the platform.

Modula.description: Modula is a customizable gallery plugin for WordPress.
Modula.history: Modula is a WordPress gallery plugin developed by WP Chill (formerly associated with Macho Themes), with a free version on WordPress.org and premium add-ons sold through wp-modula.com. The plugin targets WordPress site owners who need customizable photo grid and video galleries. The free plugin has accumulated significant installs in the WordPress ecosystem.
Modula.summary_abuse: Modula is a WordPress gallery plugin with no documented abuse signal. It is a client-side WordPress plugin that does not host content itself, so it has no direct abuse surface beyond what the underlying WordPress installation provides.

Module Federation.description: Module Federation is a webpack technology for dynamically loading parts of other independently deployed builds.
Module Federation.history: Module Federation is a Webpack 5 feature introduced in 2020 by Zack Jackson (ScriptedAlchemy). It enables micro-frontend architecture by allowing JavaScript applications to dynamically load code from other applications at runtime. It has become a key enabler for micro-frontend patterns.
scripts: Container initialization failed as it has already been initialized with a different share scope
Module Federation.summary_abuse: Module Federation is a build-time/runtime JavaScript module sharing mechanism. No hosted surface and no documented abuse patterns.

Modulify.description: Modulify is a platform that enables the creation and deployment of scalable websites.
Modulify.history: Modulify launched around 2023 as an AI-powered website builder specifically for Webflow users, enabling rapid creation of structured sitemaps, wireframes, and modular page components. It is a Webflow-ecosystem tool that exports designs directly to Webflow and Figma, targeting freelancers and agencies.
Modulify.summary_abuse: Modulify is a Webflow-integrated AI website builder with no documented abuse signal. The free tier is limited to a single project and requires account creation, making it an unlikely vector for bulk phishing or spam content generation.

MODX.description: MODX (originally MODx) is an open source content management system and web application framework for publishing content on the World Wide Web and intranets.
MODX.history: MODX is an open-source PHP CMS started in 2004 by Raymond Irving and Ryan Thrash as a mashup of Etomite. MODX LLC was formally incorporated in 2010 in Dallas, TX. Won multiple CMS awards including Packt's Most Promising OSS CMS in 2007. Known for flexibility and security.
MODX.summary_abuse: MODX is a reputable open-source CMS with a long history. No notable platform-level abuse. Like all self-hosted CMSes, security depends on user configuration and updates.

MoEngage.description: MoEngage is an intelligent customer engagement platform for the customer-obsessed marketer.
MoEngage.history: MoEngage was founded in 2014 in Bengaluru, India by Raviteja Dodda and Yashwanth Kumar. It is a customer engagement and marketing automation platform serving consumer brands. It raised $77 million in Series E funding in 2022 and serves customers in Asia, Europe, and the Americas.
MoEngage.summary_abuse: MoEngage is a marketing automation platform that embeds tracking scripts in mobile apps and websites to collect user behavioral data for push notifications, email, and in-app messaging campaigns. It processes significant volumes of user behavioral and personal data. GDPR compliance and consent management are customer responsibilities. No major platform-level security incidents documented.

Moguta.CMS.description: Moguta is a Russian-based ecommerce platform that provides tools and solutions for creating and managing online stores.
Moguta.CMS.history: Moguta.CMS is a Russian open-source e-commerce CMS developed from around 2012, targeted at small online shops in Russia and CIS countries. It is a PHP-based platform.
Moguta.CMS.summary_abuse: Moguta.CMS is a Russian open-source e-commerce platform. Russian-origin software faces heightened scrutiny. Outdated installations may be exploited for phishing storefronts. No specific major documented incidents.

MoinAI.description: MoinAI is an AI platform for digital customer communication, enabling businesses to automate interactions.
MoinAI.history: moinAI is a German AI chatbot platform for customer service, founded around 2018 in Hamburg. It provides GDPR-compliant conversational AI for B2C companies in German-speaking markets.
MoinAI.summary_abuse: moinAI is a German chatbot platform with strong GDPR focus. No documented abuse patterns.

MoinMoin.description: MoinMoin is a wiki engine implemented in Python.
MoinMoin.history: MoinMoin is a Python-based wiki engine created by Juergen Hermann in 2000. It was one of the most popular open-source wiki systems in the 2000s and 2010s. The Python Software Foundation and many other projects used MoinMoin for their wikis. Activity has slowed with competition from Confluence and other modern wikis.
MoinMoin.summary_abuse: MoinMoin is an open-source wiki engine. Self-hosted wikis can be used to publish spam or misinformation if open editing is enabled. No platform-level abuse patterns specific to MoinMoin documented.

Mojo.description: Mojo is a platform for direct-to-consumer ecommerce websites, designed for the next generation of online shop owners to boost sales through their social content.
Mojo.history: Mojo is a programming language developed by Modular Inc, designed as a superset of Python for AI/ML workloads with systems programming capabilities. It was publicly announced in May 2023. Modular was founded by Chris Lattner, the creator of LLVM and Swift.
Mojo.summary_abuse: Mojo is a new programming language for AI systems programming. No documented abuse patterns.

Mojo Helpdesk.description: Mojo Helpdesk is a ticketing system used to centralize requests, assign tasks, automate workflows, and track progress for improved organization.
Mojo Helpdesk.history: Mojo Helpdesk was developed by Metadot Corporation, based in Austin, Texas, after Metadot built the help desk tool internally to manage its own customer support. It was commercialised as a standalone SaaS product around 2010 under the leadership of founder Daniel Guermeur. The platform has grown to serve over 2.5 million users and added a European team to support GDPR-region customers.
Mojo Helpdesk.summary_abuse: Mojo Helpdesk is a customer support ticketing system with no documented abuse history. It is a B2B SaaS product accessed by businesses for internal and customer service workflows, with no anonymous content hosting features that would attract fraudsters.

Mojolicious.description: Mojolicious is a Perl-based web framework designed for building web applications and APIs.
Mojolicious.history: Mojolicious is a Perl web framework created by Sebastian Riedel, first released around 2010. It is a modern Perl MVC framework with real-time web capabilities. It is the successor to the Catalyst framework development efforts.
Mojolicious.summary_abuse: Mojolicious is an open-source Perl web framework. No documented abuse patterns.

Moka HR.description: Moka HR is an AI-driven human resources management system offering full-module support for streamlined employee administration.
Moka HR.history: Moka (Moka HR) is a Chinese SaaS HR and recruiting platform founded in 2015. It has raised over $200 million and serves thousands of Chinese companies. It is one of the leading HR-tech platforms in China.
Moka HR.summary_abuse: Moka HR is a Chinese HR platform processing employee and candidate personal data. Chinese data localization laws and PIPL (Personal Information Protection Law) apply. Data sovereignty considerations are relevant for non-Chinese organizations using the platform. No specific documented abuse incidents.

Mokini.description: Mokini is a marketing automation platform designed for ecommerce businesses with customizable templates, user segmentation, and scheduling.
Mokini.history: Mokini AB was founded in 2016 in Malmö, Sweden as an ecommerce marketing automation platform offering customizable templates, user segmentation, and multi-channel marketing scheduling for online stores.
Mokini.summary_abuse: Mokini is a Swedish ecommerce marketing automation platform. Like all email marketing tools it carries residual spam risk, but Mokini is a niche B2B subscription service with no documented abuse history or notable incidents.

Mokka.description: Mokka is a Buy Now Pay Later solution that connects target customer acquisition and settlement costs through marketing and promotion.
Mokka.history: Mokka is a Russian buy-now-pay-later (BNPL) payment service founded around 2013. It provides installment payment solutions for Russian e-commerce merchants.
Mokka.summary_abuse: Mokka is a Russian BNPL payment service. Russian payment services face limited Western regulatory oversight post-2022. Like all BNPL services, carries fraud risk. No specific major incidents documented.

Molin AI.description: Molin AI is a multilingual tool that reduces support volume by 70%, recommends relevant products to shoppers, and uses up-to-date information from client websites to enhance store revenue.
Molin AI.history: Molin AI was founded in 2018 by Alexandru Rosianu, based in the United Kingdom, and has raised $351K in funding from STRT Holding. The platform offers a multilingual AI chatbot for ecommerce stores that handles customer queries, product recommendations, and order tracking using store data combined with large language models.
Molin AI.summary_abuse: Molin AI is an ecommerce AI chatbot service that handles customer support and product recommendations. The platform operates on behalf of merchant websites using their product data; there is no documented abuse history. The API-based nature and account requirements make low-friction misuse unlikely.

Mollie.description: Mollie is a payment provider for Belgium and the Netherlands, offering payment methods such as credit card, iDEAL, Bancontact/Mister cash, PayPal, SCT, SDD, and others.
Mollie.history: Mollie was founded in 2004 in Amsterdam by Adriaan Mol. It grew to become one of Europe's largest payment service providers with over 250,000 business customers. Valued at $6.5 billion in 2021 after a $800 million Series C. Regulated by De Nederlandsche Bank (DNB).
Mollie.summary_abuse: Mollie is a major European payment processor regulated in the Netherlands. Like all payment processors, it faces payment fraud and account takeover attacks. Mollie maintains PCI DSS Level 1 compliance, implements KYC for merchants, and has an abuse team. No major platform-level incidents documented. The transparent per-transaction pricing and no monthly fees made it popular with small e-commerce merchants.

Mollom.history: Mollom was a spam protection and content moderation service founded in 2008 by Dries Buytaert (creator of Drupal) and Ben Vinegar. It was commonly used as an anti-spam solution for Drupal sites. Mollom was acquired by Acquia in 2012 and the service was shut down in April 2018.
Mollom.summary_abuse: Mollom was an anti-spam service, the opposite of an abuse platform. It was shut down in 2018. No ongoing service or abuse surface.

Momence.description: Momence is an all-in-one toolbox for gyms and studios, enabling scheduling of online and in-person classes, events, appointments, and experiences.
Momence.history: Momence was founded in 2020 as a booking and scheduling platform for fitness studios and wellness businesses. It competes with Mindbody and offers event management, live streaming, and payment processing features.
Momence.summary_abuse: Momence is a booking platform for wellness businesses. No notable documented abuse patterns.

Moment Timezone.description: Moment Timezone is a library built upon Moment.js, extending its capabilities to handle time zones in JavaScript. It allows developers to parse, manipulate, and display dates and times in various time zones, providing comprehensive support for time zone conversions and adjustments in web applications.
Moment Timezone.history: Moment Timezone is an extension of Moment.js providing timezone support, released around 2013. Like Moment.js, it is in maintenance mode and the team recommends newer alternatives for new projects.
Moment Timezone.summary_abuse: Moment Timezone is an open-source timezone handling library. No documented abuse patterns.

Moment.js.description: Moment.js is a free and open-source JavaScript library that removes the need to use the native JavaScript Date object directly.
Moment.js.history: Moment.js was created by Tim Wood in 2011 and became one of the most downloaded JavaScript libraries for date/time parsing, manipulation, and formatting. The library entered maintenance-only mode in 2020, with the team recommending modern alternatives such as Luxon, Day.js, and date-fns for new projects.
Moment.js.summary_abuse: Moment.js is one of the most widely used JavaScript date/time libraries. No documented abuse patterns. The project is in maintenance mode.

Momice.description: Momice is an event management platform that offers tools for organising and managing events.
Momice.history: Momice is a Dutch event management platform founded around 2012, specializing in professional event websites and registration. It targets the corporate events and conferences market in the Netherlands and Belgium.
Momice.summary_abuse: Momice is a Dutch event management platform. No notable documented abuse patterns.

Monaco Editor.description: Monaco Editor is the code editor that powers VS Code. Monaco Editor is a tool in the text editor category of a tech stack.
Monaco Editor.history: Monaco Editor is the code editor that powers VS Code, developed by Microsoft and first released as an open-source standalone library in 2016. It provides syntax highlighting, IntelliSense, code completion, and diff editing for the web. Widely used in online IDEs (CodeSandbox, GitHub Codespaces, StackBlitz, etc.).
Monaco Editor.summary_abuse: Monaco Editor is an open-source browser-based code editor maintained by Microsoft. No documented abuse patterns.

Mondial Relay.description: Mondial Relay is a parcel shipping and delivery service in Europe.
Mondial Relay.history: Mondial Relay was founded in 1997 in France as a parcel delivery and relay point network. It operates a network of over 25,000 pickup points across Europe. The company was acquired by InPost (the Polish parcel locker operator) in 2021 for 513 million euros.
Mondial Relay.summary_abuse: Mondial Relay is a parcel delivery and pickup point network. Parcel delivery services are sometimes used as cover for phishing (fake delivery notification scams). Mondial Relay is occasionally impersonated in phishing campaigns instructing recipients to confirm delivery details. The platform itself is legitimate infrastructure.

Mondo Media.description: Mondo Media is a German digital agency that specializes in web design, development, digital marketing, and ecommerce solutions tailored to businesses and organizations.
Mondo Media.history: Mondo Media appears to be a digital media company or web services provider. As a web technology fingerprint, it may refer to their digital ad serving or content delivery systems.
Mondo Media.summary_abuse: Mondo Media is a digital media provider. Limited information available about abuse patterns.

Moneris.description: Moneris (formerly Moneris Solutions) is Canada's largest financial technology company that specialises in payment processing.
Moneris.history: Moneris was founded in 2000 as a joint venture between Royal Bank of Canada and Bank of Montreal, making it one of Canada's largest payment processors. It processes billions in transactions annually across over 350,000 Canadian merchant locations.
Moneris.summary_abuse: Moneris is one of Canada's largest payment processors jointly owned by major Canadian banks. Subject to robust Canadian financial regulations and PCI DSS compliance. Like all payment processors, fraud is an industry challenge but Moneris maintains strong anti-fraud controls and KYC for merchants.

Moneris Payment Gateway.description: Moneris is Canada’s leading processor of Debit and credit card payments. This WooCommerce extension automatically adds moneris payment gateway to your woocommerce website and allows you to keep the customer on your site for the checkout process.
Moneris Payment Gateway.history: Founded in December 2000 as a joint venture between Royal Bank of Canada and Bank of Montreal. Became Canada's first payment processor to offer both Visa and Mastercard in one account. First Canadian processor to handle Interac Online (2005) and EMV chip transactions (2005). Processes over 3 billion transactions annually for 350,000+ merchants.
Moneris Payment Gateway.parent_company: Royal Bank of Canada / Bank of Montreal
Moneris Payment Gateway.summary_abuse: Moneris is a legitimate payment processor jointly owned by Canada's two largest banks; it is not a platform for abuse. As with any payment gateway, stolen card numbers may be tested against merchants using the gateway, but Moneris has standard fraud detection and PCI DSS compliance. No notable pattern of the platform itself being abused.

Monetate.description: Monetate is a company that specializes in providing personalisation and customer experience optimisation solutions to businesses.
Monetate.history: Founded 2008 by David Brussin and David Bookspan in Conshohocken, PA. Acquired by Kibo Commerce (Vista Equity Partners) in 2019; the Monetate brand was discontinued by Kibo in 2021 but spun back out in October 2022 and sold to Centre Lane Partners. In June 2025 Monetate was acquired by SiteSpect.
