Monetate.summary_abuse: Monetate is a B2B personalization and A/B testing platform targeting enterprise retailers. It injects JavaScript into client pages for tracking and personalization, which raises privacy considerations, but it is not a spam or phishing enabler. No known pattern of the platform being abused for malicious purposes.

Monetize Pro.description: Monetize Pro is a platform that helps users earn money through various monetization strategies for their online content.
Monetize Pro.history: Monetize Pro is a content monetization platform that launched around 2022. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Monetize Pro.summary_abuse: Monetize Pro is a content monetization SaaS. Platforms in this category can be used to implement aggressive ad monetization or paywalls with misleading UX, and are sometimes used by low-quality or deceptive content sites. No specific incidents are documented.

MongoDB.description: MongoDB is a document-oriented NoSQL database used for high volume data storage.
MongoDB.history: Founded in 2007 as 10gen by Dwight Merriman, Kevin Ryan, and Eliot Horowitz; rebranded to MongoDB Inc. in 2013. Released MongoDB as open source in 2009; launched Atlas cloud DBaaS in 2016 which grew to 70% of revenue by 2024. Acquired WiredTiger (2014), MLab (2018), Realm (2019), and Voyage AI (2025). IPO on NASDAQ in October 2017.
MongoDB.summary_abuse: MongoDB has minimal abuse signal as a backend database engine. It is not consumer-facing and not a preferred tool for fraudsters. Early versions had insecure default configurations leading to mass data exposure incidents around 2017, but current versions require authentication by default. No notable pattern of platform abuse.

Mongrel.history: Created by Zed Shaw as a fast HTTP 1.1 Ruby web server using a Ragel-based parser. Was the dominant Ruby deployment option circa 2006-2008. Development stopped after Shaw's departure from the Ruby community in 2008; incompatibility with Ruby 1.9+ made it unusable by 2010. Puma carried its architectural legacy.
Mongrel.summary_abuse: Mongrel has no meaningful abuse signal. It is an abandoned Ruby web server library no longer in active use. No known pattern of abuse.

Monitus.description: Monitus is a provider of web analytics tools for online retailers, specialising in developing solutions for the Yahoo Stores platform.
Monitus.history: Founded by Michael Whitaker in 2000 to build analytics and cart recovery tools specifically for Yahoo! Store merchants. A very small, specialized vendor serving a legacy platform (Yahoo Stores). Limited public information available.
Monitus.summary_abuse: Monitus is a niche web analytics and cart recovery tool for Yahoo Store merchants. Very low profile; no known abuse signal.

Monkcommerce.description: Monk Commerce is a software-as-a-service that aims to increase income for small e-commerce businesses by deploying AOV boosting techniques like cart and post-purchase offers
Monkcommerce.history: Founded 2021 in Bengaluru, India by Rahul Ranjan, Murtuza Merchant, and Ravish Mehta. Bootstrapped with ~16 employees as of 2025. Operates on the Shopify App Store providing upsell, cross-sell, and AOV-boosting tools for Shopify merchants.
Monkcommerce.summary_abuse: Monk Commerce is a Shopify app providing upsell and discount tools. It is not a platform for abuse, though aggressive discount or upsell popups could be considered dark patterns if misconfigured. No known pattern of abuse or malicious use.

Monkey HTTP Server.history: Monkey HTTP Server was started in 2001 as an open-source lightweight HTTP server for Linux, designed for high performance on embedded and resource-constrained systems. It is licensed under Apache 2.0 and available on GitHub, though activity appears to have slowed significantly in recent years.
Monkey HTTP Server.summary_abuse: Monkey HTTP Server is an open-source web server with no notable abuse signal. As a self-hosted web server library, it has no consumer-facing surface that scammers would exploit.

Mono.description: Mono is an open-source platform that enables developers to create and run .NET applications across different operating systems, facilitating cross-platform compatibility.
Mono.history: Started by Miguel de Icaza of Ximian on July 19, 2001 to implement .NET on Linux. Ximian was acquired by Novell, then by Xamarin, then by Microsoft. Microsoft largely superseded Mono with its own cross-platform .NET Core (now .NET). Final patch release was February 2024; Microsoft transferred Mono project to WineHQ in August 2024.
Mono.summary_abuse: Mono has no meaningful abuse signal. It is a runtime environment for .NET code on non-Windows platforms. No known pattern of abuse.

Mono.net.description: Mono.net is a website builder platform designed for businesses to create and manage their own websites with hosting and online presence tools.
Mono.net.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Mono.net.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

MonoBill.description: MonoBill is an ecommerce platform that offers built-in billing, subscription management, and payment processing features for small businesses.
MonoBill.history: MonoBill is a freemium ecommerce platform with built-in billing and subscription management. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
MonoBill.summary_abuse: MonoBill is an ecommerce/billing SaaS with no documented abuse signal.

Monocle.description: Monocle is an AI-powered platform that optimizes incentives to boost profits while maintaining brand equity and margins.
Monocle.history: Founded 2023 in New York City by Noam Szpiro and Mark Lotman, who previously built AI systems at Lyft, Instacart, McKinsey, and Stripe. Raised $7.5M seed in May 2024. Provides AI agents for CRM, lifecycle marketing, and customer retention.
Monocle.summary_abuse: Monocle is a very early-stage B2B AI platform for CRM and customer retention. No consumer-facing surface and no known abuse signal.

Monsido.description: Monsido provides a website management platform that automates processes, ensures regulatory compliance, improves collaboration in web and marketing teams, and streamlines reporting.
Monsido.history: Founded in Denmark around 2013 as a web governance SaaS scanning websites for accessibility, SEO, and content quality. Acquired by CivicPlus for government technology use, then acquired by Acquia in November 2023 and rebranded as Acquia Web Governance.
Monsido.summary_abuse: Monsido is a B2B web governance scanning tool. It has no consumer-facing surface and is not a vector for spam or phishing. No known abuse signal.

MonsterInsights.description: MonsterInsights is the most popular Google Analytics plugin for WordPress.
MonsterInsights.history: Originally Google Analytics for WordPress by Yoast, acquired in 2016 by Syed Balkhi who rebranded it MonsterInsights under Awesome Motive. Grew to 3+ million active WordPress installations. Awesome Motive also owns WPForms and OptinMonster.
MonsterInsights.summary_abuse: MonsterInsights is a Google Analytics integration plugin for WordPress. It collects analytics data via Google Analytics and routes it to Google's infrastructure; the plugin itself does not directly track for advertising. No known abuse pattern, though it increases Google's data collection surface on sites where it is installed.

Monto.description: Monto is a platform for ecommerce apps in Webflow, designed to enhance business sales and optimise Webflow ecommerce sites.
Monto.history: Israeli B2B payments startup that exited stealth in July 2024 with $9 million in seed funding led by Scale Venture Partners. Connects supplier ERPs with 300+ buyer AP portals to automate B2B invoicing. Serves enterprise clients including Shutterstock and Miro. Has helped process nearly $1 billion in supplier payments.
Monto.summary_abuse: Monto is a B2B payment infrastructure platform connecting ERP systems with AP portals. It operates in regulated financial services and serves enterprise customers. No known abuse signal.

Moodle.description: Moodle is a free and open-source Learning Management System (LMS) written in PHP and distributed under the GNU General Public License.
Moodle.history: Developed by Martin Dougiamas and first released on 20 August 2002 as a free, open-source LMS written in PHP. Led by Moodle HQ, an Australian company supported by a global network of Moodle Partners. Grew to become the world's most widely deployed open-source learning platform, used by universities, corporates, and governments worldwide.
Moodle.summary_abuse: Moodle has low abuse signal. It is a self-hosted LMS and not a typical target for fraudsters. Occasional security vulnerabilities in older versions have been exploited by attackers, but the software is actively patched. No known pattern of Moodle being used as a platform to conduct scams or phishing operations.

Moofx.description: Moofx is a CSS3-enabled JavaScript animation library for creating and managing lightweight, hardware-accelerated animations.
Moofx.history: Moofx is a CSS3-enabled JavaScript animation library created by Valerio Proietti (kamicane), the same developer behind MooTools. Released around 2012 as a lightweight, hardware-accelerated animation library. The GitHub repository has been archived and the project has received no updates in many years, effectively abandoned.
Moofx.summary_abuse: No abuse signal. Moofx is a small, archived JavaScript animation library with no consumer-facing surface or hosting capabilities.

Moon.history: Moon is a minimal JavaScript UI framework created by Kabir Shah, first released around 2017. It was designed as a lightweight alternative to Vue.js. Development has been largely inactive in recent years.
Moon.summary_abuse: Moon is a small JavaScript framework with no consumer-facing surface and no documented abuse signal.

Moon Organizer.description: Moon Organizer is a platform that tracks lunar cycles, phases, and moon-related events to support planning and provide timely updates.
Moon Organizer.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Moon Organizer.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Moori.description: Moori is a developer of Shopware 6 plugins, creating extensions that enhance functionality and customization within the ecommerce platform.
Moori.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Moori.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Moosend.description: Moosend is a platform that enables management, creation, and distribution of email marketing campaigns.
Moosend.history: Founded 2011 in Athens, Greece by Yannis Psarras and Panos Melissaropoulos. Acquired by Sitecore in May 2021 and rebranded as Sitecore Send. Constant Contact completed acquisition from Sitecore in June 2025, restoring the Moosend brand. Serves 100,000+ businesses.
Moosend.summary_abuse: Email marketing platforms including Moosend can be misused for spam campaigns. Moosend offers a free tier that lowers the barrier to sending bulk email, which attracts some spammers. The platform has anti-spam policies but free-tier mass email capabilities are a known abuse vector for unsolicited commercial email.

Moostik.description: Moostik is a French analytics counter designed to track website traffic and visitor behavior.
Moostik.history: Moostik is a French website traffic analytics counter operating since 2001. It is donation-funded, places no advertising on client sites, and offers subscription plans starting at €5/month. A very small, long-running independent analytics service.
Moostik.summary_abuse: Moostik is a niche web analytics counter that collects visitor traffic statistics. As an analytics tool it collects visitor data including IP-based geolocation, but the platform itself has no meaningful abuse vector and displays no ads. No known abuse signal.

MooTools.description: MooTools is a JavaScript framework that enhances web application development with utilities for DOM manipulation, animations, and AJAX interactions.
MooTools.history: Created by Valerio Proietti in September 2006, inspired by Prototype.js. Was a popular object-oriented JavaScript framework during the pre-ES6 era. Development slowed significantly after 2010 as contributors left; last major release (1.6.0) was in January 2016. Now effectively abandoned as ES6+ and modern bundlers rendered it obsolete.
MooTools.summary_abuse: MooTools has no meaningful abuse signal. It is an abandoned client-side JavaScript library with no backend or hosted infrastructure. Used only in legacy websites; no known pattern of abuse.

Moove GDPR Consent.description: Moove GDPR Consent is a GDPR Cookie Compliance plugin for Wordpress.
Moove GDPR Consent.history: GDPR Cookie Compliance plugin by Moove Agency, a London-based WordPress VIP Agency. The plugin stores all consent data locally on the host website and does not collect user data on Moove's servers. Installed on over 300,000 WordPress websites. Freemium model with premium add-ons.
Moove GDPR Consent.summary_abuse: Moove GDPR Consent is a privacy-oriented WordPress plugin that helps websites comply with GDPR cookie consent requirements. It explicitly stores no user data on the vendor's servers. No abuse signal; the plugin is designed to enhance user privacy.

Moovin.history: Founded 2006 in Porto Alegre, Brazil. One of Brazil's established e-commerce platform providers supporting B2C and B2B omnichannel commerce with integrations to Amazon, Shopee, Mercado Livre, and other Brazilian marketplaces. About 200 stores use the SaaS solution.
Moovin.summary_abuse: Moovin is a Brazilian e-commerce SaaS platform. It is primarily used by legitimate SMB and mid-market retailers. No known pattern of abuse or fraudulent storefronts associated with the platform.

Mopinion.description: Mopinion is an all-in-one user feedback platform that helps digital enterprises listen, understand, and act across all digital touchpoints.
Mopinion.history: Founded 2013 in Rotterdam, Netherlands. Served enterprise clients including KLM, Vodafone, and Allianz. Acquired by Stockholm-based Netigate in September 2025 to create a combined AI-driven experience management platform.
Mopinion.summary_abuse: Mopinion is a B2B digital feedback and voice of customer platform. It collects user feedback on client websites via embedded surveys, with no meaningful abuse vector. No known pattern of abuse.

Mopro.description: Mopro is a growth platform designed to help small businesses improve their online presence and streamline digital operations.
Mopro.history: Founded 2013 in Orange County, California. Positions itself as a Do-It-For-Me (DIFM) website builder using AI to produce mobile-first business websites for SMBs in under 24 hours. Grown to 240+ employees and 5,500+ business clients. Also serves enterprise clients including PepsiCo.
Mopro.summary_abuse: Mopro is a DIFM website builder for small businesses. AI-generated website services can produce low-quality or misleading sites, though no documented pattern of abuse is associated with Mopro specifically. The platform targets legitimate SMBs.

MoreVago.description: MoreVago is a platform that displays customer testimonials and reviews to help businesses increase conversions and sales.
MoreVago.history: Morevago is a small Ohio-based company offering a 'Sticky Reviews' social proof widget that displays customer testimonials on websites. It integrates with WordPress, ClickFunnels, and similar platforms. Very limited public company information available.
MoreVago.summary_abuse: Morevago provides social proof widgets used to display testimonials. Social proof tools can be misused to display fake reviews to manipulate consumer behavior. No specific documented incidents of Morevago being misused, but the tool category has elevated dark pattern potential.

Morningtrain.description: Morningtrain is a pop-up window tool designed in Denmark for generating and capturing leads through on-site user engagement.
Morningtrain.history: Morningtrain (MorningCatch) was a Danish popup and lead-capture widget tool. The website now shows a closure notice (vi-lukker-morningcatch), indicating the service has been shut down.
Morningtrain.summary_abuse: Morningtrain was a Danish lead capture popup tool that has since been shut down. No notable abuse signal was documented during its operation.

Morphext.description: Morphext is a lightweight jQuery plugin that rotates text using Animate.css transitions for smooth, dynamic visual effects.
Morphext.history: Morphext is a small jQuery text rotation plugin powered by Animate.css, created by Ian Lai. Designed to cycle through text phrases with CSS animations on landing pages. Fell into disuse as modern landing page builders absorbed this functionality. No known maintenance activity in recent years.
Morphext.summary_abuse: Morphext has no meaningful abuse signal. It is a tiny abandoned jQuery text animation plugin with no hosted infrastructure or data collection. No known pattern of abuse.

Morris.js.description: A JavaScript library that simplifies the process of creating interactive and responsive data visualizations on the web.
Morris.js.history: Morris.js was a JavaScript charting library built on jQuery and Raphael.js for time-series and bar charts. The original author acknowledged being unable to actively maintain it, with the last significant update many years prior. Still found in legacy dashboards and admin templates but no longer actively developed.
Morris.js.summary_abuse: Morris.js has no meaningful abuse signal. It is an abandoned client-side chart rendering library with no hosted infrastructure. No known pattern of abuse.

Moshimo.description: Moshimo is a free affiliate service for individuals.
Moshimo.history: Moshimo is a Japanese affiliate marketing platform and one of the leading affiliate networks in Japan with approximately 57% market share among Japanese affiliates. It is a subsidiary of a Tokyo Prime Market-listed company. Offers a 12% bonus reward to affiliates on top of advertiser commissions.
Moshimo.summary_abuse: Moshimo is an affiliate marketing network primarily serving Japanese content creators. Affiliate networks can be used to promote spam or misleading offers; Moshimo primarily operates in the Japanese market under stricter consumer protection norms. No specific documented incidents of major abuse.

Mosio.description: Mosio is a text messaging software solution for research that supports participant communication and data collection.
Mosio.history: Mosio is a US-based text messaging and mobile communication platform focused on healthcare providers and clinical research. Offers HIPAA-compliant two-way SMS for appointment reminders, patient surveys, and discharge follow-up. Hosted on HIPAA-compliant servers with SSL encryption.
Mosio.summary_abuse: Mosio is a HIPAA-compliant healthcare messaging SaaS serving medical providers and clinical researchers. It operates in a regulated sector and handles protected health information. No known pattern of abuse; the platform is not consumer-facing and is not a typical vector for spam or phishing.

Motherhost.description: Motherhost is a web hosting company based in India that offers a range of hosting services, including shared hosting, VPS hosting, dedicated servers, and cloud hosting.
Motherhost.history: Motherhost is an Indian web hosting company offering shared, VPS, dedicated, cloud, and WordPress hosting since 2009. Claims 99.99% uptime and uses NGINX Plus with all-SSD storage. Primarily serves Indian and South Asian SMBs.
Motherhost.summary_abuse: Motherhost is an Indian shared web hosting provider. Like other low-cost shared hosting providers, some of its IP space may be used for hosting phishing pages or spam-linked sites by malicious tenants. No specific documented incidents attributable to Motherhost itself.

Motion.description: Motion is an AI-powered tool that helps users manage their calendars, meetings, and projects.
Motion.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Motion.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Motion.page.description: Motion.page is a WordPress animation plugin utilizing GSAP and ScrollTrigger.
Motion.page.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Motion.page.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Motive.description: Motive is an all-in-one website platform designed to create and manage online presence for the automotive industry.
Motive.history: Motive (motivehq.com) is an Oklahoma City-based SaaS platform for automotive dealerships, founded in 2020. Formerly known as Wackk, it rebranded to Motive and raised $6.38M from investors including Armory Square Ventures and Cortado Ventures. The platform provides automotive dealers with websites, AI chat, digital retailing tools, and trade-in appraisals.
Motive.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal. Motive is a niche B2B SaaS serving automotive dealerships with no documented abuse patterns.

MotoCMS.history: Launched in 2008 by Demetrio Fortman initially as FlashMoto, a Flash-based CMS. As Flash declined, it was rebuilt as MotoCMS using HTML5. Offers a template-based drag-and-drop website builder with 14-day free trial. Primarily targets small business owners.
MotoCMS.summary_abuse: MotoCMS is a niche website builder primarily for SMBs. Like other site builders it could host low-quality or misleading content, but the vendor enforces terms of service. No documented pattern of widespread abuse or phishing campaigns hosted on the platform.

Mottle.description: Mottle is a custom chatbot creation tool that allows users to build expressive ChatGPT-like chatbots using their own data.
Mottle.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Mottle.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Mottor.description: Mottor is a no-code tool for creating websites, online stores, landing pages, and more.
Mottor.history: Mottor (formerly LPmotor) is a Russian no-code website and landing page builder with chatbot features. A small team of approximately 8 employees. Primarily serves the Russian-speaking market for small businesses and marketers building landing pages and funnels.
Mottor.summary_abuse: Mottor is a Russian landing page and website builder. Russian-hosted website builders have elevated risk of hosting phishing pages, fake storefronts, and spam landing pages, particularly given limited regulatory enforcement. Platform itself is not documented as a purposeful enabler, but the Russian hosting context raises the general abuse risk.

Mountain.description: MNTN Performance TV is a CTV advertising platform with targeting, measurement, and optimization for amplifying marketers' performance goals.
Mountain.history: MNTN (Mountain) is a Connected TV advertising platform for performance marketers. Provides targeting, measurement, and optimization for CTV/OTT ad campaigns. Based in Los Angeles, CA. Has grown as streaming TV advertising has expanded.
Mountain.summary_abuse: MNTN is a CTV/OTT advertising platform. As an ad-tech company it processes viewer behavioral data for targeting purposes. It is not a typical phishing or spam vector, but it participates in the digital advertising ecosystem with its associated privacy tradeoffs. No known specific abuse incidents.

Mouse Flow.history: Founded in Denmark, Mouseflow is a session replay, heatmap, and form analytics platform. Has grown to serve 190,000+ clients worldwide. Built in Europe with privacy compliance focus including GDPR controls.
Mouse Flow.summary_abuse: Mouseflow records user sessions and mouse movements on client websites. As a session recording tool it captures sensitive user activity including form input, which raises privacy concerns if misconfigured. The vendor offers masking controls, but improper deployment can capture PII. No evidence of Mouseflow being used offensively by the vendor itself.

Movable Ink.description: Movable Ink is a technology company that allows marketers to change emails after they have been sent out.
Movable Ink.history: Founded 2010 in New York City by Vivek Sharma and Michael Knott. Pioneered real-time email personalization technology embedding dynamic content in email HTML. Raised over $69M total including a $30M Series C in 2020. Serves 700+ brands including Hilton, Expedia, and eBay. Acquired by private equity firm STG in June 2025.
Movable Ink.summary_abuse: Movable Ink injects JavaScript and dynamically rendered images into email campaigns for personalization. As a marketing platform it processes customer behavior data for targeting. Not a phishing or spam enabler; serves Fortune-500 clients. No known significant abuse incidents.

Movable Type.history: Launched October 2001 by Ben and Mena Trott via Six Apart. Was the dominant blogging platform before WordPress. Six Apart merged with VideoEgg to form SAY Media in 2010; Movable Type was sold to Infocom (Japanese IT company) in 2011. Now primarily maintained for the Japanese market.
Movable Type.summary_abuse: Movable Type is a legacy CMS primarily used in Japan. Older self-hosted installations have been exploited via vulnerabilities. No significant current abuse pattern; the platform is largely declining in Western markets.

Moveo.AI.description: Moveo.AI is a no-code platform that allows businesses to create, manage and deploy AI virtual agents (chatbots).
Moveo.AI.history: Founded in 2020, headquartered in New Milford, NJ. Raised a $2.6M seed round from European and US investors including Eleven Ventures and UniFund. Targets banking, insurance, healthcare, and collections verticals with AI-powered conversational agents.
Moveo.AI.summary_abuse: Moveo.AI is a B2B enterprise conversational AI platform. It is not consumer-facing and operates within regulated industries like banking. No known abuse signal or pattern of misuse.

MoverBase.description: MoverBase is a software solution that includes features designed to support the management of a moving company.
MoverBase.history: Moverbase is a cloud-based CRM and operations management platform built specifically for moving companies. Based in Philadelphia, PA. Developed collaboratively with high-volume New York City movers. Plans start at $19/month. Used by moving companies across the US and internationally.
MoverBase.summary_abuse: Moverbase is a niche vertical SaaS for moving companies. No consumer-facing surface and no meaningful abuse signal.

Movylo.description: Movylo is an AI-driven platform that automates customer engagement across websites and social media to drive sales.
Movylo.history: Movylo is a Boston-based automated marketing and loyalty platform targeting local brick-and-mortar businesses. Provides email/SMS campaigns, AI chatbots, and loyalty programs on autopilot for retail, food, beauty, and local services. Has a WordPress plugin available.
Movylo.summary_abuse: Movylo is a marketing automation platform for local businesses using email and SMS channels. Email/SMS marketing platforms can be misused for spam, but Movylo targets verified local merchants. No documented pattern of abuse.

Moxie.description: Pollyfills for XHR2 and File API
Moxie.history: Moxie (formerly Hectic) is an all-in-one freelancer business management platform providing CRM, proposals, contracts, invoicing, and time tracking. Targets solo freelancers and small agencies. Available on iOS and Android.
Moxie.summary_abuse: Moxie is a freelancer business management SaaS with no meaningful abuse signal. It handles invoicing and client management for individual freelancers. No known pattern of abuse.

mOxie.description: mOxie is a library providing polyfills for XHR2 and File API, ensuring compatibility with older browsers that lack native support.
mOxie.history: mOxie is a runtime abstraction library developed by Moxiecode Systems (Ephox, the makers of Plupload and TinyMCE) to provide polyfills for HTML5 File API and XHR2. It was used internally in Plupload and has been largely superseded by native browser support.
mOxie.summary_abuse: mOxie has no notable abuse signal; it is a client-side file upload polyfill library with no server-side component.

Moyklass.description: Moyklass is a Russian customer relationship management platform designed to help schools manage student data, track performance, and organize administrative tasks.
Moyklass.history: Moyklass (My Class) is a Russian CRM system for automating operations at schools, educational studios, and children's centers. Provides scheduling, attendance, and payment management for educational institutions. Primarily serves the Russian-speaking market.
Moyklass.summary_abuse: Moyklass is a vertical SaaS CRM for Russian educational institutions. No significant consumer-facing surface or known abuse vector. Low abuse signal.

Mozard Suite.history: Mozard is a Dutch government software company providing the Mozard Suite for case management, permits, oversight, and public service delivery for Dutch municipalities and public organizations. The name stands for MidOffice, Zaken, Antwoord, Registraties en Dienstverlening. Integrates with DigiD, eHerkenning, and Dutch government systems.
Mozard Suite.summary_abuse: Mozard Suite is a specialized Dutch municipal government software platform with no public-facing abuse surface. It is used exclusively by Dutch public bodies for administrative case management. No abuse signal.

mParticle.description: mParticle is a mobile-focused event tracking and data ingestion tool.
mParticle.history: mParticle was founded in 2013 by Michael Katz, Andrew Katz, Dave Myers, and Jason Lynn, the team behind interclick (acquired by Yahoo for $270M in 2011). The company raised $272M in total funding through multiple rounds including a $150M Series E in 2021, establishing itself as a leading customer data platform for mobile-first businesses. In 2025 mParticle was acquired by Rokt, a global ecommerce technology company.
mParticle.summary_abuse: mParticle is an enterprise customer data platform used to collect and route user behavioral data across marketing and analytics tools. It is a backend data pipeline with no consumer-facing surface, so it has no notable direct abuse signal. The platform does handle significant volumes of user behavioral data, raising privacy considerations for the sites that deploy it.

MRI Box and Dice.description: MRI Box and Dice is a CRM platform designed for real estate agencies, providing tools for managing client relationships, property listings, and transactions.
MRI Box and Dice.history: Box+Dice is an Australian real estate SaaS CRM for residential sales, launched in 2005, and acquired by MRI Software. Used by 1,100+ agencies and 13,000+ active users, handling ~10% of Australian real estate transactions. Integrates with MRI PropertyTree.
MRI Box and Dice.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Established Australian real estate CRM backed by major proptech company MRI Software.

MRI Eagle.description: MRI Eagle is a technology solution catering to real estate firms, offering efficiency and cost-saving benefits.
MRI Eagle.history: MRI Eagle (formerly Eagle Software) is an Australian property management SaaS for residential agencies. Acquired by MRI Software and integrated into their product suite. Provides lease and contract management with embedded AI for contact insights and workflow automation.
MRI Eagle.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Legitimate Australian property management software backed by MRI Software.

MRW.description: MRW is a Spanish courier company specialised in express national and international shipping services.
MRW.history: MRW is a Spanish courier and logistics company founded in 1977, headquartered in Barcelona. Provides express national and international shipping, e-commerce logistics, and digital logistics solutions. Operates in Spain, Portugal, Andorra, and Gibraltar with 56 platforms, 3,300+ vehicles, and 8 aircraft. Processes ~70 million shipments annually.
MRW.summary_abuse: No abuse concerns. Established Spanish logistics company with decades of operations and standard courier services.

MSAAQ.description: MSAAQ is a platform that allows users to create, sell, and manage their products or services from a single location.
MSAAQ.history: MSAAQ (مساق) is a Saudi-founded LMS platform launched in 2020 from Riyadh. Built by a team of 40+ EdTech experts. Focuses on Arabic-language online training academies and e-learning for organizations. Targets the MENA region market.
MSAAQ.summary_abuse: MSAAQ is a legitimate Arabic LMS platform. No documented abuse incidents. Low risk platform for education and corporate training.

MsCode.pl.description: MsCode.pl is a platform focused on developing innovative solutions in programming, hosting, and marketing.
MsCode.pl.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
MsCode.pl.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

MSHOP.description: MSHOP is an all-in-one ecommerce platform.
MSHOP.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
MSHOP.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

MTCaptcha.description: MTCaptcha is a captcha service that is GDPR and WCAG compliant, providing the confidence of privacy and accessibility.
MTCaptcha.history: MTCaptcha was founded in 2018 in San Francisco by Joe Selvaraj, Huan Chen and others. It is a GDPR/WCAG 2.1-AAA compliant CAPTCHA service using AI-driven adaptive challenges. Differentiates itself by not collecting personally identifiable information unlike Google reCAPTCHA.
MTCaptcha.summary_abuse: MTCaptcha is a privacy-focused CAPTCHA service designed to protect against bots. No documented abuse. Strong privacy stance — no PII collection.

Muchat.description: Muchat is a customer support tool that uses artificial intelligence to deliver personalized conversations by leveraging customer data.
Muchat.history: Muchat is an AI-powered customer support live chat SaaS. No notable ownership or acquisition events are documented.
Muchat.summary_abuse: Muchat is an AI live chat SaaS with no documented abuse signal.

MudBlazor.description: MudBlazor is a component library for Blazor implementing Material Design.
MudBlazor.history: MudBlazor is an open-source Material Design component library for Blazor written entirely in C#. It emerged around 2020 as Blazor adoption grew and became one of the most popular Blazor component libraries. Actively maintained by a community of contributors with a strong GitHub presence.
MudBlazor.summary_abuse: MudBlazor has no meaningful abuse signal. It is a client-side .NET Blazor UI component library with no hosted infrastructure or data collection. No known pattern of abuse.

MUI.description: MUI(formerly Material UI) is a simple and customisable component library to build faster, beautiful, and more accessible React applications.
dom: style[data-meta='MuiPaper'], div.MuiBox-root, div.MuiPaper-root, style[data-meta='MuiButton'], input.MuiInputBase-input, div.MuiContainer-root
MUI.history: MUI (Material UI) is the most popular React UI component library, created in 2014 by Olivier Tassinari and others. Implements Google's Material Design. Has 90,000+ GitHub stars. MUI Core is MIT licensed; MUI X advanced components have commercial licenses.
MUI.summary_abuse: MUI is a foundational React UI library with no abuse risk. One of the most trusted open-source projects in the React ecosystem.

Mul-Pay.description: Mul-Pay is a payment gateway platform from Japan.
Mul-Pay.history: Mul-Pay (PG Multi-Payment Service) is GMO Payment Gateway's comprehensive online payment platform in Japan. GMO Payment Gateway is a publicly listed company on the Tokyo Stock Exchange. The service handles over 22 trillion yen in annual transactions for 150,000+ merchants including NHK and the National Tax Agency.
Mul-Pay.summary_abuse: Mul-Pay is a major Japanese payment gateway operated by a publicly listed company. It handles high-volume legitimate commerce. As with any payment platform, stolen card testing may occasionally occur, but GMO-PG maintains PCI DSS compliance and fraud controls. No significant abuse incidents attributed to the platform itself.

Mulberry.description: Mulberry is a developer of personalised product protection solutions used to help brands unlock additional revenue.
Mulberry.history: Mulberry Technology Inc. was founded in 2018 in New York City. It provides AI-driven extended warranty and product protection solutions for e-commerce merchants via a Shopify app and API. Offers free one-year coverage to customers with optional paid extended protection plans.
Mulberry.summary_abuse: Mulberry is a product protection and extended warranty platform for e-commerce. BBB-registered company. Some customer reviews raise concerns about claims handling, but this represents service quality disputes rather than fraud or platform abuse. No pattern of using the platform for phishing or scams.

Mura CMS.description: Mura CMS is the cloud-based, API driven, content management platform.
Mura CMS.history: Mura CMS is a ColdFusion-based CMS developed by Blue River Interactive Group. Was open source until 2020 when version 10 became closed-source proprietary. Open source support ended in 2024; a community fork called MASA CMS continues as open source. Adobe disclosed an authentication bypass vulnerability in 2023.
Mura CMS.summary_abuse: Mura CMS had a notable authentication bypass vulnerability disclosed in January 2023 (CVE-2023-...) affecting both Mura and its fork MASA CMS. Self-hosted ColdFusion CMS installations can be exploited if not patched. Low current risk as the platform is largely deprecated.

Muscula.description: Muscula is an error logging solution that collects and analyzes errors for websites or applications, providing actionable insights.
Muscula.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
Muscula.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

Musement.description: Musement is a platform that allows users to book tickets for attractions, museums, and activities, providing access to various cultural and entertainment experiences.
Musement.history: Founded 2013 in Milan, Italy by Paolo Giulini, Claudio Bellinzona, Alessandro Petazzi, and Fabio Zecchini. Raised $16.4M over 4 rounds. Acquired Triposo in 2017. Acquired by TUI Group in September 2018 and rebranded as TUI Musement. Has 12M+ users.
Musement.summary_abuse: Musement is a travel activity booking marketplace owned by TUI Group. Fraudulent activity listings or fake booking scams are potential abuse vectors for consumer-facing travel platforms. No specific documented incidents on Musement; TUI's scale implies reasonable trust and abuse monitoring.

Mustache.description: Mustache is a web template system.
Mustache.history: Created by Chris Wanstrath (co-founder of GitHub) in 2009, inspired by ctemplate and et. Mustache pioneered the logic-less template philosophy using double-curly-brace syntax. Spawned implementations in 40+ languages and inspired Handlebars.js. Still actively used in legacy codebases worldwide.
Mustache.summary_abuse: Mustache has no meaningful abuse signal. It is a simple client/server-side templating library with no hosted infrastructure or user-facing surface. No known pattern of abuse.

Mutiny.description: Mutiny is a no-code AI platform designed to aid marketers in converting top-of-funnel demand into revenue without requiring the expertise of engineers.
Mutiny.history: Founded 2018 and backed by Sequoia Capital, Tiger Global, Insight Partners, and Y Combinator (YC S18). Mutiny offers AI-powered B2B website personalization enabling marketers to create account-based experience microsites without code.
Mutiny.summary_abuse: Mutiny is a B2B website personalization platform targeting enterprise marketers. It injects JavaScript to modify website content based on visitor firmographics. Low abuse risk; no consumer-facing surface. No known incidents of abuse.

Muuri.description: Muuri is a JavaScript layout engine that allows you to build all kinds of layouts and make them responsive, sortable, filterable, draggable and/or animated.
Muuri.history: Muuri is a JavaScript grid layout library created by Niklas Rämö at Haltu Oy in Finland. It provides responsive, sortable, filterable, and draggable grid layouts using Web Animations API. The project has framework wrappers for React and Angular contributed by the community.
Muuri.summary_abuse: Muuri has no meaningful abuse signal. It is a client-side JavaScript grid layout library with no hosted infrastructure or data collection. No known pattern of abuse.

Muvi.description: Muvi is a SaaS-based provider of over-the-top (OTT) platform solutions for video streaming, offering end-to-end infrastructure for content delivery and monetization.
Muvi.history: Muvi is an all-in-one OTT and video streaming platform provider with over a decade of operations. Provides white-label streaming solutions for broadcasters, content owners, and enterprises. Clients include Sony and Philips. Listed in Gartner Market Guide for Video Platform Services. Recognized as OTT Platforms Leader since 2023.
Muvi.summary_abuse: Muvi is a white-label OTT streaming platform. White-label platforms can be used to distribute pirated content under a custom brand. Muvi serves legitimate media enterprises but the white-label model means content compliance relies on individual operator policies. No documented large-scale abuse incidents.

Mux.description: Mux is a platform offering integration to monitor video streaming performance using minimal lines of code.
Mux.history: Founded 2015 in San Francisco; backed by Y Combinator (2016). Raised $177M total, reaching unicorn status with a $105M Series D in April 2021 led by Coatue. Mux provides developer-focused video streaming APIs and analytics used by media companies and SaaS platforms.
Mux.summary_abuse: Mux is a developer video infrastructure API platform. It is not a consumer-facing video hosting service with open uploads. Abuse risk is low; primary concern would be a compromised customer account streaming unauthorized content. No known significant abuse incidents.

My Agile Privacy.description: My Agile Privacy is a GDPR solution that ensures website compliance with data protection regulations.
My Agile Privacy.history: My Agile Privacy is an Italian IAB Europe, Microsoft, and Google-certified Consent Management Platform (CMP). Supports GDPR, CCPA, LGPD, and other global privacy frameworks. Available as a WordPress plugin on WordPress.org. Primarily serves the Italian and European market.
My Agile Privacy.summary_abuse: My Agile Privacy is a certified GDPR consent management platform designed to improve user privacy and compliance. It enhances privacy, not harms it. No abuse signal.

My Flying Box.description: My Flying Box is an international parcel shipping company.
My Flying Box.history: My Flying Box was founded in 2011 in Nice, France. Provides a multi-carrier shipping integration platform for e-commerce businesses, connecting with DHL, UPS, FedEx, Colissimo, Mondial Relay, and others. Has Shopify, PrestaShop, Magento, and WooCommerce integrations. Rated 4.8/5 on Google.
My Flying Box.summary_abuse: My Flying Box is a multi-carrier shipping SaaS for e-commerce. Shipping APIs can be misused in drop-shipping fraud schemes, but My Flying Box serves legitimate merchants. No known pattern of abuse.

My Food Link.description: My Food Link provides a fully hosted specialist online supermarket ecommerce platform to supermarkets and grocers.
My Food Link.history: My Food Link is an Australian e-commerce platform providing fully hosted online supermarket solutions for supermarkets and independent grocers. Offers a white-label storefront with grocery catalog management and online ordering capabilities.
My Food Link.summary_abuse: My Food Link is a white-label grocery e-commerce platform for Australian supermarkets. No meaningful abuse signal; primarily used by legitimate grocery retailers.

MyAlice.description: MyAlice is a customer support platform designed for ecommerce brands to manage and streamline customer service operations.
MyAlice.history: Niche commercial SaaS with no notable ownership or deprecation events documented.
MyAlice.summary_abuse: No notable abuse signal documented for this niche commercial SaaS.

MyBB.description: MyBB is a free and open-source forum software written in PHP.
MyBB.history: Founded as DevBB in 2002 by Chris Boulton, forked from XMB. First public release as MyBB in December 2003; stable 1.0 in December 2005. Moved to open source LGPL license in 2009 and to GitHub in 2013. Volunteer-driven project that has maintained a small but consistent community.
MyBB.summary_abuse: MyBB sites can be targets for spam bots and account registration abuse. Forum software platforms are sometimes used to host spam content or phishing links in posts. MyBB has a history of security vulnerabilities in older versions that have been exploited. The software is self-hosted so abuse depends on individual forum operators' practices.

MyBlogLog.history: MyBlogLog was founded in 2005 as a blog community and analytics service. It was acquired by Yahoo in January 2007 for approximately $10 million when the company had just five employees and no revenue. Yahoo integrated the community features into its broader blog and social networking strategy, but terminated the service on May 24, 2011.
MyBlogLog.summary_abuse: MyBlogLog has been shut down since 2011 and no longer operates. The service provided blog community widgets that tracked visitor data, creating a minor third-party tracking footprint during its active years. No significant abuse patterns were documented during its operation.

MyCashFlow.description: MyCashFlow is an ecommerce platform based in Finland that provides tools and services for businesses to create, manage, and optimize online stores.
